41	/-! ### Recipe 1 helpers (iter-184 Lane B, `analogies/gmscaling-projection-idiom.md`):
42	project-side simp helpers for `pullback.map ≫ pullback.fst/snd`.
43	
44	Mathlib's `pullback.lift_fst` / `pullback.lift_snd` are `@[reassoc]` ONLY, not
45	`@[simp]`, so the abbrev `pullback.map _ _ _ _ i₁ i₂ i₃ _ _ ≫ pullback.fst _ _`
46	is not collapsed by `simp` even though the equation holds definitionally. These
47	two local simp lemmas unblock projection-chain collapse for
48	`gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso` whose STEP 4 uses
49	`asIso (pullback.map …)`. Candidate for upstream Mathlib contribution. -/
50	
51	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
52	lemma pullback_map_fst_proj {C : Type*} [Category C] {W X Y Z S T : C}
53	    (f₁ : W ⟶ S) (f₂ : X ⟶ S) [Limits.HasPullback f₁ f₂] (g₁ : Y ⟶ T)
54	    (g₂ : Z ⟶ T) [Limits.HasPullback g₁ g₂] (i₁ : W ⟶ Y) (i₂ : X ⟶ Z) (i₃ : S ⟶ T)
55	    (eq₁ : f₁ ≫ i₃ = i₁ ≫ g₁) (eq₂ : f₂ ≫ i₃ = i₂ ≫ g₂) :
56	    Limits.pullback.map f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ i₁ i₂ i₃ eq₁ eq₂ ≫
57	        Limits.pullback.fst g₁ g₂ =
58	      Limits.pullback.fst f₁ f₂ ≫ i₁ :=
59	  Limits.pullback.lift_fst _ _ _
60	
61	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
62	lemma pullback_map_snd_proj {C : Type*} [Category C] {W X Y Z S T : C}
63	    (f₁ : W ⟶ S) (f₂ : X ⟶ S) [Limits.HasPullback f₁ f₂] (g₁ : Y ⟶ T)
64	    (g₂ : Z ⟶ T) [Limits.HasPullback g₁ g₂] (i₁ : W ⟶ Y) (i₂ : X ⟶ Z) (i₃ : S ⟶ T)
65	    (eq₁ : f₁ ≫ i₃ = i₁ ≫ g₁) (eq₂ : f₂ ≫ i₃ = i₂ ≫ g₂) :
66	    Limits.pullback.map f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ i₁ i₂ i₃ eq₁ eq₂ ≫
67	        Limits.pullback.snd g₁ g₂ =
68	      Limits.pullback.snd f₁ f₂ ≫ i₂ :=
69	  Limits.pullback.lift_snd _ _ _
70	
71	/-! ### Chart-bridge: `Proj.awayι ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away _ _))`
72	
73	The helper below is the iter-173 `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe step (a)
74	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). Used by `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (below the `gmScalingP1_cover`
75	definition). -/
76	
77	/-- **`Proj.awayι 𝒜 f _ _ ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 f))`** for any
78	homogeneous element `f` of positive degree.
79	
80	Generic in the element `f` AND in the degree `m` so we can apply it to either
81	`(![X 0, X 1]) i` (the actual chart input from `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover.f i`,
82	which uses `m = (![1, 1]) i`) or `X i` (which uses `m = 1`). A pure rewrite chasing
83	`awayι_toSpecZero` + `Spec.map_comp` + the `algebraKbarAway` defeq. -/
84	private lemma awayι_comp_PLB_hom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
85	    {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m)
86	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (hf : f ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar m) :
87	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f hf hm ≫
88	      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
89	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar
90	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f))) := by
91	  change Proj.awayι _ _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
92	  rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.awayι_toSpecZero, ← Spec.map_comp,
93	    ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
94	  rfl
95	
96	/-! ### (D) The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`
97	
98	`gmScalingP1` is a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism (the analogist D3 verdict:
99	no `IsAction`/`MulAction`-style typeclass at scheme level — Mathlib has no such precedent;
100	the rigidity consumer needs only the bare morphism + the named fixed-point lemma).
101	
102	Chartwise definition: on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m` (target chart `D₊(X₀)` of `ℙ¹`), the morphism is
103	the polynomial map `(x, λ) ↦ λx`; near `∞` (target chart `D₊(X₁)`, coordinate `u = 1/x`),
104	the target coordinate `1/(λx) = u/λ` is regular because `λ ∈ 𝔾_m` is invertible. The two
105	chart-restrictions agree on `(𝔸¹ ∖ {0}) × 𝔾_m`, so they glue via
106	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`.
107	
108	The companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposes the load-bearing fixed-point
109	property `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` for all `λ ∈ 𝔾_m`, packaged as the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis
110	that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes. -/
111	
112	/-- **Chart-1 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
113	coord `u = X 0 / X 1 ↦ u ⊗ λ`, where `λ = X () ∈ GmRing kbar`. Uses
114	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` with the algebra-map `kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing`
115	(target carrier carries `Algebra kbar` because both factors do). Axiom-clean. -/
116	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
117	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
118	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
119	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
120	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X ())))
121	
122	/-- **Chart-0 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
123	coord `t = X 1 / X 0 ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The `λ⁻¹` is `IsLocalization.Away.invSelf (X ())` in
124	`GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`. Axiom-clean. -/
125	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart0_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
126	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
127	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
128	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
129	      (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
130	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) :
131	        GmRing kbar))
132	
133	/-- **The pullback open cover of `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left`** along `pullback.fst`, indexed by
134	the 2-chart cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` of `ProjectiveLineBar.left`. The `i`-th
135	component is `pullback (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom) (Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X i) …)`. -/
136	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
137	    ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left.OpenCover :=
138	  (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.pullback₁
139	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
140	
141	/-- **The chart-`i` source of `gmScalingP1_cover` is
142	`Spec ((Away 𝒜 ((![X 0, X 1]) i)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)`.**
143	
144	Built by composing `pullbackSymmetry`, `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso`, the
145	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` rewrite via `pullback.congrHom`, and `pullbackSpecIso`. Mirrors the
146	Mathlib precedent `OpenCover.pullbackCoverAffineRefinementObjIso`
147	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open:160-166`). Used by `gmScalingP1_chart`.
148	
149	**Uniform-in-`i` (iter-179 refactor `cover-bridge-uniform-i`):** the target type carries
150	`((![X 0, X 1]) i)` rather than `MvPolynomial.X i`. The hoisted helpers
151	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg`/`_hm` together with the m-generalised
152	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` keep the bridge chain syntactic so `pullbackSpecIso` applies
153	generically — no `match`-on-`i` decoration. -/
154	private noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
155	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ≅
156	      Spec (CommRingCat.of
157	        (TensorProduct kbar
158	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
159	            ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
160	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))
161	          (GmRing kbar))) :=
162	  pullbackSymmetry _ _ ≪≫
163	    pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
164	    pullback.congrHom
165	      (awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm i)
166	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
167	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)
168	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i))
169	      (show (Gm kbar).hom =
170	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) from rfl) ≪≫
171	    pullbackSpecIso kbar _ (GmRing kbar)
172	
173	/-- **The chart-`i` scheme morphism** `(gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme`
174	defining `σ_×` on the `i`-th chart. On chart-1 (target `D₊(X 1)`), the affine coord
175	`u = X 0 / X 1` is sent to `u ⊗ λ`; on chart-0 (target `D₊(X 0)`), `t = X 1 / X 0` is sent
176	to `t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The scheme map is built from `gmScalingP1_chart{0,1}_ringMap` (the chart-side
177	ring maps) via `pullbackSpecIso` + (the chart-ring iso
178	`HomogeneousLocalization.Away ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar`) + `Proj.awayι`.
179	
180	**Status (iter-173):** body landed via the `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe
181	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). The bridge `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (above) identifies the
182	source with `Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X i)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing)`. The chart-ring iso
183	`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso` plus a chart-`i`-specific `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` produces
184	the ring map `Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* Away 𝒜 (X i) ⊗ GmRing`, then `Proj.awayι` lands the
185	result in `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`. -/
186	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
187	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar :=
188	  (gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso kbar i).hom ≫
189	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
190	      ((MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
191	          (fun _ : Unit =>
192	            (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem
193	                (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i)
194	                (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar (otherFin i))) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
195	              (match i with
196	               | ⟨0, _⟩ =>
197	                  (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
198	                    (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) : GmRing kbar)
199	               | ⟨1, _⟩ =>
200	                  algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)
201	                    (MvPolynomial.X ())))).comp
202	        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i).toRingHom)) ≫
203	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
204	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
205	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i) Nat.one_pos
206	
207	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
208	/-- **Shared per-chart helper for `gmScalingP1`** (iter-174 Sub-task A per
209	`analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Decision 3). On the `i`-th chart of the
210	`gmScalingP1_cover`, the composition `gmScalingP1_chart kbar i ≫ PLB.hom` agrees with
211	`(gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom`.
212	
213	This is the per-chart certificate used by `gmScalingP1_over_coherence` (via
214	`Scheme.Cover.hom_ext` + `Scheme.Cover.ι_glueMorphisms_assoc`).
215	
216	**Status (iter-180):** body retired axiom-clean via the empirically-verified
217	`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` recipe of
218	`analogies/pullbackspeciso-bypass.md` (Decision 4). The option collapses the
219	`Algebra.compHom`-driven heartbeat sink on the `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` rewrite
220	that blocked iter-175 through iter-179. -/
221	private lemma gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
222	    gmScalingP1_chart kbar i ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
223	      (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom := by
224	  -- Stage 1 (iter-179): unfold the chart map, apply `awayι_comp_PLB_hom` after a
225	  -- type-realigning `change`, and collapse the right-hand `Spec.map` chain into
226	  -- `Spec.map (algMap kbar (Away_i ⊗ GmRing))` via
227	  -- `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap` + `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_comp_C`.
228	  unfold gmScalingP1_chart
229	  have h := awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (m := 1) Nat.one_pos
230	    (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
231	    (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
232	  change (gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso kbar i).hom ≫ _ ≫
233	      ((Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
234	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
235	          (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i) Nat.one_pos :
236	        Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
237	          (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (MvPolynomial.X i))) ⟶
238	          Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)) ≫
239	        (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom) = _
240	  rw [h, ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
241	    homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq,
242	    MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_comp_C]
243	  -- Stage 2 (iter-180 fix): the `respectTransparency` option lets the
244	  -- `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` simp lemma fire on the buried iso chain;
245	  -- it produces `pullback.fst _ _ ≫ Spec.map (algMap kbar Away_i)` in place
246	  -- of `(pullbackSpecIso).hom ≫ Spec.map (algMap kbar (Away_i ⊗ GmRing))`.
247	  -- The follow-up simps collapse the `pullback.congrHom`-wrapped map into a
248	  -- bare `pullback.fst (cover.f i ≫ PLB.hom) Gm.hom`.
249	  unfold gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso
250	  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, Category.assoc, pullbackSpecIso_hom_base,
251	    pullback.congrHom_hom, pullback.lift_fst_assoc, Category.id_comp]
252	  -- Stage 3: re-align `Proj.awayι (![X 0, X 1] i) ⋯ ⋯` with `(cover.openCover.f i)`
253	  -- so `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_hom_fst_assoc` can match its `f' ≫ f` pattern.
254	  change (pullbackSymmetry (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
255	        ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i)).hom ≫
256	      (pullbackRightPullbackFstIso (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom
257	          ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i)).hom ≫
258	        pullback.fst
259	            ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i ≫
260	              (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom)
261	            (Gm kbar).hom ≫
262	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar
263	            (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
264	              ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
265	                Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)))) =
266	        (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom
267	  simp only [pullbackRightPullbackFstIso_hom_fst_assoc,
268	    pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_fst_assoc]
269	  -- Stage 4: reverse the chart bridge back to `cover.f i ≫ PLB.hom` so the residual
270	  -- matches `(PLB ⊗ Gm).hom`'s definitional form.
271	  rw [← awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm i)
272	    ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
273	      Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)
274	    (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i)]
275	  -- Stage 5: expose `(PLB ⊗ Gm).hom = pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom ≫ PLB.hom`
276	  -- and `(gmScalingP1_cover).f i = pullback.fst (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom)
277	  -- ((cover).openCover.f i)`, then close via `pullback.condition_assoc`.
278	  change pullback.snd (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
279	        ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i) ≫
280	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i ≫
281	        (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
282	    pullback.fst (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
283	        ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i) ≫
284	      pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom ≫
285	        (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom
286	  rw [← pullback.condition_assoc]
287	
288	/-- **Intersection-cover X iso** (iter-182 Lane B helper per
289	`analogies/intersection-ring-cross01.md` Decision 3 Recipe 1).
290	
291	Identifies the pullback over the cross chart `(cover.f 0) ⨯ (cover.f 1)` with
292	`Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X 0 * X 1)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing)`, mirroring `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`
293	with the merged generator `X 0 * X 1` of degree 2.
294	
295	The body is a chain of iso steps:

356	This is the *single* non-trivial ring-level identity for the cocycle:
357	
358	`pullback.fst ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≫ chart 0 =
359	   pullback.snd ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≫ chart 1`.
360	
361	On the basic-open intersection `D₊(X 0 · X 1) ⊆ ProjectiveLineBarScheme` both chart
362	coordinates are units (chart-0 coord `t = X 1 / X 0`, chart-1 coord `u = X 0 / X 1`,
363	with `t · u = 1`). Under `σ_×([X 0 : X 1], λ) = [λ · X 0 : X 1]` the two chart maps
364	satisfy on the intersection:
365	
366	* chart-0: `t ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹` (i.e. `t/λ`)
367	* chart-1: `u ↦ u ⊗ λ` (i.e. `λ · u`)
368	
369	Substituting `u = 1/t` on the chart-1 side gives `λ · u = λ · (1/t) = λ/t`, and on the
370	chart-0 side `t/λ` corresponds to `u' = 1/(t/λ) = λ/t` under the `u ↔ 1/t` change of
371	coords. Hence the ring-level identity is `λ · u = (1/t) · λ` in
372	`Localization.Away t ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar`, which after multiplying both sides by `t`
373	reduces to `λ · u · t = λ`, true because `u · t = 1`.

603	/-- **The load-bearing fixed-point property of `σ_×`:** at the scaling fixed point
604	`0 ∈ ℙ¹`, the morphism `σ_×(0, ·) : 𝔾_m → ℙ¹` is the constant morphism at `0`. That is,
605	the composite `(0 ≫ toUnit) × 𝟙 : 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹` equals `toUnit ≫ 0`.

642	/-! ### (E) Product-stability instances on `ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m`
643	
644	These instances are exported for Lane B's consumer `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux`
645	(in `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`), so its previously local `haveI ... := by sorry`
646	ad-hoc scaffolds collapse to `inferInstance`. Each instance is justified as follows:
647	
648	* `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).hom` is locally of finite type — by composition with `pullback.fst`
649	  (`LocallyOfFiniteType` is `IsStableUnderComposition` and `IsStableUnderBaseChange`,
650	  with both factors LOFT).
651	* `ℙ¹` is reduced — **closed axiom-clean iter-168** via the chart-cover + `val_injective`
652	  bridge (`projectiveLineBar_isReduced`).
653	* `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).hom` is geometrically irreducible — scaffold (Mathlib gap: `GeometricallyIrreducible`
654	  on `Gm.hom` needs the alg-closed-base reduction, currently not bridged).
655	* `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left` is reduced — scaffold (Mathlib gap: `Smooth → GeometricallyReduced`
656	  not shipped at scheme level).
657	* `Gm.hom` is geometrically irreducible — scaffold (Mathlib gap: see above). -/

659	/-- **`(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).hom` is locally of finite type.** Decomposes as
660	`pullback.fst ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom`; `LocallyOfFiniteType` is stable under composition
661	and pullback (Mathlib's `locallyOfFiniteType_comp`,
662	`locallyOfFiniteType_isStableUnderBaseChange`). -/
663	instance projGm_locallyOfFiniteType (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
664	    LocallyOfFiniteType ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom := by
665	  change LocallyOfFiniteType
666	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom)
667	  infer_instance
668	
669	/-- **`ℙ¹` is reduced.** Closed axiom-clean iter-168 via `IsReduced.of_openCover` over
670	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover`; each chart `Spec (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X_i))`
671	is a domain because the canonical `val`-injection into `Localization.Away (X_i)` (a
672	localization of `k̄[X_0, X_1]` at a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain) factors through
673	`Function.Injective.isDomain`. Exported here for Lane B (replaces its inline `haveI hP1red`). -/
674	instance projectiveLineBar_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
675	    IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left := by
676	  change IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar)
677	  -- Strategy: `IsReduced.of_openCover` over `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover`.
678	  -- Each chart is `Spec(.of (Away 𝒜 (X i)))`; `IsReduced (Spec R)` if `R` is reduced.
679	  -- `Away 𝒜 (X i)` is a domain (and hence reduced) because it embeds via `val_injective`
680	  -- into `Localization.Away (X i)`, which is a localization of `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar`
681	  -- (a domain) at a non-zero-divisor — hence a domain.
682	  haveI : ∀ i : Fin 2, IsReduced ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.X i) := by
683	    intro i
684	    -- (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.X i = Spec (.of (Away 𝒜 (X i)))
685	    -- Need IsReduced of that Spec.
686	    change IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
687	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
688	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))))
689	    haveI : IsDomain (Localization.Away ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar),
690	        MvPolynomial.X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)) := by
691	      fin_cases i <;>
692	        exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
693	          (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
694	    haveI : IsDomain (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
695	        ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
696	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)) := by
697	      refine Function.Injective.isDomain
698	        (algebraMap
699	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
700	            ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
701	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))
702	          (Localization.Away
703	            ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
704	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))) ?_
705	      intro x y h
706	      exact HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective _ h
707	    infer_instance
708	  exact IsReduced.of_openCover _ (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover
709	
710	/-- **`𝔾_m` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Scaffold (Mathlib gap: the
711	direct `GeometricallyIrreducible` consequence of `IrreducibleSpace + Spec(domain over alg
712	closed)` is not bridged; the analogist's recipe would require base-change reduction via

716	instance gm_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
717	    GeometricallyIrreducible (Gm kbar).hom := by
718	  sorry
719	
720	/-- **`(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).hom` is geometrically irreducible.** Derives from the individual factors
721	via `GeometricallyIrreducible.comp` (with `UniversallyOpen` discharged for free by smoothness
722	of each factor). The `(X ⊗ Y).hom = pullback.fst ≫ X.hom` defeq unfolds, then
723	`GeometricallyIrreducible.comp` chains `pullback.fst`'s GI (by base-change stability of GI

729	    GeometricallyIrreducible ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom := by

10	/-!
11	# The identity component of the Picard scheme (A.3)
12	
13	This file is the **A.3** sub-build chapter for the project's positive-genus arm
14	of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It scaffolds the abstract identity-component
15	substrate for a `k`-group scheme locally of finite type and specialises it to
16	`G = Pic_{C/k}`, packaging:
17	
18	1. the open-and-closed subgroup-scheme structure of the identity component,
19	2. the degree map `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`,
20	3. the abelian-variety identification of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` (the Jacobian variety
21	   of `C` when `C/k` is a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of
22	   positive genus).
23	
24	## Status (iter-185 NEW file-skeleton)
25	
26	This file is the **iter-185 IdentityComponent** file-skeleton (NEW lane,
27	blueprint-reviewer HARD GATE cleared for the iter-184 plan-phase chapter
28	`Picard_IdentityComponent.tex`). Each of the five blueprint-pinned
29	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
30	`\lean{...}` pin in `Picard_IdentityComponent.tex`) with a `sorry` body. The
31	bodies are iter-186+ work; iter-185's mandate is the mechanical scaffold only.
32	
33	The 5 pinned declarations are:
34	
35	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.GroupScheme.IdentityComponent` (def, ~5 LOC) — the
36	   **identity component** `G^0` of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of finite
37	   type, as a `k`-scheme (an `Over (Spec k)`-object). Abstract substrate
38	   reusable outside the Picard context; not yet in Mathlib.
39	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`
40	   (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the bundled statement that `G^0` is an open and
41	   closed subscheme of `G` via an open immersion `G^0 ↪ G`.
42	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0Scheme` (def, ~5 LOC) — the **identity
43	   component of the Picard scheme** `Pic⁰_{C/k}`, obtained by applying
44	   `IdentityComponent` to `G = PicScheme C`.
45	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.degree` (def, ~5 LOC) — the **degree
46	   map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`, extracting the leading coefficient of the
47	   Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf relative to a fixed
48	   degree-one polarisation `O_C(1)`.
49	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pic0Scheme.isAbelianVariety` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
50	   — the **abelian-variety identification** of `Pic⁰_{C/k}`: smooth, proper,
51	   geometrically irreducible `k`-group scheme of dimension `g(C)` --- the
52	   Jacobian variety of `C`.
53	
54	## Note on type expressivity
55	
56	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
57	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
58	
59	- `IdentityComponent G : Over (Spec (.of k))` — a `k`-scheme, not the
60	  tautological `G` itself.
61	- `IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme G` — asserts the *existence* of an
62	  open immersion morphism `IdentityComponent G ⟶ G` whose underlying map of
63	  schemes is both an open and a closed immersion (clopen subscheme).
64	- `Pic0Scheme C : Over (Spec (.of k))` — a `k`-scheme.
65	- `PicScheme.degree C : (Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left) → ℤ` —
66	  a genuine function from `k`-points to `ℤ`, not a constant.
67	- `Pic0Scheme.isAbelianVariety C` — asserts the conjunction of the four
68	  abelian-variety properties (proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible,
69	  group-object structure); not vacuous because each conjunct is a genuine
70	  property/structure on the (typed-sorry) `Pic0Scheme C`.
71	
72	## References
73	
74	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_IdentityComponent.tex` (560 LOC,
75	5 pins). Sources:
76	- Kleiman, "The Picard scheme", §5, Lem.~`lem:agps` (identity component
77	  substrate) + Prp.~`prp:pic0` (specialisation to `Pic_{C/k}`) +
78	  Thm.~`th:qpp&p` (quasi-projectivity/projectivity) + Cor.~`cor:sm`
79	  (smoothness/dimension) + Ex.~`ex:jac` + Rmk.~`rmk:Jac`
80	  (arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 36, 38, 47, 50–51);
81	- Milne, "Abelian Varieties" (course notes, 2008), §III.1
82	  (def. of abelian variety, p. 8; dimension equals genus, Rmk. III.1.4(e),
83	  p. 86).

92	namespace AlgebraicGeometry

94	/-! ## §1. The identity component of a group scheme — abstract substrate
95	
96	The identity component is an abstract feature of `k`-group schemes locally of
97	finite type. We package the definition + open-and-closed-subgroup-scheme
98	structure in the `GroupScheme` namespace so they are reusable outside the
99	Picard context.
100	
101	In this project, a "`k`-group scheme" is encoded as an
102	`Over (Spec (.of k))`-object `G` carrying a `[GrpObj G]` instance (the
103	group-object structure in the over-category). The locally-of-finite-type
104	hypothesis is the `[LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]` instance on the structural
105	morphism to the base.
106	
107	Blueprint references: `def:identity_component_group_scheme` +
108	`thm:identity_component_open_subgroup` (Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`). -/
109	
110	namespace GroupScheme
111	
112	/-- The **identity component** `G^0` of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of
113	finite type.
114	
115	Encoded as a `k`-scheme: an object of `Over (Spec (.of k))`, carrying the
116	intended substantive identity "this is the connected component of `|G|`
117	containing the identity section `e`, equipped with the open-subscheme
118	structure inherited from `G`". The associated open immersion
119	`IdentityComponent G ⟶ G` is the content of
120	`IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`.
121	
122	iter-186+: the body constructs the identity component as the open subscheme
123	of `G` whose underlying topological space is the connected component of `|G|`
124	at the image of the identity section. The construction uses
125	`ConnectedComponents.mk` on the underlying topological space, then the
126	open-subscheme structure (EGA I 6.1.9: locally Noetherian spaces have open
127	connected components). For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed
128	`sorry`. -/
129	noncomputable def IdentityComponent {k : Type u} [Field k]
130	    (_G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
131	    [GrpObj _G] [LocallyOfFiniteType _G.hom] :
132	    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
133	  sorry
134	
135	/-- **The identity component is an open and closed subgroup scheme.**
136	
137	The bundled statement of Kleiman §5 Lem.~`lem:agps`~(3): the identity
138	component `G^0 = IdentityComponent G` of a `k`-group scheme `G` locally of
139	finite type comes with a morphism `IdentityComponent G ⟶ G` (in
140	`Over (Spec (.of k))`) whose underlying scheme morphism is both an open
141	immersion and a closed immersion (i.e. the inclusion of a clopen
142	subscheme).
143	
144	The full Kleiman conclusion also packages the group-subscheme property (the
145	inclusion is a homomorphism of `k`-group schemes), finite-type-ness over `k`
146	(`LocallyOfFiniteType` + quasi-compactness), geometric irreducibility, and
147	base-change-commutation. Those refinements live as separate instances /
148	follow-up lemmas in iter-186+; the file-skeleton pins only the clopen
149	open-immersion conclusion as a Nonempty-witness.
150	
151	iter-186+: the body constructs the inclusion morphism via the open-subscheme
152	structure of `IdentityComponent G`, applies EGA I 6.1.9 (locally Noetherian
153	⟹ open connected components) for the open-immersion conclusion, and applies
154	the "closure of a connected subspace is connected" elementary argument for
155	the closed-immersion conclusion. For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is
156	a typed `sorry`. -/
157	theorem IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
158	    (G : Over (Spec (.of k)))
159	    [GrpObj G] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom] :
160	    Nonempty {f : IdentityComponent G ⟶ G //
161	        IsOpenImmersion f.left ∧ IsClosedImmersion f.left} :=
162	  sorry
163	
164	end GroupScheme
165	
166	/-! ## §2. The identity component of the Picard scheme
167	
168	We specialise the abstract identity-component substrate to
169	`G = PicScheme C`, the Picard scheme of a smooth proper geometrically
170	integral curve `C/k` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`).
171	
172	Blueprint reference: `def:pic_zero_subscheme` (Kleiman §5 opening + Prp.
173	`prp:pic0`). -/
174	
175	namespace Scheme
176	
177	/-- The **identity component of the Picard scheme** `Pic⁰_{C/k}`.
178	
179	Defined as the identity component
180	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)` of the Picard scheme
181	`Pic_{C/k}` (from sibling `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`,
182	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme`). By
183	`GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme`, `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an
184	open and closed subgroup scheme of `Pic_{C/k}` of finite type over `k`,
185	geometrically irreducible, and its formation commutes with extension of the
186	base field.
187	
188	iter-186+: the body unwinds to `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent (PicScheme C)`
189	once the `[LocallyOfFiniteType (PicScheme C).hom]` instance lands on
190	`PicScheme C` (it follows from the Kleiman §4 structure: `Pic_{C/k}` is
191	locally of finite type as the disjoint union of open quasi-projective
192	`k`-subschemes). For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
193	noncomputable def Pic0Scheme {k : Type u} [Field k]
194	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
195	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
196	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
197	    Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
198	  sorry
199	
200	/-! ## §3. The degree map
201	
202	The disjoint-union structure of `Pic_{C/k}` (a disjoint union of open
203	quasi-projective `k`-subschemes, indexed by Hilbert polynomial via
204	`PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient`) stratifies its `T`-points by the leading
205	coefficient of the Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf
206	relative to a fixed degree-one polarisation. On `k`-points this gives the
207	**degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
208	
209	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree_pic` (Milne III.1, p.~88). -/
210	
211	namespace PicScheme
212	
213	/-- The **degree map** `Pic_{C/k}(k) → ℤ`.
214	
215	Sends a `k`-point `λ ∈ Pic_{C/k}(k)` --- a morphism
216	`Spec k ⟶ (PicScheme C).left` --- to the leading coefficient of the
217	Hilbert polynomial of a representing invertible sheaf `L` on `C` (relative
218	to a fixed degree-one polarisation `O_C(1)`). By Riemann--Roch,
219	`χ(C, L ⊗ O_C(n)) = n · deg L + 1 - g`, so the degree is the leading
220	coefficient of `Φ_L(n)`, well-defined on the isomorphism class `[L]` and on
221	the `k`-point `λ` (because `PicScheme C` represents the étale-sheafified
222	relative Picard functor).
223	
224	The degree map is a group homomorphism for the additive structure on
225	`Pic_{C/k}(k)` (tensor product on `L`) and the standard `(ℤ, +)`. The full
226	group-homomorphism refinement / functoriality in `k` lives as a follow-up
227	lemma in iter-186+; the file-skeleton pins only the underlying function.
228	
229	iter-186+: the body extracts the representing invertible sheaf from
230	`(PicScheme.representable C)`, forms its Hilbert polynomial via the project's
231	Hilbert-polynomial machinery (sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`), and returns
232	the leading coefficient. For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed
233	`sorry`. -/
234	noncomputable def degree {k : Type u} [Field k]
235	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
236	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
237	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
238	    (Spec (.of k) ⟶ (PicScheme C).left) → ℤ :=
239	  sorry
240	
241	end PicScheme
242	
243	/-! ## §4. `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety
244	
245	The terminal statement of the chapter identifies `Pic⁰_{C/k}` with an
246	abelian variety of dimension `g(C)` --- the Jacobian variety of `C`. In
247	this project, "abelian variety" is the conjunction of the four properties
248	`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]`
249	threaded through `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity` and consumed by
250	`AlgebraicJacobian.Albanese.AlbaneseUP`.
251	
252	Blueprint reference: `thm:pic_zero_is_abelian_variety` (Kleiman §5
253	Ex.~`ex:jac` + Rmk.~`rmk:Jac`; cf. Milne §I.1, Rmk. III.1.4(e)). -/
254	
255	namespace Pic0Scheme
256	
257	/-- **`Pic⁰_{C/k}` is an abelian variety.**
258	
259	For `C/k` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve of genus `g = g(C)`
260	over a field `k`, the identity component `Pic⁰_{C/k}` of the Picard scheme
261	is an *abelian variety over* `k`: a smooth, proper, geometrically
262	irreducible `k`-group scheme. (Commutativity follows automatically from
263	Milne §I.1, Cor. 1.4, and is not separately stated here.)
264	
265	The bundled statement: the four pieces `[IsProper] ∧ [Smooth] ∧
266	[GeometricallyIrreducible] ∧ Nonempty (GrpObj _)` hold on
267	`(Pic0Scheme C).hom` and `(Pic0Scheme C)` respectively.
268	
269	Dimension `g = g(C)`: not stated separately at the file-skeleton level
270	(it requires `dim_k (Pic0Scheme C).left = AlgebraicGeometry.genus C`, which
271	involves the Krull-dimension API on the underlying scheme); iter-186+
272	follow-up.
273	
274	iter-186+: the body assembles the four conjuncts from
275	- `GroupScheme.IdentityComponent.isOpenSubgroupScheme` (clopen subscheme of
276	  `PicScheme C`),
277	- Kleiman §5 Thm.~`th:qpp&p` (`X/k` projective + geom. integral ⟹
278	  `Pic⁰_{X/k}` quasi-projective; upgrade to projective when geom. normal,
279	  which holds for smooth proper curves of positive genus),
280	- Kleiman §5 Cor.~`cor:sm` + Ex.~`ex:jac` (smoothness of `Pic_{X/k}` at the
281	  identity for smooth proper curves; hence smooth of dimension
282	  `dim_k H¹(C, O_C) = g(C)` everywhere by uniform smoothness on the open
283	  identity component).
284	For the iter-185 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
285	theorem isAbelianVariety {k : Type u} [Field k]
286	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
287	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
288	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
289	    IsProper (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧ Smooth (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
290	      GeometricallyIrreducible (Pic0Scheme C).hom ∧
291	      Nonempty (GrpObj (Pic0Scheme C)) :=
292	  sorry
293	
294	end Pic0Scheme
295	
296	end Scheme
297	
298	end AlgebraicGeometry

8	/-!
9	# The Quot scheme (A.2.b)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman Quot-scheme construction
14	`Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` — a projective `S`-scheme representing the functor of
15	`T`-flat coherent quotients of `E_T` on `X_T = X ×_S T` with Hilbert
16	polynomial `Φ` on every fiber — together with the in-project sub-build for
17	the Grassmannian *scheme* (Mathlib at the pinned commit carries only a
18	linear-algebra Grassmannian).
19	
20	## Status (iter-176 Lane H file-skeleton — re-dispatch)
21	
22	iter-175 Lane H died to the Anthropic session-limit reset window without
23	ever calling `Write` (the file was never created). iter-176 re-dispatches
24	the file-skeleton verbatim. Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations
25	carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
26	`\lean{...}` pin in `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`) with a
27	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-177+ work; the substantive proofs are
28	deep (Nitsure §5: boundedness ⟶ Grassmannian embedding ⟶ flattening
29	stratification ⟶ valuative criterion).
30	
31	The 6 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
32	
33	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` (def, ~5 LOC) — the
34	   **Hilbert polynomial function** `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]` of a coherent
35	   sheaf `F` on `X` over a finite-type `π : X ⟶ S` with respect to a
36	   line bundle `L`. Encoded as a function `S → Polynomial ℚ`.
37	
38	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` (def, ~6 LOC) — the **Quot
39	   functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` sending an
40	   `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the set of equivalence classes
41	   `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with `F` a `T`-flat coherent sheaf on
42	   `X_T`, `q : E_T ↠ F` a surjection, and `F|_{X_t}` having Hilbert
43	   polynomial `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
44	
45	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian` (def, ~5 LOC) — the
46	   **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
47	   rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V`.
48	
49	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable` (theorem, ~8 LOC)
50	   — the **representability of the Grassmannian** by a smooth projective
51	   `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with
52	   the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
53	
54	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the
55	   **Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman representability theorem** for the Quot
56	   functor: for a noetherian `S`, a projective `π : X ⟶ S`, a relatively
57	   very ample `L` on `X`, a coherent `E`, and `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`, the functor
58	   `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` is representable by a projective `S`-scheme.
59	
60	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChangeCohomology` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the
61	   **flat base-change theorem of cohomology** (Stacks tag 02KH): for a
62	   cartesian square with `g` flat and `f` quasi-compact quasi-separated,
63	   the canonical base-change map `g* (f_* F) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* F)` is an
64	   isomorphism. The current scaffold encodes the `i = 0` direct-image
65	   form (substantive content of (ii) of the Stacks 02KH statement); the
66	   `R^i f_*` form for `i ≥ 1` requires the higher-direct-image
67	   infrastructure not present at the pinned commit.
68	
69	## Note on type expressivity
70	
71	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
72	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
73	
74	- `hilbertPolynomial` returns `Polynomial ℚ` keyed by `s : S`, not
75	  `Unit`; the Hilbert polynomial is a non-trivial invariant of the
76	  coherent sheaf at the fiber over `s`.
77	- `QuotFunctor` and `Grassmannian` return contravariant functors into
78	  `Type u` — substantive presheaves of sets, not constant functors.
79	- `Grassmannian.representable` and `QuotScheme` package the
80	  `Functor.RepresentableBy` Yoneda-bijection structure: existence of a
81	  scheme `Y` together with a `RepresentableBy Y` witness — substantive
82	  content (a representable functor is determined by its representing
83	  object up to canonical isomorphism, and the witness is the data of
84	  that isomorphism family).
85	- `flatBaseChangeCohomology` produces a `Nonempty (... ≅ ...)` of an
86	  isomorphism between two `S'`-modules built via the pullback/pushforward
87	  bifunctor; the iso is non-trivial (it is `Stacks 02KH` content, not
88	  the identity-on-the-same-object iso `Iso.refl _`).
89	
90	## Mathlib status
91	
92	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides:
93	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` (the category `X.Modules`),
94	- `Scheme.Modules.pullback`, `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` (the
95	  pullback–pushforward adjunction at level `i = 0`),
96	- `CategoryTheory.IsPullback` for cartesian squares,
97	- `CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy` for representable functors,
98	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat`, `AlgebraicGeometry.QuasiCompact`,
99	  `AlgebraicGeometry.QuasiSeparated`, `AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper`,
100	  `AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType`, `AlgebraicGeometry.IsLocallyNoetherian`
101	  (morphism / object property predicates), and
102	- `Polynomial` for `ℚ[λ]`.
103	
104	Mathlib does NOT provide (at the pinned commit):
105	- a Grassmannian *scheme* (only a linear-algebra Grassmannian
106	  as a finite-rank-quotient variety),
107	- a `IsProjective` morphism property,
108	- the Quot/Hilbert functor or its representability,
109	- `R^i f_*` higher direct images on `Scheme.Modules`,
110	- Castelnuovo–Mumford `m`-regularity,
111	- Snapper's Lemma for the polynomial property of Euler characteristics.
112	
113	The current file-skeleton uses `IsProper π` as the structural stand-in
114	for "projective `π`" (every projective morphism is proper; the
115	restriction is harmless in the Route A consumer setting where `π` comes
116	from a smooth proper curve, which is automatically projective).
117	iter-177+ refinement: once Mathlib gains an `IsProjective` morphism
118	property, the hypothesis tightens.

139	/-! ## §1. Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf
140	
141	For a finite-type morphism `π : X ⟶ S` with `S` noetherian and a coherent
142	sheaf `F` on `X` whose schematic support is proper over `S` (here encoded
143	as plain `X.Modules` for the file-skeleton), the per-fiber Hilbert
144	polynomial is the function
145	
146	`s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ],   Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`.
147	
148	Snapper's Lemma ensures this is a polynomial in `m`; the proof requires
149	graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure and is not stated here.
150	
151	Blueprint reference: `def:hilbert_polynomial` (Nitsure §1; cf. Hartshorne
152	III.5.2). -/
153	
154	/-- The **Hilbert polynomial** of a coherent sheaf `F` on `X` over `S` at
155	the fiber over `s ∈ S` with respect to a line bundle `L` on `X`.
156	
157	Encoded as a function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]`. The defining formula
158	
159	`Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗ m})
160	            = Σ_i (-1)^i dim_{κ(s)} H^i(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`
161	
162	is a polynomial in `m` by Snapper's Lemma; the polynomial coefficients
163	depend on `s` through the fiber `F|_{X_s}`. When `F` is `S`-flat the
164	function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s}` is locally constant on `S`.
165	
166	iter-177+: the body unfolds to the graded-Euler-characteristic
167	construction once `χ` of a coherent sheaf on a noetherian scheme +
168	Snapper's polynomial-eventually-property are in scope. For the iter-176
169	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
170	noncomputable def hilbertPolynomial {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
171	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _F : X.Modules) (_s : S) :
172	    Polynomial ℚ :=
173	  sorry
174	
175	/-! ## §2. The Quot functor
176	
177	The Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` sends an `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the
178	set of equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` where
179	- `F` is a coherent sheaf on `X_T = X ×_S T` whose schematic support is
180	  proper over `T` and which is `T`-flat,
181	- `q : E_T ↠ F` is a surjective `O_{X_T}`-linear homomorphism,
182	- the fiberwise Hilbert polynomial of `F|_{X_t}` with respect to `L|_{X_t}`
183	  equals `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
184	
185	Two pairs `(F, q)` and `(F', q')` are equivalent iff `ker(q) = ker(q')`.
186	
187	The Hilbert scheme is the special case `E = O_X`:
188	`Hilb^{Φ,L}_{X/S} = Quot^{Φ,L}_{O_X/X/S}`.
189	
190	Blueprint reference: `def:quot_functor` (Nitsure §1; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
191	
192	/-- The **Quot functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of coherent quotients of `E`
193	on `X ×_S -` with Hilbert polynomial `Φ`.
194	
195	Encoded as a contravariant functor `(Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u`, sending an
196	`S`-scheme `T → S` (i.e. an object of `Over S`) to the set of
197	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` of a `T`-flat coherent
198	sheaf `F` on `X ×_S T` with proper support and a surjection
199	`q : E_T ↠ F` whose fiberwise Hilbert polynomial is `Φ`, modulo
200	`ker(q) = ker(q')`. Functoriality is pullback of the quotient along
201	`X ×_S T' ⟶ X ×_S T`.
202	
203	iter-177+: the body packages the on-objects / on-morphisms data using the
204	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` bifunctor on the relative product
205	`X ×_S T`, with the equivalence relation `ker(q) = ker(q')` quotiented
206	out via `Setoid` / `Quotient`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body
207	is a typed `sorry`. -/
208	noncomputable def QuotFunctor {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
209	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _E : X.Modules)
210	    (_Φ : Polynomial ℚ) :
211	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=
212	  sorry
213	
214	end Scheme
215	
216	/-! ## §3. The Grassmannian scheme
217	
218	Since Mathlib carries no Grassmannian *scheme*, we encode it here as a
219	contravariant functor on `Over S` together with a representability
220	statement. The construction proceeds by gluing `binom(r, d)` affine
221	charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the Plücker cocycle, yielding a smooth
222	projective `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`,
223	equipped with a tautological rank-`d` quotient
224	`π* V ↠ U` and the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
225	
226	Blueprint references: `def:grassmannian_scheme`,
227	`thm:grassmannian_representable` (Nitsure §1 Exercise (2),
228	"Construction of Grassmannian"; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
229	
230	namespace Scheme
231	
232	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
233	rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r ≥ d`.
234	
235	Encoded as the functor sending an `S`-scheme `T → S` to the set of
236	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with
237	`q : V_T ↠ F` a surjection of `O_T`-modules and `F` locally free of
238	rank `d`, modulo `ker(q) = ker(q')`. Concretely
239	`Grass(V, d) = Quot^{d, O_S}_{V/S/S}` (the Quot functor for `X = S`,
240	`E = V`, constant Hilbert polynomial `d`).
241	
242	iter-177+: the body re-exports `QuotFunctor (𝟙 S) (?) V Φ_d`, where
243	`Φ_d : Polynomial ℚ` is the constant polynomial `d`. For the iter-176
244	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
245	noncomputable def Grassmannian {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
246	    (_V : S.Modules) (_d : ℕ) :
247	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=
248	  sorry
249	
250	/-- **Representability of the Grassmannian.**
251	
252	For a noetherian scheme `S`, a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r`,
253	and `1 ≤ d ≤ r`, the Grassmannian functor `Grass(V, d)` of
254	`Grassmannian` is representable by a smooth projective `S`-scheme
255	`Gr_S(V, d) ⟶ S` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with a
256	tautological rank-`d` quotient `π* V ↠ U`. The determinant line bundle
257	`det(U)` is relatively very ample, giving a Plücker closed embedding
258	`Gr_S(V, d) ↪ ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
259	
260	We package the conclusion as the existence of a representing
261	`Y : Over S` together with a `Functor.RepresentableBy Y` witness for
262	`Grassmannian V d`; the additional projective / smooth / Plücker
263	structure is implicit in the construction and is iter-177+ refinement
264	work (once the proof body lands).
265	
266	iter-177+: the body follows Nitsure §1 "Construction of Grassmannian":
267	glue the `binom(r, d)` affine charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the
268	Plücker cocycle, verify separatedness via the diagonal cut, verify
269	properness by the DVR valuative criterion, build the tautological
270	quotient `U`, exhibit the Plücker embedding via the determinant line
271	bundle. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
272	theorem Grassmannian.representable {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
273	    (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) :
274	    ∃ (Y : Over S), Nonempty ((Grassmannian V d).RepresentableBy Y) := by
275	  sorry
276	
277	/-! ## §4. Representability of the Quot scheme
278	
279	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman: for a noetherian `S`, a projective
280	`π : X ⟶ S`, a relatively very ample `L` on `X`, a coherent
281	`E` on `X`, and `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`, the Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` is
282	representable by a *projective* `S`-scheme.
283	
284	The proof has four steps (Nitsure §5):
285	1. **Boundedness** via Castelnuovo–Mumford `m`-regularity (uniform across
286	   fibers of `π` and across coherent quotients of `E_s` with Hilbert
287	   polynomial `Φ`).
288	2. **Grassmannian embedding**
289	   `α : Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} ↪ Grass(W ⊗_{O_S} Sym^r V, Φ(r))`
290	   for `r ≥ m`, sending `⟨F, q⟩ ↦ ⟨(π_T)_* F(r), (π_T)_*(q(r))⟩`.
291	3. **Locally closed in Grassmannian** via the flattening stratification
292	   applied to the universal cokernel on the Grassmannian, producing the
293	   stratum `T_0^Φ`.
294	4. **Closed embedding** by the valuative criterion of properness for
295	   DVRs.
296	
297	The reduction to the universal case `X = ℙ(V)`, `E = π*W` is recorded as
298	`lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W` in the blueprint chapter.
299	
300	Blueprint reference: `thm:quot_representable` (Nitsure §5; FGA Explained
301	Ch. 5; Grothendieck, FGA TDTE-IV). -/
302	
303	/-- **Representability of the Quot scheme** (Grothendieck, Altman–Kleiman).
304	
305	Let `S` be a noetherian scheme, `π : X ⟶ S` a projective morphism (here
306	encoded as a proper `LocallyOfFiniteType` morphism; the projectivity
307	upgrades once `IsProjective` lands in Mathlib), `L` a line bundle on `X`
308	(relatively very ample), `E` a coherent `O_X`-module, and
309	`Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`. Then the Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of `QuotFunctor`
310	is representable by an `S`-scheme.
311	
312	We package the conclusion as the existence of `Q : Over S` together with
313	a `Functor.RepresentableBy Q` witness for `QuotFunctor π L E Φ`; the
314	*projectivity* of `Q ⟶ S` (and the universal quotient
315	`q^univ : π^*_Q E ↠ F^univ` on `X ×_S Q^{Φ,L}`) is implicit in the
316	construction (Plücker-embedded into a projective Grassmannian over `S`)
317	and is iter-177+ refinement work.
318	
319	iter-177+: the body follows the four-step Nitsure §5 proof
320	(boundedness ⟶ Grassmannian embedding ⟶ flattening stratification ⟶
321	valuative-criterion closed embedding); the sub-lemmas live in
322	`lem:quot_boundedness`, `lem:quot_alpha_injective`,
323	`lem:quot_valuative_criterion`, and the existential reduction in
324	`lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the
325	body is a typed `sorry`. -/
326	theorem QuotScheme {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
327	    (π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType π] [IsProper π]
328	    (L E : X.Modules) (Φ : Polynomial ℚ) :
329	    ∃ (Q : Over S), Nonempty ((QuotFunctor π L E Φ).RepresentableBy Q) := by
330	  sorry
331	
332	end Scheme
333	
334	/-! ## §5. Cohomology and base change
335	
336	The Quot construction uses cohomology-and-base-change in two places: the
337	boundedness step (Nitsure §5 "Use of m-Regularity") and the Grassmannian
338	embedding (Nitsure §5 "Embedding Quot into Grassmannian"). We record the
339	relevant statement as a named theorem so the Lean encoding can cite it
340	directly.
341	
342	The Stacks 02KH form is the statement for higher direct images
343	`R^i f_*` on quasi-coherent sheaves; for the iter-176 file-skeleton we
344	state the `i = 0` form on `Scheme.Modules`, which is the substantive
345	content of `lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology(ii)` of Stacks 02KH. The
346	`R^i` form is iter-177+ work after higher-direct-image infrastructure
347	is in scope.
348	
349	Blueprint reference: `thm:flat_base_change_cohomology` (Stacks 02KH). -/
350	
351	/-! ### Flat base change of cohomology (Stacks tag 02KH, `i = 0` form)
352	
353	Let
354	```
355	   g'
356	X' ───→ X
357	│       │
358	f'      f
359	↓       ↓
360	S' ───→ S
361	   g
362	```
363	be a cartesian square of schemes with `g` flat and `f` quasi-compact
364	quasi-separated. Let `F` be a sheaf of `O_X`-modules. Then the canonical
365	base-change map `g* (f_* F) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* F)` is an isomorphism in
366	`S'.Modules`.
367	
368	(The full Stacks 02KH statement covers all higher direct images
369	`R^i f_* F` for `i ≥ 0`; the `i = 0` form encoded here is the
370	substantive content of `lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology(ii)` of
371	Stacks 02KH, with the `i ≥ 1` form post-iter-177 work after the
372	higher-direct-image bifunctor lands.)
373	
374	iter-177 (Lane QS-FLAT): the body constructs the canonical base-change
375	natural transformation via the mate equivalence of the
376	`pullback ⊣ pushforward` adjunction (Mathlib's `mateEquiv` of
377	`Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction`), then exhibits the iso
378	via the `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso` helper. The deep mathematical
379	content (Stacks tag 02KH / 02KE / 00H8) lives entirely in the helper;
380	it reduces affine-locally to: for a flat ring map `A → B` and an
381	`A`-algebra `R`, the canonical map `B ⊗_A H^i(X, F) → H^i(X_B, F_B)`
382	is an iso for any quasi-coherent `F` (the `i = 0` form is what we use).
383	The helper remains a typed `sorry` pending the affine-local reduction
384	+ algebraic flat base change; this is iter-178+ body work after
385	quasi-compact open-cover Mayer-Vietoris infrastructure is in scope. -/
386	
387	/-- The canonical base-change natural transformation `g* (f_* -) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* -)`
388	associated to a cartesian square
389	```
390	     g'
391	X' ─────→ X
392	│         │
393	f'        f
394	↓         ↓
395	S' ─────→ S
396	     g
397	```
398	in `Scheme`. Constructed as the *mate* (Beck–Chevalley transform)
399	under the `pullback ⊣ pushforward` adjunctions on sheaves of modules
400	of the canonical 2-isomorphism
401	`pullback g ⋙ pullback f' ≅ pullback f ⋙ pullback g'` coming from
402	`g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`.
403	
404	This natural transformation always exists (no flatness needed). The
405	content of the flat base-change theorem (Stacks tag 02KH) is the
406	*isomorphism* claim under the hypotheses

751	/-- **Flat base-change is an isomorphism** (Stacks tag 02KH, `i = 0`).
752	
753	The canonical base-change natural transformation `canonicalBaseChangeMap`
754	is an isomorphism at every coherent sheaf `F` under the hypotheses
755	`[QuasiCompact f]`, `[QuasiSeparated f]`, `[Flat g]`.
756	
757	The proof reduces section-wise via `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`
758	to the section-form helper `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso`,
759	which captures Stacks 02KH(ii) — the substantive algebraic content
760	(`Module.Flat.isBaseChange` on each affine open + Mayer-Vietoris for
761	quasi-separated `f`). -/
762	theorem canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}
763	    {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
764	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g)
765	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] [Flat g]
766	    (F : X.Modules) :
767	    IsIso ((canonicalBaseChangeMap sq).app F) :=
768	  Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app.mpr
769	    (fun U => canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso sq F U)
770	
771	theorem flatBaseChangeCohomology {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}
772	    {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
773	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g)
774	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] [Flat g]
775	    (F : X.Modules) :
776	    Nonempty ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj
777	                ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj F) ≅
778	              (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f').obj
779	                ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F)) :=
780	  -- Build the canonical Beck-Chevalley base-change map and wrap it in `asIso`
781	  -- using the iso-claim from `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso`.
782	  ⟨@asIso _ _ _ _ _ (canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso sq F)⟩
783	
784	end AlgebraicGeometry

12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the relative-spectrum
13	functor `Spec_X(𝒜) : QcohAlg(X)^op ⥤ Sch/X` used by the relative Picard functor on a
14	product `C ×_k T`.

94	namespace Scheme

96	/-! ## §1. Quasi-coherent sheaves of `O_X`-algebras
97	
98	For a scheme `X`, a quasi-coherent sheaf of `O_X`-algebras is a sheaf of
99	`O_X`-algebras whose underlying `O_X`-module is quasi-coherent. Stacks tag
100	01LL packages the notion as a sheaf $\mathcal{A}$ on $X$ taking values in
101	commutative rings together with a unit map from the structure sheaf
102	$\mathcal{O}_X$, plus the quasi-coherence requirement.
103	
104	iter-179 (Block A) packages this as a triple of (i) a sheaf of CommRings,
105	(ii) an `O_X`-algebra unit from the structure sheaf, and (iii) the
106	`Coequifibered` overlay (Stacks 01LL form): the affine restriction of the
107	unit is `NatTrans.Coequifibered`, i.e. on every affine open `U` and section
108	`f`, the restriction-to-basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is `IsLocalization.Away f`.
109	This is the strictly-weaker, sheafified-tensor-free formulation that
110	Mathlib's `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData` consumes
111	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`); it is
112	equivalent to the full `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` predicate under the
113	dense-subsite equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. See
114	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` for the iter-178 consult that
115	identified this idiom.
116	
117	Blueprint reference: `def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras` (Stacks 01LL,
118	situation-relative-spec). -/
119	
120	/-- A **quasi-coherent sheaf of `O_X`-algebras** (iter-179 Block A,
121	Mathlib-aligned form).
122	
123	A triple of
124	- `sheaf` : a sheaf of commutative rings on the underlying topological space
125	  of `X`,
126	- `unit` : an `O_X`-algebra unit, i.e. a morphism of sheaves of commutative
127	  rings `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf` from the structure sheaf to the carrier, and
128	- `coequifibered` : the Stacks-01LL form of quasi-coherence — every
129	  restriction of `sheaf` to a basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is the
130	  `IsLocalization.Away f` of `sheaf U`. This is the exact predicate
131	  consumed by `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
132	  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`).
133	
134	The shape matches the input of Mathlib's relative-gluing construction
135	verbatim, so `RelativeSpec` is defined directly as
136	`(relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`. The
137	`NatTrans.Coequifibered` predicate is strictly weaker than the full
138	sheaf-of-modules quasi-coherence predicate
139	`SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent`, but equivalent under the dense-subsite
140	equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`, so no information is lost. See
141	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 2 for the consult that
142	identified this carrier shape. -/
143	structure QcohAlgebra (X : Scheme.{u}) where
144	  /-- The underlying sheaf of commutative rings on `X`. -/
145	  sheaf : TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat.{u} X.toPresheafedSpace
146	  /-- The `O_X`-algebra unit `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf` exhibiting `sheaf` as a sheaf
147	  of `O_X`-algebras. -/
148	  unit : X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf
149	  /-- **Stacks 01LL quasi-coherence overlay (`Coequifibered` form)**: every
150	  restriction of `sheaf` to a basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is `IsLocalization.Away f`.
151	  Strictly weaker than `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` (which needs
152	  sheafified-tensor infrastructure not yet in Mathlib); equivalent under the
153	  dense-subsite equivalence `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. This is the
154	  exact predicate consumed by `AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
155	  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`); see
156	  `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 2. -/
157	  coequifibered : NatTrans.Coequifibered
158	    (Functor.whiskerLeft (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op unit.hom)
159	
160	namespace RelativeSpec
161	
162	/-! ## §2. The relative-spectrum scheme
163	
164	The construction proceeds affine-locally: on an affine open `U = Spec R ⊆ X` we
165	set `π⁻¹(U) := Spec(𝒜(U))`, where `𝒜(U)` is regarded as an `R`-algebra. The local
166	pieces glue compatibly because `𝒜` is quasi-coherent (the transition isomorphism
167	`𝒜(U) ⊗_R S ≅ 𝒜(V)` for `V = Spec S ⊆ U` gives an open immersion of the
168	corresponding Specs).
169	
170	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_exists` (Stacks 01LQ
171	lemma-glue-relative-spec). -/
172	
173	end RelativeSpec
174	
175	/-- The **relative spectrum** scheme `Spec_X(𝒜)` of a quasi-coherent sheaf of
176	`O_X`-algebras `𝒜`.
177	
178	**iter-179 body (Block A)**: built as the canonical Mathlib value
179	`(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`
180	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:102`,
181	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`). The
182	`coequifibered` field of `QcohAlgebra` is precisely the Stacks-01LL form of
183	quasi-coherence that Mathlib's `relativeGluingData` consumes: the affine
184	restriction of the `O_X`-algebra unit is `NatTrans.Coequifibered`, i.e. every
185	basic-open restriction is an `IsLocalization.Away`. The construction glues the
186	affine pieces `Spec(𝒜(U))` along the directed affine open cover
187	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.directedCover X` and is the exact Mathlib-aligned
188	template used in `Hom.normalization`
189	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Normalization.lean:120`); see
190	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` for the consult that identified this
191	idiom. -/
192	noncomputable def RelativeSpec {X : Scheme.{u}} (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : Scheme.{u} :=
193	  (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued
194	
195	/-- The **structure morphism** `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` of the relative spectrum.
196	
197	This auxiliary declaration (not in the 6 blueprint pins) is needed to express
198	the intended substantive types of `UniversalProperty`, `base_change`, and
199	`functor` — they all reference the structure morphism.
200	
201	**iter-179 body (Block A)**: built as the canonical Mathlib value
202	`(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).toBase`
203	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:114`). The map is the colimit
204	descent of the natural transformation `Spec(𝒜(U)) → U` over the directed
205	affine open cover; see `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 3.
206	
207	Blueprint reference: implicit in `thm:relative_spec_exists`. -/
208	noncomputable def RelativeSpec.structureMorphism {X : Scheme.{u}}
209	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : X.RelativeSpec 𝒜 ⟶ X :=
210	  (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).toBase
211	
212	/-! ### Note: `QcohAlgebra.pullback` constructor (iter-179 Lane B helper)
213	
214	Given a morphism `g : T ⟶ X` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the
215	pulled-back qcoh algebra `g^* 𝒜 : T.QcohAlgebra` is realised as the
216	pushforward of the structure sheaf of the relative-spec pullback. The
217	`sheaf` and `unit` fields are concrete (the topological pushforward of the
218	pullback's structure sheaf and the canonical natural transformation `q.c`
219	respectively, where `q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`). The
220	`coequifibered` field is the Mathlib-gap claim that pushforward along an
221	affine morphism preserves the Stacks-01LL Coequifibered overlay
222	(Stacks 01LR pullback compatibility): `q` is affine via
223	`MorphismProperty.pullback_fst` applied to `UniversalProperty 𝒜`, so on
224	every affine `U ⊆ T` with section `f ∈ Γ(T, U)` we have
225	`Γ(P, q⁻¹(D(f))) = Γ(P, q⁻¹U)[1/q.app f]`, i.e. the basic-open restriction
226	of the pushforward is the relevant `IsLocalization.Away`. iter-180 Lane C
227	factors this into two named helpers
228	(`QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom`,
229	`QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered`) declared after the universal
230	property has landed; the definition itself is also placed after the
231	namespace so it can consume those helpers. -/
232	
233	namespace RelativeSpec
234	
235	/-! ## §3. Universal property — affine structure morphism
236	
237	The Stacks 01LQ universal property says `Spec_X(𝒜)` represents the functor
238	sending an `X`-scheme `g : T → X` to the set of `O_X`-algebra maps
239	`𝒜 → g_* O_T`. A direct structural consequence of representability is that the
240	structure morphism `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` is *affine* (Stacks 01LR
241	lemma-spec-properties, immediate corollary). For the iter-173 file-skeleton we
242	encode the universal property by this affine-morphism consequence — the
243	substantive content is the same up to body unfolding, and the type is
244	non-tautological.
245	
246	iter-174+: refine the signature to a `CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy`
247	witness against the functor of `O_X`-algebra maps once `QcohAlgebra` is
248	unpacked and the Hom-set on algebras is available.
249	
250	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_univ` (Stacks 01LQ lemma-spec). -/
251	
252	/-- **Universal property of the relative spectrum (affine-structure form).**
253	
254	The structure morphism `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` of the relative spectrum is affine.
255	This is the substantive consequence of the Stacks 01LQ representability
256	statement (an `X`-scheme is the relative spectrum of some quasi-coherent
257	algebra iff its structure morphism is affine).
258	
259	iter-174+: refine the type signature to the full Yoneda-bijection statement
260	`Hom_X(T, Spec_X(𝒜)) ≃ Hom_{O_X-alg}(𝒜, g_* O_T)` once `QcohAlgebra` is
261	unpacked and an `O_X`-algebra Hom-set is in scope. The current type is the
262	non-tautological structural consequence used downstream by `affine_base_iff`
263	and `base_change`. -/
264	theorem UniversalProperty {X : Scheme.{u}} (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
265	    IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) := by
266	  -- Mathlib `relativeGluingData` builder; per `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
267	  -- Block B. We invoke `isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen`: for each `x : X`
268	  -- pick an affine open `U ∋ x` (every `X` has such by `exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset`),
269	  -- and identify the structure-morphism preimage of `U` with the range of the colimit
270	  -- inclusion of the affine fiber `Spec(𝒜(U))` (via
271	  -- `Cover.RelativeGluingData.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι`); affineness of the
272	  -- opens-range then follows from `isAffineOpen_opensRange` since the source is
273	  -- `Scheme.Spec` of `𝒜.sheaf.val.obj (op U)`, an affine scheme.
274	  apply isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen
275	  intro x
276	  obtain ⟨U, hU, hxU, _⟩ :=
277	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (U := ⊤) (Set.mem_univ x)
278	  refine ⟨U, hxU, hU, ?_⟩
279	  set d := AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered
280	  let i : X.AffineZariskiSite := ⟨U, hU⟩
281	  change IsAffineOpen (d.toBase ⁻¹ᵁ U)
282	  have key : d.toBase ⁻¹ᵁ U = (d.cover.f i).opensRange := by
283	    have h := d.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι i
284	    simpa [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι] using h
285	  rw [key]
286	  have : IsAffine (d.cover.X i) := by
287	    change IsAffine (Scheme.Spec.obj _)
288	    infer_instance
289	  exact isAffineOpen_opensRange _
290	
291	/-! ## §4. Affine base case
292	
293	When the base `X = Spec R` is affine, `Spec_X(𝒜)` reduces to the absolute
294	spectrum of the global sections: `Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))`. The substantive
295	content for the iter-173 scaffold is that the relative spectrum is itself
296	affine. This is Stacks 01LO lemma-spec-affine.
297	
298	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_affine_base`. -/
299	
300	/-- **Affine-base reduction of the relative spectrum.**
301	
302	For an affine scheme `X = Spec R` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`,
303	the relative spectrum `Spec_X(𝒜)` is itself an affine scheme. (More precisely,
304	there is a canonical isomorphism `Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))`, but extracting
305	`Γ(X, 𝒜) : CommRingCat` requires the unpacked structure of `QcohAlgebra`,
306	which is iter-174+ work.)
307	
308	iter-174+: refine to the full statement
309	`Nonempty ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜 ≅ Spec (Γ((Spec R), 𝒜)))`
310	once `Γ` for `QcohAlgebra` is in scope. -/
311	theorem affine_base_iff {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (𝒜 : (Spec R).QcohAlgebra) :
312	    IsAffine ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜) := by
313	  -- Affineness of the relative-spec total space when the base is affine is the
314	  -- standard "affine over affine is affine" consequence: by
315	  -- `UniversalProperty` the structure morphism is `IsAffineHom`, and `Spec R`
316	  -- is itself `IsAffine`, so `isAffine_of_isAffineHom` closes the goal.
317	  have h : IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) := UniversalProperty 𝒜
318	  exact isAffine_of_isAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
319	
320	end RelativeSpec
321	
322	/-! ## §4.5 `QcohAlgebra.pullback` helpers (iter-180 Lane C)
323	
324	Two named helpers feeding the `coequifibered` field of the `QcohAlgebra.pullback`
325	constructor below. Helper budget = 2 per iter-180 plan. -/
326	
327	/-- **Affineness of the structural pullback projection.**
328	
329	Given `g : T ⟶ X` and `𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra`, the projection
330	`q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜) : (T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜)) ⟶ T` is an affine
331	morphism. Combined with `RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty 𝒜` (which exhibits the
332	relative-spec structure morphism as affine) and the fact that affineness is
333	stable under base change (`MorphismProperty.pullback_fst` on `@isAffineHom`),
334	this is purely an instance derivation. -/
335	lemma QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
336	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
337	    IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
338	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) := by
339	  haveI : IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) :=
340	    RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty 𝒜
341	  exact MorphismProperty.pullback_fst _ _ inferInstance
342	
343	/-- **Stacks 01LL Coequifibered overlay for the pushforward of `O_P` along an
344	affine `q : P ⟶ T`** (iter-180 Lane C, Stacks 01LR pushforward compatibility).
345	
346	Per the Mathlib characterization
347	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway`, the
348	claim reduces to: for every affine open `U ⊆ T` and section `r : Γ(T, U.1)`,
349	the basic-open restriction `q_* O_P (D(r)) = Γ(P, q⁻¹(D(r)))` is the
350	`IsLocalization.Away` of `q.app r` over `Γ(P, q⁻¹U)`. The named helper
351	`pullback_fst_isAffineHom` exhibits `q` as affine, so every preimage
352	`q⁻¹U` of an affine open `U` is itself affine; combined with
353	`Scheme.Hom.preimage_basicOpen` (which identifies `q⁻¹(T.basicOpen r)` with
354	`P.basicOpen (q.app U.1 r)`), `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`
355	supplies the localization with the desired algebra structure (restriction
356	along the inclusion `q⁻¹(U.basicOpen r) ⊆ q⁻¹U` induced by
357	`q.preimage_mono` on the AffineZariski-site inclusion). -/
358	lemma QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
359	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
360	    NatTrans.Coequifibered ((AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor T).op.whiskerLeft
361	      ({ hom := (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
362	            (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).c } :
363	        T.sheaf ⟶ (TopCat.Sheaf.pushforward CommRingCat.{u}
364	          (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
365	            (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).base).obj
366	          (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g
367	            (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).sheaf).hom) := by
368	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
369	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
370	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
371	  refine AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway.mpr
372	    fun U r => ?_
373	  set q := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
374	  have hqU : IsAffineOpen (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1) := U.2.preimage q
375	  have hle : (U.basicOpen r).toOpens ≤ U.toOpens := T.basicOpen_le r
376	  exact hqU.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen (q.app U.1 r)
377	    (homOfLE (q.preimage_mono hle)) (q.preimage_basicOpen r)

379	/-- **Base change of a quasi-coherent algebra (pushforward formulation).**
380	
381	Given a morphism `g : T ⟶ X` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the
382	pulled-back qcoh algebra `g^* 𝒜 : T.QcohAlgebra` is realised as the
383	pushforward of the structure sheaf of the relative-spec pullback. The
384	`sheaf` and `unit` fields are concrete (the topological pushforward of the
385	pullback's structure sheaf and the canonical natural transformation `q.c`
386	respectively, where `q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`). The
387	`coequifibered` field is supplied by the named helper
388	`QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered`, which packages the Stacks-01LR
389	pushforward-along-affine-morphism compatibility. -/
390	noncomputable def QcohAlgebra.pullback {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
391	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : T.QcohAlgebra :=
392	  let q : CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ⟶ T :=
393	    CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
394	  { sheaf := (TopCat.Sheaf.pushforward CommRingCat.{u} q.base).obj
395	      (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).sheaf
396	    unit := ⟨q.c⟩
397	    coequifibered := QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered g 𝒜 }
398	
399	namespace RelativeSpec
400	
401	/-! ## §5. Base change
402	
403	The relative spectrum commutes with base change: for a morphism `g : T → X`
404	and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the pullback `g^* 𝒜` is a
405	quasi-coherent `O_T`-algebra and
406	`T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec_T(g^* 𝒜)`.
407	
408	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_base_change` (Stacks 01LS
409	lemma-spec-base-change). -/
410	
411	/-! ### Helpers for `base_change` (iter-179 Lane B + iter-181 Lane D)
412	
413	The base-change theorem packages the canonical iso content into named helpers
414	(`pullback_iso_affine_piece`, `pullback_iso_construction`, and `pullback_iso`,
415	below) and consumes the file-level `Scheme.QcohAlgebra.pullback` constructor
416	declared just above the namespace. -/
417	
418	/-- **Per-affine-open identification for the base-change iso (iter-181 Lane D
419	helper 1, axiom-clean).**
420	
421	For an affine open `U : T.AffineZariskiSite`, the preimage
422	`q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1` under `q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜)` is itself affine
423	(since `q` is `IsAffineHom` by `QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom`). Its
424	canonical iso with `Spec(Γ(pullback, q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1))` (`IsAffineOpen.isoSpec`)
425	matches `Spec((QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf.val(.op U.1))`
426	**by definitional unfolding** of the pushforward sheaf
427	(`(QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf := (pushforward q.base).obj (pullback _).sheaf`,
428	so its value at `U` is `(pullback _).sheaf.val(.op (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1))` defeq).
429	
430	This is the per-affine-piece iso feeding into the global `pullback_iso`
431	construction. Body is a single `(U.2.preimage q).isoSpec` invocation. -/
432	noncomputable def pullback_iso_affine_piece {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
433	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) (U : T.AffineZariskiSite) :
434	    ((CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
435	        (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toScheme ≅
436	      Scheme.Spec.obj (.op ((QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf.obj.obj (.op U.1))) :=
437	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
438	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
439	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
440	  (U.2.preimage _).isoSpec
441	
442	/-! ### iter-183 Lane D structural decomposition
443	
444	Five helpers feeding `pullback_iso_construction`:
445	
446	* `pullback_iso_affine_piece` (already declared above, axiom-clean) — per-affine
447	  iso `(q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toScheme ≅ Spec((g^*𝒜)(U))`.
448	* `pullback_cocone` — cocone on `(relativeGluingData _).functor` with point
449	  `pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)` whose components are the `fromSpec` maps
450	  of the pulled-back affine opens.
451	* `pullback_cocone_desc_comp_fst` — the cocone descent composed with the
452	  pullback projection equals the relative-gluing-data structure morphism;
453	  the load-bearing identification that makes the open-cover argument work.
454	* `pullback_iso_desc_isIso` — the cocone descent is an iso, via the local
455	  affine-open argument on `pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`.
456	* `pullback_iso_construction` — final assembly: `asIso desc |>.symm`.
457	-/

459	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
460	/-- **Cocone on `(relativeGluingData _).functor` with point
461	`pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`** (iter-183 Lane D helper 3,
462	axiom-clean modulo naturality unfolding).
463	
464	The components are the `IsAffineOpen.fromSpec` maps of the pulled-back affine
465	opens `q⁻¹U.1`. Naturality follows from `IsAffineOpen.map_fromSpec` once
466	the relative-gluing-data functor's `map` action is unfolded as
467	`Spec.map (P.presheaf.map ((q.preimage_mono ...).op))`. The unfolding chase
468	is intricate (deep definitional unfolding of `pushforward` and `rightOp`);
469	deferred to iter-184+ as the only remaining work for axiom-clean closure
470	of the entire base-change iso. -/
471	noncomputable def pullback_cocone {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
472	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
473	    haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
474	        (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
475	      QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
476	    Limits.Cocone (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
477	      (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor :=
478	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
479	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
480	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
481	  { pt := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
482	    ι :=
483	    { app := fun U => (U.2.preimage (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
484	        (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜))).fromSpec
485	      naturality := fun U V x => by
486	        -- After unfolding `relativeGluingData.functor.map` through its
487	        -- `rightOp ⋙ Spec` template and the pushforward sheaf, the goal
488	        -- becomes `Spec.map ((pullback _ _).presheaf.map _) ≫
489	        --   (V.2.preimage q).fromSpec = (U.2.preimage q).fromSpec ≫ 𝟙 _`,
490	        -- which is `IsAffineOpen.map_fromSpec` + `Category.comp_id`.
491	        set q := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
492	          (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
493	        simp only [AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData, Functor.comp_obj,
494	          Functor.comp_map, Functor.rightOp_map, Functor.const_obj_obj,
495	          Functor.const_obj_map]
496	        rw [Category.comp_id]
497	        exact (V.2.preimage q).map_fromSpec (U.2.preimage q) _ } }
