500	helper 4, axiom-clean).
501	
502	The pullback-cocone descent `T.RelativeSpec(g^*𝒜) ⟶ pullback g _`, composed
503	with the pullback projection `q := pullback.fst _ _`, equals the relative-
504	gluing-data's structure morphism `d.toBase`. Proof: `colimit.hom_ext`, then
505	the per-component identity uses `SpecMap_appLE_fromSpec`. -/
506	lemma pullback_cocone_desc_comp_fst {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
507	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
508	    haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
509	        (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
510	      QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
511	    haveI : ((AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
512	        (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor ⋙
513	        Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected :=
514	      Cover.RelativeGluingData.instIsLocallyDirectedI₀CompFunctorForgetOfIsThin _
515	    Limits.colimit.desc _ (pullback_cocone g 𝒜) ≫
516	        CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) =
517	      (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
518	        (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).toBase := by
519	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
520	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
521	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
522	  haveI : ((AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
523	      (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor ⋙
524	      Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected :=
525	    Cover.RelativeGluingData.instIsLocallyDirectedI₀CompFunctorForgetOfIsThin _
526	  set q := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
527	  set d := AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered
528	  refine Limits.colimit.hom_ext fun U => ?_
529	  rw [← Category.assoc, Limits.colimit.ι_desc, d.ι_toBase U]
530	  change (U.2.preimage q).fromSpec ≫ q = _
531	  rw [← U.2.SpecMap_appLE_fromSpec q (U.2.preimage q) le_rfl,
532	    Scheme.Hom.appLE_eq_app, ← IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_inv_ι, ← Category.assoc]
533	  rfl

535	/-- **The descent map is an isomorphism** (iter-183 Lane D helper 5,
536	substantive — axiom-clean modulo upstream `pullback_cocone_desc_comp_fst`
537	and per-piece restrict-iso identification).
538	
539	The canonical descent map `T.RelativeSpec(g^*𝒜) ⟶ pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`
540	defined by the cocone `pullback_cocone g 𝒜` is an isomorphism. Proof:
541	`IsZariskiLocalAtTarget` for the isomorphism property applied to the
542	affine open cover `{q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1}` of `pullback g _`. The preimage of each
543	piece under `desc` matches `(colimit.ι d.functor U).opensRange` by
544	`pullback_cocone_desc_comp_fst` + `Cover.RelativeGluingData.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι`,
545	and the local restriction is then a composition of three explicit isos. -/
546	lemma pullback_iso_desc_isIso {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
547	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
548	    haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
549	        (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
550	      QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
551	    haveI : ((AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
552	        (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor ⋙
553	        Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected :=
554	      Cover.RelativeGluingData.instIsLocallyDirectedI₀CompFunctorForgetOfIsThin _
555	    IsIso (Limits.colimit.desc _ (pullback_cocone g 𝒜)) := by
556	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
557	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
558	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
559	  haveI : ((AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
560	      (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor ⋙
561	      Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected :=
562	    Cover.RelativeGluingData.instIsLocallyDirectedI₀CompFunctorForgetOfIsThin _
563	  set q := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
564	  set d := AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered
565	  set desc := Limits.colimit.desc d.functor (pullback_cocone g 𝒜) with desc_def
566	  refine (IsZariskiLocalAtTarget.iff_of_iSup_eq_top
567	    (P := MorphismProperty.isomorphisms Scheme)
568	    (fun U : T.AffineZariskiSite => q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1) ?_).mpr fun U ↦ ?_
569	  · -- iSup of preimages = preimage of iSup = preimage of ⊤ = ⊤.
570	    rw [← Scheme.Hom.preimage_iSup]
571	    convert Scheme.Hom.preimage_top q using 2
572	    exact iSup_affineOpens_eq_top T
573	  · -- Per-piece: `desc ∣_ q⁻¹U.1` is iso. Following the template at
574	    -- `SmallAffineZariski.isColimitCocone` (file
575	    -- `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:343`), reduce
576	    -- to `IsIso (pullback.snd desc (q⁻¹U.1).ι)` and build an iso
577	    -- `pullback desc (q⁻¹U.1).ι ≅ d.functor.obj U` via range matching using
578	    -- the key fact `hPre`.
579	    have hPre : desc ⁻¹ᵁ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1 = (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).opensRange := by
580	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_preimage, pullback_cocone_desc_comp_fst g 𝒜]
581	      have h := d.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι U
582	      simpa [AffineZariskiSite.directedCover, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι] using h
583	    -- The per-piece factorisation: `desc ∣_ q⁻¹U.1` factors as three isos:
584	    --   (1) `Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre`:
585	    --       `(desc⁻¹q⁻¹U.1).toScheme ≅ (colim.ι U).opensRange.toScheme`
586	    --   (2) `colim.ι U .isoOpensRange.symm`:
587	    --       `(colim.ι U).opensRange.toScheme ≅ d.functor.obj U`
588	    --   (3) `(pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U).symm`:
589	    --       `d.functor.obj U ≅ Spec((g^*𝒜)(U)) ≅ (q⁻¹U.1).toScheme`
590	    --       (the inverse via `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec.inv ≫ ι`).
591	    -- Commute the chain through `(q⁻¹U.1).ι` by canceling the mono ι.
592	    let iso_chain : (desc ⁻¹ᵁ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toScheme ≅ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toScheme :=
593	      Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre ≪≫
594	        (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).isoOpensRange.symm ≪≫
595	        (pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U).symm
596	    suffices h : desc ∣_ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1 = iso_chain.hom by
597	      change IsIso (desc ∣_ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1)
598	      rw [h]; exact iso_chain.isIso_hom
599	    -- Verify equality by post-composing with `(q⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι` (which is mono).
600	    have h_paff_inv : (pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U).inv ≫ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι =
601	        (U.2.preimage q).fromSpec := (U.2.preimage q).isoSpec_inv_ι
602	    have h_cocone : (U.2.preimage q).fromSpec =
603	        Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U ≫ desc := by
604	      change _ = Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U ≫
605	        Limits.colimit.desc d.functor (pullback_cocone g 𝒜)
606	      rw [Limits.colimit.ι_desc]
607	      rfl
608	    have h_post : (desc ∣_ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1)) ≫ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι =
609	        iso_chain.hom ≫ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι := by
610	      refine (morphismRestrict_ι desc (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1)).trans ?_
611	      calc (desc ⁻¹ᵁ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι ≫ desc
612	          = ((Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre).hom ≫
613	              (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).opensRange.ι) ≫ desc := by
614	            rw [Scheme.isoOfEq_hom_ι]
615	        _ = (Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre).hom ≫
616	              (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).opensRange.ι ≫ desc := by
617	            rw [Category.assoc]
618	        _ = (Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre).hom ≫
619	              ((Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).isoOpensRange.inv ≫
620	              Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U) ≫ desc := by
621	            rw [(Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).isoOpensRange_inv_comp]
622	        _ = (Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre).hom ≫
623	              (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).isoOpensRange.inv ≫
624	              (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U ≫ desc) := by
625	            rw [Category.assoc]
626	        _ = (Scheme.isoOfEq _ hPre).hom ≫
627	              (Limits.colimit.ι d.functor U).isoOpensRange.inv ≫
628	              (pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U).inv ≫ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι := by
629	            rw [← h_cocone, ← h_paff_inv]; rfl
630	        _ = iso_chain.hom ≫ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι := by
631	            simp [iso_chain, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, Category.assoc]
632	    exact (cancel_mono (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι).mp h_post

634	/-- **Canonical base-change iso construction** (iter-181 Lane D helper 2,
635	iter-183 Lane D structural close).
636	
637	Builds the iso `pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅ T.RelativeSpec(g^*𝒜)`.
638	
639	**Construction**: the cocone `pullback_cocone g 𝒜` on the relative-gluing-data
640	functor of `g^*𝒜` has point `pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`. The colimit
641	descent map `T.RelativeSpec(g^*𝒜) ⟶ pullback g _` from this cocone is an
642	isomorphism by `pullback_iso_desc_isIso`; the result is the inverse iso. -/
643	noncomputable def pullback_iso_construction {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
644	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
645	    CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅
646	      T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜) := by
647	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
648	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
649	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
650	  haveI : ((AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
651	      (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor ⋙
652	      Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected :=
653	    Cover.RelativeGluingData.instIsLocallyDirectedI₀CompFunctorForgetOfIsThin _
654	  set d := AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered
655	  let desc : T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜) ⟶
656	      CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) :=
657	    Limits.colimit.desc d.functor (pullback_cocone g 𝒜)
658	  haveI : IsIso desc := pullback_iso_desc_isIso g 𝒜
659	  exact (asIso desc).symm
660	
661	/-- **Canonical base-change isomorphism (Nonempty form).** The pullback
662	`pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)` (formed in `Scheme`) is canonically
663	isomorphic to the relative spectrum of the pulled-back qcoh algebra
664	`QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜`. Witnessed by `pullback_iso_construction`. -/
665	theorem pullback_iso {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
666	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
667	    Nonempty (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅
668	              T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜)) :=
669	  ⟨pullback_iso_construction g 𝒜⟩
670	
671	/-- **Base change of the relative spectrum.**
672	
673	For a morphism `g : T → X` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, there
674	exists a quasi-coherent `O_T`-algebra `𝒜' = g^* 𝒜` and a canonical isomorphism
675	of `T`-schemes `T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec_T(g^* 𝒜)`.
676	
677	iter-179 body (Block B): witnessed by the named helpers `QcohAlgebra.pullback`
678	and `pullback_iso`. -/
679	theorem base_change {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X) (_𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
680	    ∃ (𝒜' : T.QcohAlgebra),
681	      Nonempty (pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism _𝒜) ≅
682	                  T.RelativeSpec 𝒜') :=
683	  ⟨QcohAlgebra.pullback g _𝒜, pullback_iso g _𝒜⟩
684	
685	/-! ## §6. Functoriality
686	
687	The construction `𝒜 ↦ Spec_X(𝒜)` extends to a contravariant functor
688	`Spec_X : QcohAlg(X)^op ⥤ Over X`. The iter-173 file-skeleton encodes the
689	object-level functorial assignment as `X.QcohAlgebra → Over X`; the
690	morphism-level action and full `Functor` packaging are iter-174+ work after
691	`QcohAlgebra` is unpacked to a category.
692	
693	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_functorial` (Stacks 01LR
694	definition-relative-spec + lemma-glueing-gives-functor-spec). -/
695	
696	/-- **The relative-spectrum functor (object level).**
697	
698	The object-level functorial action `𝒜 ↦ Over.mk (π_𝒜) : Over X`, packaging
699	the relative spectrum together with its structure morphism over `X`.
700	
701	iter-174+: the body is concrete via `Over.mk (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)`
702	but is left as `sorry` here because `RelativeSpec.structureMorphism` is itself
703	a typed `sorry`; once the structure morphism lands the body collapses to
704	`fun 𝒜 => Over.mk (structureMorphism 𝒜)`. The full categorical functor
705	`X.QcohAlgebra ⥤ Over X` (with `map` action induced by the universal property)
706	becomes expressible once `QcohAlgebra` carries its category structure. -/
707	noncomputable def functor (X : Scheme.{u}) :
708	    X.QcohAlgebra → Over X :=
709	  fun 𝒜 => Over.mk (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
710	
711	end RelativeSpec

11	/-!
12	# The Riemann–Roch formula in genus zero (RR.2)
13	
14	This file is the **RR.2** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
15	headline `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically
16	irreducible genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma in
17	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
18	
19	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain for the genus-`0` ↦ `ℙ¹` classification routes
20	through:
21	
22	- `RR.1` (`RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` / `WeilDivisor.lean`): the Weil
23	  divisor group `Div(C)` and the degree map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ`.
24	- **`RR.2` (this file, `RiemannRoch_RRFormula.tex`)**: the Riemann–Roch
25	  dimension formula `ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1` in genus `0` (with `deg D ≥ 0`),
26	  via the auxiliary Euler-characteristic identity
27	  `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g`.
28	- `RR.3` (`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, future): the invertible sheaf
29	  `𝒪_C(D)`, the linear-equivalence isomorphism `𝒪_C(D) ≅ 𝒪_C(D')` for
30	  `D ∼ D'`, and the `H¹`-vanishing input
31	  `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(D)) = 0` for `deg D ≥ 0` on a genus-`0` curve.
32	- `RR.4` (`RiemannRoch_RationalIsoP1.tex`, future): the "two-section
33	  ⇒ `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` ⇒ `≅ ℙ¹`" classification.
34	
35	## Status (iter-174 Lane F file-skeleton)
36	
37	This file is the **iter-174 Lane F** file-skeleton: each of the four pinned
38	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
39	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/RiemannRoch_RRFormula.tex`). The
40	Euler-characteristic carrier definition is concrete (a one-line subtraction
41	of `H⁰` and `H¹` `Module.finrank`s, mirroring the `genus` definition of
42	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus`); the remaining pins carry `sorry` bodies whose
43	closure is iter-175+ work after the sibling chapters `RR.3`
44	(`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`) and `RR.4` (`RiemannRoch_RationalIsoP1.tex`) land.
45	
46	The 4 pinned declarations are:
47	
48	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.eulerCharacteristic` — Euler characteristic
49	   `χ(𝓕) = dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝓕) − dim_{k̄} H¹(C, 𝓕)` of a `ModuleCat k̄`-valued
50	   sheaf on `C` (the curve specialisation of the alternating sum, since
51	   `H^i = 0` for `i ≥ 2` on a one-dimensional scheme by Grothendieck
52	   vanishing).
53	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.l` — the `ℓ`-invariant
54	   `ℓ(D) = dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(D))` of a Weil divisor `D`.
55	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus`
56	   — the Euler-characteristic identity `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` for
57	   every `D ∈ Div(C)` on a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
58	   `C / k̄` of genus `g = g(C)`.
59	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.l_eq_degree_plus_one_of_genus_zero`
60	   — the Riemann–Roch formula in genus `0`: `ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1` for any
61	   Weil divisor `D ∈ Div(C)` with `deg D ≥ 0` on a smooth proper
62	   geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄` with `g(C) = 0` (threading
63	   the `H¹`-vanishing of `𝒪_C(D)` explicitly as a named premise until
64	   `RR.3` lands).
65	
66	## Note on `𝒪_C(D)` (the invertible sheaf of a divisor)
67	
68	The chapter's proof of `eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus`
69	and the statement of `l_eq_degree_plus_one_of_genus_zero` both reference
70	the line bundle `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor `D`. Mathlib `b80f227` ships no
71	`Scheme.lineBundleOfDivisor` (the closest is `WeierstrassCurve.lineBundle`
72	in the elliptic-curve formalisation), and the project-side construction of
73	`𝒪_C(D)` is queued for `RR.3` (`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`). To keep the type
74	signatures of pins 2–4 substantive in the iter-174 skeleton, we expose a
75	**typed-`sorry` placeholder**
76	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf` that pairs each divisor with
77	the `ModuleCat k̄`-valued sheaf carrier `𝒪_C(D)` is intended to occupy. The
78	iter-175+ closure of `RR.3` replaces this placeholder's body with the
79	honest invertible-sheaf construction; the present pins consume it only
80	through its `H⁰` and `H¹` cohomology, so the consumer signatures are
81	substantive in the type sense (each asserts an arithmetic identity on the
82	finiteness-of-`H^*` outputs).
83	
84	## References
85	
86	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/RiemannRoch_RRFormula.tex` (Hartshorne
87	IV.1 verbatim quotes; 4 pins).
88	Source: Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, IV §1 (pp. 294–297), Theorem 1.3
89	(Riemann–Roch) and Example 1.3.5 (genus-`0` specialisation). Stacks Project

101	/-! ## §1. The Euler characteristic of a coherent sheaf on a curve -/
102	
103	/-- **Euler characteristic of a `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf on a smooth proper
104	curve `C / k̄`.**
105	
106	On a curve (one-dimensional scheme), Grothendieck vanishing
107	(Hartshorne III.2.7) gives `H^i(C, 𝓕) = 0` for `i ≥ 2`, so the classical
108	alternating sum
109	`χ(𝓕) = Σ_{i ≥ 0} (-1)^i dim_{k̄} H^i(C, 𝓕)` collapses to the two-term
110	expression
111	`χ(𝓕) = dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝓕) − dim_{k̄} H¹(C, 𝓕)`.
112	
113	This is the definition we ship. Coherence of `𝓕` on the proper `k̄`-scheme
114	`C` guarantees that both `H⁰` and `H¹` are finite-dimensional `k̄`-vector
115	spaces (Serre's coherent-cohomology finiteness theorem, the same
116	finiteness backing `AlgebraicGeometry.genus`), so the two `Module.finrank`s
117	are honest natural numbers and the difference is a well-defined integer.
118	
119	The `Module k̄`-valued cohomology pipeline is the project's
120	`Scheme.HModule` (iter-009), the same wrapper used by
121	`AlgebraicGeometry.genus`.
122	
123	Blueprint reference: `def:eulerChar_curve`
124	(Hartshorne IV.1 p. 295, displayed inside the proof of Theorem 1.3). -/
125	noncomputable def Scheme.eulerCharacteristic
126	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
127	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
128	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
129	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
130	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
131	      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) : ℤ :=
132	  (Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar F 0) : ℤ)
133	    - (Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar F 1) : ℤ)
134	
135	/-! ## §2. The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor (sibling chapter)
136	
137	The honest construction of `𝒪_C(D)` lives in the sibling chapter `RR.3`
138	(`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, `AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/OcOfD.lean`),
139	where the locally-principal ideal sheaves of closed points are glued
140	into an invertible `𝒪_C`-module. **iter-183 Lane K** opened that file
141	with the typed-`sorry` pin `Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf` plus the three
142	immediate corollaries `sheafOf_zero`, `sheafOf_singlePoint`, and
143	`sheafOf_ses_single_add`. **iter-183 Lane H** (this file) retires the
144	former local `sheafOf` typed-`sorry` placeholder (previously at L168 in
145	the iter-174 skeleton) by importing `OcOfD.lean`; downstream pins (the
146	`ℓ`-invariant, the χ-identity, the genus-`0` Riemann–Roch formula) now
147	reference the chapter's canonical `sheafOf` directly. -/
148	
149	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
150	
151	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
152	  {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
153	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
154	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
155	
156	/-! ## §3. The `ℓ`-invariant of a Weil divisor -/
157	
158	/-- **The `ℓ`-invariant of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth proper curve
159	`C / k̄`.**
160	
161	By definition,
162	`ℓ(D) := dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(D)) ∈ ℕ`,
163	the `k̄`-dimension of the space of global sections of the invertible sheaf
164	`𝒪_C(D)` associated to `D`. Finiteness of `ℓ(D)` is a consequence of the
165	coherent-cohomology finiteness theorem on a proper `k̄`-scheme
166	(Hartshorne II.5.19 / III.5.2), the same input that backs the finiteness
167	of `genus C`.
168	
169	The classical *complete linear system* `|D|` has `k̄`-projective dimension
170	`ℓ(D) − 1`; the chapter never uses this projective interpretation —
171	`ℓ(D)` is the only quantity consumed by the Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain.
172	
173	Blueprint reference: `def:l_invariant` (Hartshorne IV.1 p. 295,
174	"We denote `dim_k H⁰(X, 𝒪(D))` by `l(D)`"). -/
175	noncomputable def l (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) : ℕ :=
176	  Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar (sheafOf (C := C) D) 0)
177	
178	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
179	
180	/-! ## §4. The χ-identity: `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g`
181	
182	The bridge from the structural Euler characteristic to the arithmetic
183	degree of a divisor. Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3 reduces Riemann–Roch to
184	this identity; the proof is the inductive `D ↔ D + [P]` step (additivity
185	of χ on the closed-point short exact sequence, with base case `D = 0`
186	giving `χ(𝒪_C) = 1 − g` from `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1` and the definition of
187	the genus). The closure of the body is iter-175+ work after the
188	`Euler-characteristic additivity on a short exact sequence`-style
189	project-side helper is supplied and the `RR.3` sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` has a
190	body.
191	
192	**Iter-181 Lane H factoring.** Both Hartshorne IV.1.3 inputs (the base
193	case `χ(𝒪_C) = 1 − g` and the inductive step `χ(𝒪_C(D + Y)) = χ(𝒪_C(D))
194	+ n`) intrinsically reference the body of `Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf`,
195	which is still a typed-`sorry` placeholder waiting on the sibling
196	chapter `RR.3` (`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`). We therefore factor the proof
197	into two named substantive helper lemmas (3-tier disclosure: **honest
198	named-sorry helpers** — each helper has a substantive type encoding a
199	nontrivial mathematical claim about the `sheafOf` line bundle whose
200	closure is downstream of the `RR.3` body):
201	
202	1. `eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_zero` — base case `χ(sheafOf 0) = 1 − g`,
203	   which on closure of `RR.3` reduces to `sheafOf 0 = toModuleKSheaf C`
204	   plus the Hartshorne I.3.4 input `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1` plus the
205	   definition of the genus.
206	2. `eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add` — inductive step
207	   `χ(sheafOf (Finsupp.single Y n + D)) = χ(sheafOf D) + n` for any
208	   `Y : C.left.PrimeDivisor` and `n : ℤ`. On closure of `RR.3` this
209	   reduces to the Hartshorne IV.1.3 SES additivity argument iterated
210	   `|n|` times (with sign for `n < 0`).
211	
212	The main theorem then closes by induction on the `Finsupp`-structure of
213	`D : C.left.WeilDivisor` (via `Finsupp.induction`), using the two
214	helpers and the additivity of `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree`. -/
215	
216	/-- **Hartshorne I.3.4 bridge** (Lane H helper A — iter-183).
217	On a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄`, the global
218	sections of the structure sheaf form a one-dimensional `k̄`-vector space:
219	`dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1`.
220	
221	This is the Hartshorne~I.3.4 statement "for any projective variety `X`
222	over an algebraically closed field `k`, `H⁰(X, 𝒪_X) ≅ k`" specialised to
223	the project's `ModuleCat k̄`-flavoured cohomology pipeline
224	(`Scheme.HModule kbar (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 0`). The closure lives
225	in the project's `Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK` chapter (the H⁰
226	bridge from the constant-sheaf adjunction) and is gated on the
227	remaining cohomology-API work in that file.
228	
229	**iter-183 Lane H status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. Body iter-184+
230	via the `Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK` H⁰-bridge. -/
231	private theorem Scheme.finrank_H0_toModuleKSheaf_eq_one
232	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
233	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
234	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
235	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left] :
236	    Module.finrank kbar
237	        (Scheme.HModule kbar (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 0) = 1 := by
238	  -- Step 1: H⁰-bridge LinearEquiv chain (existing project infrastructure).
239	  -- `HModule kbar (toModuleKSheaf C) 0` is identified, step by step, with
240	  -- the underlying global-sections ring `C.left.presheaf.obj (op ⊤)` via:
241	  --   `HModule_zero_linearEquiv`         -- Ext₀ ≃ Hom from constant sheaf;
242	  --   `constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv` -- constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction;
243	  --   `homFromOne_linearEquiv`            -- Hom-from-`k` evaluation at `1`;
244	  --   `SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top`     -- `Sheaf.Γ` reads the top section.
245	  let LE1 :=
246	    Scheme.HModule_zero_linearEquiv kbar (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)
247	  let LE2 :=
248	    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv kbar
249	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)
250	      (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)
251	  let LE3 :=
252	    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.homFromOne_linearEquiv kbar
253	      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
254	        (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
255	  let LE4 :=
256	    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top kbar
257	      (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)
258	  let LE := ((LE1.trans LE2).trans LE3).trans LE4
259	  rw [LE.finrank_eq]
260	  -- Step 2: `(toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj (op ⊤)` is definitionally
261	  -- `ModuleCat.of kbar (C.left.presheaf.obj (op ⊤))` (per `toModuleKPresheaf_obj`),
262	  -- so we transport to the global-sections ring and apply
263	  -- `Module.finrank_of_bijective_algebraMap` + `IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral`.
264	  -- Convert the ModuleCat coercion to the underlying global sections ring.
265	  change Module.finrank kbar
266	      (C.left.presheaf.obj
267	        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))) = 1
268	  -- Module-finiteness via the iter-044 Stein input.
269	  haveI hFin : Module.Finite kbar
270	      (C.left.presheaf.obj
271	        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))) :=
272	    AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper kbar C
273	  -- Integrality of `C.left` propagates to the global sections being a domain
274	  -- (`IsIntegral.component_integral`), with the nonempty side-condition
275	  -- discharged from `IsIntegral.nonempty`.
276	  haveI hNECurve : Nonempty C.left.toTopCat :=
277	    AlgebraicGeometry.IsIntegral.nonempty
278	  -- `component_integral` requires `[Nonempty ↥↑U]` for the open `U = ⊤`; supply
279	  -- it inline via the curve nonemptiness, sidestepping the `↥↑` / `↑↑` coercion
280	  -- spelling mismatch that defeats `haveI`-based instance registration.
281	  haveI hDom : IsDomain
282	      (C.left.presheaf.obj
283	        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))) :=
284	    @AlgebraicGeometry.IsIntegral.component_integral
285	      C.left _ (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)
286	      ⟨⟨hNECurve.some, Set.mem_univ _⟩⟩
287	  -- `Module.Finite kbar A` → `Algebra.IsIntegral kbar A` (auto-instance).
288	  -- Then `IsAlgClosed` + integrality + domain ⇒ algebraMap is bijective ⇒ finrank = 1.
289	  exact Module.finrank_of_bijective_algebraMap
290	    (IsAlgClosed.algebraMap_bijective_of_isIntegral (k := kbar)
291	      (K := C.left.presheaf.obj
292	        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat))))
293	
294	/-- **χ-additivity at a closed-point skyscraper extension** (Lane H sub-helper —
295	iter-185).
296	
297	Substantive packaging of the three Hartshorne IV.1.3 inputs into a single
298	named Tier-3 typed sorry, scoped to the precise SES shape consumed by
299	`Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_succ`. Given:
300	
301	* a short exact sequence `0 → F → G → H → 0` of `ModuleCat kbar`-valued sheaves
302	  on the curve `C` (`hSE : S.ShortExact`),
303	* an isomorphism witness identifying `S.X₃` with the closed-point skyscraper
304	  `skyscraperSheaf P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar)` (`h13`),
305	
306	we conclude `χ(S.X₂) = χ(S.X₁) + 1`.
307	
308	The body bundles:
309	
310	1. χ-additivity on a short-exact sequence of coherent sheaves: from the long
311	   exact sequence of cohomology
312	   `H⁰(F) → H⁰(G) → H⁰(H) → H¹(F) → H¹(G) → H¹(H) → H²(F) → ...`
313	   together with Grothendieck vanishing on a curve (`H^i = 0` for `i ≥ 2`),
314	   apply the finite-rank alternating-sum identity to get
315	   `χ(G) = χ(F) + χ(H)`.
316	2. Iso-invariance of `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic`: `S.X₃ ≅ k(P)` lifts to
317	   `Module.finrank` equalities on each `HModule` carrier, so χ transports
318	   across the iso witness.
319	3. χ of the closed-point skyscraper: `H⁰(C, k(P)) ≅ kbar` (the stalk at `P`)
320	   gives finrank 1, and `H¹(C, k(P)) = 0` (skyscraper cohomology vanishes
321	   above degree 0), so `χ(k(P)) = 1`.
322	
323	**iter-185 Lane H status** — Tier-3 honest typed sorry. The body assembly
324	depends on (a) the project-side LES carrier for `ModuleCat kbar`-valued
325	sheaves (queued downstream of `Cohomology/MayerVietorisCore.lean`), and
326	(b) the project-side skyscraper-cohomology bridge (currently unsupplied at
327	Mathlib `b80f227` for the `ModuleCat`-flavoured constant-sheaf-Γ setup; the
328	existing `instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf` template would lift). -/
329	private theorem Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_of_shortExact_skyscraper
330	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
331	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
332	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
333	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
334	    (S : CategoryTheory.ShortComplex
335	      (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
336	        (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)))
337	    (_hSE : S.ShortExact)
338	    (P : C.left.PrimeDivisor)
339	    [∀ (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left), Decidable (P.point ∈ U)]
340	    (_h13 : Nonempty (S.X₃ ≅ skyscraperSheaf (C := ModuleCat.{u} kbar)
341	      P.point (ModuleCat.of kbar kbar))) :
342	    Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C S.X₂
343	      = Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C S.X₁ + 1 := by
344	  sorry
345	
346	private theorem Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_succ
347	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
348	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
349	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
350	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
351	    (D : C.left.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) (Y : C.left.PrimeDivisor) :
352	    Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
353	        (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) (Finsupp.single Y 1 + D))
354	      = Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
355	          (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D) + 1 := by
356	  classical
357	  -- Unpack the Hartshorne IV.1.3 closed-point SES from `RR.3`
358	  -- (Lane K typed-sorry `OcOfD.sheafOf_ses_single_add`):
359	  --   0 → 𝒪_C(D) → 𝒪_C(single Y 1 + D) → k(Y) → 0
360	  obtain ⟨S, hSE, hX1, hX2, h13⟩ :=
361	    Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf_ses_single_add (C := C) D Y
362	  -- Apply the packaged χ-additivity-with-skyscraper helper to convert the SES
363	  -- into an arithmetic identity at the χ level.
364	  have hχ :=
365	    Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_of_shortExact_skyscraper C S hSE Y h13
366	  -- Rewrite the X₁/X₂ identifications back to `sheafOf` to obtain the goal.
367	  rw [hX1, hX2] at hχ
368	  exact hχ
369	
370	/-- **Base case of the χ-identity** (iter-183 Lane H): on a smooth
371	proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄`, the Euler characteristic
372	of the structure-sheaf-side line bundle `sheafOf 0` is `1 − g(C)`.
373	
374	The proof rewrites `sheafOf 0 = toModuleKSheaf C` (the chapter
375	`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex` `sheafOf_zero` lemma), unfolds the
376	two-term `eulerCharacteristic`, and combines `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1`
377	(`Scheme.finrank_H0_toModuleKSheaf_eq_one`) with the definitional
378	unfold `genus C = dim H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`.
379	
380	**iter-183 Lane H status** — body assembled axiom-clean modulo
381	`OcOfD.sheafOf_zero` (Lane K typed sorry) and the H⁰ bridge typed
382	sorry; both gated on iter-184+ infrastructure. -/
383	private theorem Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_zero
384	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
385	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
386	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
387	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left] :
388	    Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
389	        (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) (0 : C.left.WeilDivisor))
390	      = 1 - (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : ℤ) := by
391	  rw [Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf_zero (C := C)]
392	  unfold Scheme.eulerCharacteristic
393	  rw [Scheme.finrank_H0_toModuleKSheaf_eq_one C]
394	  simp [AlgebraicGeometry.genus]
395	
396	/-- **Inductive step of the χ-identity** (iter-183 Lane H): on a smooth
397	proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄`, the Euler characteristic
398	of `sheafOf` transports across the elementary modification
399	`D ↦ Finsupp.single Y n + D` with arithmetic increment `n`. The argument
400	`D` carries the underlying `Finsupp` type rather than `Scheme.WeilDivisor`
401	so that the `Finsupp.single Y n + D` term elaborates cleanly; the result
402	is consumed via the definitional equality `WeilDivisor = (PrimeDivisor →₀
403	ℤ)`.
404	
405	The body inducts on `n : ℤ` via `Int.induction_on`:
406	- `n = 0`: `Finsupp.single Y 0 = 0`, so `single Y 0 + D = D`.
407	- `n = k + 1` (positive direction): rewrite
408	  `single Y (k+1) + D = single Y 1 + (single Y k + D)`
409	  via `Finsupp.single_add`, apply
410	  `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_succ`, then the inductive
411	  hypothesis.
412	- `n = -(k+1)` (negative direction): apply
413	  `Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_succ` to
414	  `D' := single Y (-(k+1)) + D` and simplify; the LHS `single Y 1 + D'`
415	  rewrites to `single Y (-k) + D` via `Finsupp.single_add` and
416	  arithmetic, giving the identity backwards from the inductive
417	  hypothesis at `-k`.
418	
419	**iter-183 Lane H status** — body sorry-free assembly modulo the
420	`_succ` typed-sorry helper. Once `_succ` closes iter-184+, this lemma
421	upgrades to Tier-1. -/
422	private theorem Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add
423	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
424	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
425	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
426	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
427	    (D : C.left.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ) (Y : C.left.PrimeDivisor) (n : ℤ) :
428	    Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
429	        (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) (Finsupp.single Y n + D))
430	      = Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
431	          (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D) + n := by
432	  refine Int.induction_on (motive := fun m : ℤ =>
433	      Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
434	          (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) (Finsupp.single Y m + D))
435	        = Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
436	            (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D) + m) n ?_ ?_ ?_
437	  · -- n = 0
438	    simp
439	  · -- positive step: motive (↑k) → motive (↑k + 1)
440	    intro k ih
441	    have hsplit :
442	        (Finsupp.single Y ((k : ℤ) + 1) + D : C.left.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)
443	          = Finsupp.single Y 1 + (Finsupp.single Y (k : ℤ) + D) := by
444	      rw [show ((k : ℤ) + 1) = (1 + (k : ℤ)) from by ring,
445	        Finsupp.single_add, add_assoc]
446	    have hstep := Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_succ
447	      C (Finsupp.single Y (k : ℤ) + D) Y
448	    have h1 : Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
449	        (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C)
450	          (Finsupp.single Y ((k : ℤ) + 1) + D))
451	        = Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
452	            (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C)
453	              (Finsupp.single Y 1 + (Finsupp.single Y (k : ℤ) + D))) :=
454	      congrArg (fun e => Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
455	        (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) e)) hsplit
456	    linarith [h1, hstep, ih]
457	  · -- negative step: motive (-↑k) → motive (-↑k - 1)
458	    intro k ih
459	    have hsplit :
460	        (Finsupp.single Y (-(k : ℤ)) + D : C.left.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)
461	          = Finsupp.single Y 1 +
462	              (Finsupp.single Y (-(k : ℤ) - 1) + D) := by
463	      rw [← add_assoc, ← Finsupp.single_add]
464	      congr 2
465	      ring
466	    have hpred := Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_succ
467	      C (Finsupp.single Y (-(k : ℤ) - 1) + D) Y
468	    -- hpred: χ(sheafOf (single Y 1 + (single Y (-k-1) + D))) = χ(sheafOf (single Y (-k-1) + D)) + 1
469	    -- Combine via hsplit and congr 1 to bridge dot-notation vs full-name display:
470	    have key : Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
471	        (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) (Finsupp.single Y (-(k : ℤ)) + D))
472	        = Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
473	            (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C)
474	              (Finsupp.single Y 1 + (Finsupp.single Y (-(k : ℤ) - 1) + D))) :=
475	      congrArg (fun e => Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C
476	        (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) e)) hsplit
477	    -- ih: χ(sheafOf (single Y (-k) + D)) = χ(sheafOf D) + (-k)
478	    -- key: χ(sheafOf (single Y (-k) + D)) = χ(sheafOf (single Y 1 + (single Y (-k - 1) + D)))
479	    -- hpred: χ(sheafOf (s Y 1 + (s Y (-k-1) + D))) = χ(sheafOf (s Y (-k-1) + D)) + 1
480	    -- Goal: χ(sheafOf (single Y (-k - 1) + D)) = χ(sheafOf D) + (-k - 1)
481	    linarith [key, hpred, ih]
482	
483	/-- **Euler-characteristic identity for `𝒪_C(D)` on a smooth proper curve
484	of genus `g`.**
485	
486	For every Weil divisor `D ∈ Div(C)`,
487	`χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g(C)`.
488	
489	The proof is Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3's reduction: induction on the
490	free-abelian-group structure of `Div(C)` on closed points, base case `D =
491	0` giving `χ(𝒪_C) = 1 − g` (the `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1` is the Hartshorne
492	I.3.4 input via the project's `H⁰`-bridge), and inductive step via
493	additivity of χ on the closed-point short exact sequence
494	`0 → 𝒪_C(D) → 𝒪_C(D + [P]) → k(P) → 0`.
495	
496	Blueprint reference: `thm:euler_char_eq_deg_plus_one_minus_genus`
497	(Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3, p. 295).
498	
499	**Iter-181 Lane H status** — the main theorem is closed by induction on
500	the `Finsupp` structure of `D`, consuming the two `sheafOf`-side helper
501	sorries `eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_zero` (base case) and
502	`eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add` (inductive step). The body
503	itself is honest; its `sorryAx`-transitivity is the inevitable
504	consequence of `sheafOf` still being a typed sorry waiting on `RR.3`. -/
505	theorem Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus
506	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
507	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
508	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
509	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
510	    (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) :
511	    Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D)
512	      = (Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D) + 1 - (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : ℤ) := by
513	  -- Unfold `WeilDivisor` to expose the underlying `Finsupp` structure so
514	  -- `Finsupp.induction` applies directly.
515	  unfold Scheme.WeilDivisor at D
516	  induction D using Finsupp.induction with
517	  | zero =>
518	    -- D = 0: χ(sheafOf 0) = 1 - g by helper 1, and degree 0 = 0.
519	    exact (Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_zero C).trans
520	      (by simp [Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree])
521	  | single_add Y n D' _hY _hn hD' =>
522	    -- D = single Y n + D': use helper 2 then the inductive hypothesis.
523	    rw [Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_sheafOf_single_add, hD']
524	    -- Goal: (degree D' + 1 - g) + n = degree (single Y n + D') + 1 - g.
525	    have hdeg : Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree
526	        ((Finsupp.single Y n + D' : C.left.WeilDivisor))
527	        = n + Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D' := by
528	      change ((Finsupp.single Y n + D' : C.left.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ)).sum
529	          (fun _ z => z) = _
530	      classical
531	      rw [Finsupp.sum_add_index (fun _ _ => rfl) (fun _ _ _ _ => rfl)]
532	      simp [Finsupp.sum_single_index]
533	      rfl
534	    linarith
535	
536	/-! ## §5. The Riemann–Roch formula in genus zero -/
537	
538	/-- **Riemann–Roch in genus zero (Hartshorne IV.1 Example 1.3.5).**
539	
540	Let `C` be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over the
541	algebraically closed field `k̄` with `g(C) = 0`, and let `D ∈ Div(C)` with
542	`deg D ≥ 0`. Then
543	`ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1`.
544	
545	The proof specialises the χ-identity
546	`eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus` to `g = 0`,
547	unfolds `χ` via `def:eulerChar_curve`, and absorbs the `H¹`-vanishing
548	hypothesis (named premise `_hH1`: `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(D)) = 0`, which is the
549	`H¹`-vanishing of a non-negative-degree invertible sheaf on a
550	genus-`0` curve, to be discharged by `RR.3` once `𝒪_C(D)` has a body and
551	the cohomology of `𝒪_{ℙ¹}(d)` is computed).
552	
553	Blueprint reference: `thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero` (Hartshorne IV.1
554	Example 1.3.5, p. 297). -/
555	theorem Scheme.WeilDivisor.l_eq_degree_plus_one_of_genus_zero
556	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
557	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
558	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
559	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
560	    (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) (_hg : AlgebraicGeometry.genus C = 0)
561	    (_hdeg : (0 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D)
562	    (_hH1 : Module.finrank kbar
563	      (Scheme.HModule kbar (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D) 1) = 0) :
564	    (Scheme.WeilDivisor.l (C := C) D : ℤ)
565	      = Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D + 1 := by
566	  have h := Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus C D
567	  simp only [Scheme.eulerCharacteristic, _hg, _hH1,
568	    Nat.cast_zero, sub_zero] at h
569	  exact h
570	
571	end AlgebraicGeometry
572
Spawning parallel explores to audit the remaining files.

content:
1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Christian Merten. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Christian Merten
5	-/
6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# Coheight ↔ Krull dimension of stalk bridge
10	
11	This file packages the project-side bridge between the **topological coheight**
12	of a scheme point and the **ring-theoretic Krull dimension** of its stalk. It
13	is the iter-183 Lane M deliverable per
14	`analogies/stacks-00tt-coheight.md` Decision 2, with blueprint chapter
15	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CoheightBridge.tex`.
16	
17	The four declarations are:
18	
19	1. `Order.coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding` — for `X` a topological space and
20	   `U ⊆ X` open, `coheight z (in X) = coheight ⟨z, hz⟩ (in U)` (both wrt the
21	   specialisation preorder, pinned explicitly via `@`).
22	2. `Order.coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum` — for `R` a `CommRingCat` and
23	   `p : Spec R`, `coheight p (in Spec R) = height ⟨p.asIdeal, p.isPrime⟩
24	   (in PrimeSpectrum R)`.
25	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` — the bridge:
26	   `ringKrullDim (X.presheaf.stalk z) = coheight z` for any scheme point.
27	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one` — codim-1
28	   wrapper: `coheight z = 1 → Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk z)`.
29	
30	The first three lemmas are natural Mathlib upstream candidates; the fourth is
31	the project-facing consumer wrapper used in
32	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (`hreg_dim` Krull-dim conjunct) and
33	`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean` (`Scheme.RationalMap.order` instance argument).
34	-/
35	
36	open Order TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory
37	
38	universe u
39	
40	namespace Order
41	
42	/-- **Open subsets preserve `Order.coheight`** for points of a topological
43	space carrying the specialisation preorder. Both `Order.coheight` instances
44	are pinned to `specializationPreorder` explicitly (via `@`) so the statement
45	is well-defined even when `X` carries no other `Preorder` instance.
46	
47	Forward bound: `Subtype.val` is continuous, hence spec-monotone, and is
48	injective; therefore strictly monotone. Reverse bound: every generisation of a
49	point in an open set lies in that open (`Specializes.mem_open`), so a chain
50	starting at `z` in `X` lifts to a chain in `↥U` of the same length. -/
51	lemma coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding
52	    {X : Type*} [TopologicalSpace X] {U : Set X} (hU : IsOpen U)
53	    (z : X) (hz : z ∈ U) :
54	    @Order.coheight X (specializationPreorder X) z
55	      = @Order.coheight U (specializationPreorder U) ⟨z, hz⟩ := by
56	  letI : Preorder X := specializationPreorder X
57	  letI : Preorder ↥U := specializationPreorder ↥U
58	  -- `Subtype.val : ↥U → X` is monotone for the spec preorder (since it is
59	  -- continuous), and is injective; hence strictly monotone.
60	  have hmono : Monotone (Subtype.val : ↥U → X) :=
61	    continuous_subtype_val.specialization_monotone
62	  have hstrict : StrictMono (Subtype.val : ↥U → X) := by
63	    intro a b hab
64	    refine ⟨hmono hab.le, fun h => ?_⟩
65	    apply hab.not_ge
66	    change a ⤳ b
67	    exact (subtype_specializes_iff a b).mpr h
68	  refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
69	  · -- Reverse direction: coheight z (in X) ≤ coheight ⟨z, hz⟩ (in ↥U).
70	    refine Order.coheight_le_iff'.mpr ?_
71	    intro p hphead
72	    -- Every p i lies in U: p.head ≤ p i in X means p i ⤳ z; combined with
73	    -- `z ∈ U` open, gives p i ∈ U via `Specializes.mem_open`.
74	    have hmem : ∀ i, p i ∈ U := by
75	      intro i
76	      have hle : z ≤ p i := by
77	        have := p.head_le i
78	        rwa [hphead] at this
79	      exact Specializes.mem_open (show p i ⤳ z from hle) hU hz
80	    -- Construct the LTSeries in ↥U.
81	    let q : LTSeries ↥U :=
82	      { length := p.length
83	        toFun := fun i => ⟨p i, hmem i⟩
84	        step := by
85	          intro i
86	          have hlt : p i.castSucc < p i.succ := p.step i
87	          have hspec : (p i.succ) ⤳ (p i.castSucc) := hlt.le
88	          have hsub : (⟨p i.succ, hmem _⟩ : ↥U) ⤳ ⟨p i.castSucc, hmem _⟩ :=
89	            (subtype_specializes_iff _ _).mpr hspec
90	          refine ⟨hsub, fun hbad => ?_⟩
91	          -- hbad : (⟨p i.castSucc, _⟩) ⤳ ⟨p i.succ, _⟩ in ↥U
92	          apply hlt.not_ge
93	          exact (subtype_specializes_iff _ _).mp hbad }
94	    have hqhead : q.head = ⟨z, hz⟩ := by
95	      apply Subtype.ext
96	      change p 0 = z
97	      exact hphead
98	    have hlen : q.length = p.length := rfl
99	    have hbound :=
100	      Order.length_le_coheight (x := (⟨z, hz⟩ : ↥U)) (p := q) (by rw [hqhead])
101	    simpa [hlen] using hbound
102	  · -- Forward direction: coheight ⟨z, hz⟩ (in ↥U) ≤ coheight z (in X).
103	    have h :=
104	      Order.coheight_le_coheight_apply_of_strictMono
105	        (Subtype.val : ↥U → X) hstrict ⟨z, hz⟩
106	    simpa using h
107	
108	end Order
109	
110	namespace Order
111	
112	/-- **The specialisation preorder on `Spec R` is the dual of the inclusion
113	preorder on `PrimeSpectrum R`.** Coheight in `Spec R` equals height in
114	`PrimeSpectrum R`. -/
115	lemma coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum
116	    {R : CommRingCat} (p : Spec R) :
117	    Order.coheight (α := Spec R) p
118	      = Order.height (α := PrimeSpectrum R) ⟨p.asIdeal, p.isPrime⟩ := by
119	  -- The order iso `Spec R ≃o (PrimeSpectrum R)ᵒᵈ` built from `spec_le_iff`.
120	  let e : Spec R ≃o (PrimeSpectrum R)ᵒᵈ :=
121	    { toFun := fun q => OrderDual.toDual ⟨q.asIdeal, q.isPrime⟩
122	      invFun := fun q => (OrderDual.ofDual q : PrimeSpectrum R)
123	      left_inv := fun _ => rfl
124	      right_inv := fun _ => rfl
125	      map_rel_iff' := by
126	        intro a b
127	        -- After unfolding the dual order, this becomes
128	        -- `b.asIdeal ≤ a.asIdeal ↔ a ≤ b in Spec R`, i.e. `spec_le_iff`.
129	        exact (AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.spec_le_iff R a b).symm }
130	  have h1 :
131	      Order.coheight (α := (PrimeSpectrum R)ᵒᵈ) (e p)
132	        = Order.coheight (α := Spec R) p :=
133	    Order.coheight_orderIso e p
134	  rw [← h1]
135	  -- `e p = OrderDual.toDual ⟨p.asIdeal, p.isPrime⟩`; reduce via
136	  -- `coheight (toDual x) = height x`.
137	  rfl
138	
139	end Order
140	
141	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
142	
143	namespace Scheme
144	
145	/-- **Coheight ↔ Krull dimension bridge for scheme points.** For any scheme
146	`X` and any point `z : X`, the Krull dimension of the stalk at `z` equals the
147	coheight of `z` in the underlying topological space (with the specialisation
148	preorder). -/
149	theorem ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight
150	    (X : Scheme.{u}) (z : X) :
151	    ringKrullDim (X.presheaf.stalk z) = Order.coheight z := by
152	  -- Step 1: pick an affine open U ∋ z.
153	  obtain ⟨U, hU, hzU, _⟩ :=
154	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (x := z) (U := ⊤)
155	      (by trivial)
156	  -- Step 2: name the prime corresponding to z inside U.
157	  set p : PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U) := hU.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzU⟩ with hp
158	  -- Bind the section-to-stalk algebra explicitly so the localisation theorem
159	  -- sees the expected `Algebra` instance.
160	  letI : Algebra Γ(X, U) (X.presheaf.stalk z) :=
161	    TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.presheaf ⟨z, hzU⟩
162	  -- Step 3: stalk is the localisation of Γ(X,U) at p.
163	  haveI hloc : IsLocalization.AtPrime (X.presheaf.stalk z) p.asIdeal :=
164	    hU.isLocalization_stalk ⟨z, hzU⟩
165	  -- Step 4: ringKrullDim stalk = p.asIdeal.height (= primeHeight p).
166	  have h4 :
167	      ringKrullDim (X.presheaf.stalk z)
168	        = (Order.height (α := PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U)) p : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
169	    rw [IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height
170	          (R := Γ(X, U)) p.asIdeal (X.presheaf.stalk z),
171	        Ideal.height_eq_primeHeight]
172	    rfl
173	  -- Step 5: relate height(p) to coheight(z) via Decls 1 + 2 and the
174	  -- homeomorphism `Spec Γ(X,U) ≃ U.toScheme` from `hU.isoSpec`.
175	  -- (5a) Decl 1: coheight z in X = coheight ⟨z, hzU⟩ in U.1 subspace.
176	  have h1' :
177	      @Order.coheight X (specializationPreorder X) z
178	        = @Order.coheight U.1 (specializationPreorder U.1) ⟨z, hzU⟩ :=
179	    Order.coheight_eq_of_isOpenEmbedding (X := X) (U := U.1) U.isOpen z hzU
180	  -- For a Scheme, the standard `Preorder` instance is definitionally
181	  -- `specializationPreorder`. Cast h1' to use the Scheme's preorder on the
182	  -- X side and U.toScheme's preorder on the U side (both defeq).
183	  have h1 :
184	      Order.coheight (α := X) z
185	        = Order.coheight (α := U.toScheme) ⟨z, hzU⟩ := h1'
186	  -- (5b) The scheme iso `hU.isoSpec : U.toScheme ≅ Spec Γ(X,U)` is a Scheme
187	  -- iso; the underlying carrier-level map is a homeomorphism, giving an
188	  -- order iso of spec preorders.
189	  let hHomeo : U.toScheme ≃ₜ Spec Γ(X, U) :=
190	    TopCat.homeoOfIso (Scheme.forgetToTop.mapIso hU.isoSpec)
191	  let eOrder : U.toScheme ≃o Spec Γ(X, U) :=
192	    { toEquiv := hHomeo.toEquiv
193	      map_rel_iff' := by
194	        intro a b
195	        constructor
196	        · intro h
197	          have hsp : hHomeo b ⤳ hHomeo a := h
198	          have hsp' := hsp.map hHomeo.symm.continuous
199	          rw [hHomeo.symm_apply_apply, hHomeo.symm_apply_apply] at hsp'
200	          exact (hsp' : a ≤ b)
201	        · intro h
202	          have hsp : b ⤳ a := h
203	          exact (hsp.map hHomeo.continuous : hHomeo a ≤ hHomeo b) }
204	  have h2 : Order.coheight (α := U.toScheme) ⟨z, hzU⟩
205	      = Order.coheight (α := Spec Γ(X, U)) (eOrder ⟨z, hzU⟩) :=
206	    (Order.coheight_orderIso eOrder ⟨z, hzU⟩).symm
207	  have h3 : eOrder ⟨z, hzU⟩ = p := rfl
208	  -- (5c) Decl 2: coheight (Spec R) p = height (PrimeSpectrum R) p.
209	  have h6 : Order.coheight (α := Spec Γ(X, U)) p
210	      = Order.height (α := PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U))
211	          ⟨p.asIdeal, p.isPrime⟩ :=
212	    Order.coheight_spec_eq_height_primeSpectrum p
213	  have h7 : (⟨p.asIdeal, p.isPrime⟩ : PrimeSpectrum Γ(X, U)) = p := rfl
214	  -- Assemble.
215	  rw [h4, h1, h2, h3, h6, h7]
216	
217	end Scheme
218	
219	end AlgebraicGeometry
220	
221	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
222	
223	namespace Scheme
224	
225	/-- **Codim-1 wrapper: coheight = 1 ⟹ stalk Krull dim ≤ 1.** Consumer-facing
226	specialisation of `ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` for the codim-`1` case used
227	in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` and `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`. -/
228	lemma ringKrullDimLE_of_coheight_eq_one
229	    (X : Scheme.{u}) (z : X) (hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
230	    Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk z) := by
231	  rw [Ring.krullDimLE_iff, ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight, hz]
232	  -- Goal: ((1 : ℕ∞) : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ((1 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞)
233	  norm_cast
234	
235	end Scheme
236	
237	end AlgebraicGeometry

9	/-!
10	# Milne Theorem 3.2: a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian
11	variety extends (A.4.c)
12	
13	This file is the **A.4.c** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
14	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the single
15	theorem of Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3, Theorem 3.2:
16	**a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety extends,
17	uniquely, to a regular morphism**. This is the input that the Albanese
18	universal-property build (A.4.d, `Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`) consumes when
19	promoting the symmetric-product rational map
20	`C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism `J → A`.
21	
22	Milne's proof is two lines: combine Theorem 3.1 (the codim-$\geq 2$ extension
23	result for rational maps from a normal variety to a complete variety —
24	project-side `thm:codim_one_extension` of
25	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, A.4.a) with Lemma 3.3 (the
26	pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy structure for rational maps into a group variety
27	— project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` of the same chapter). The
28	combination forces the indeterminacy locus of `f` to be simultaneously of
29	codimension `≥ 2` and pure codimension `1`, hence empty, so `f` is everywhere
30	defined.
31	
32	## Status (iter-175 Lane J file-skeleton)
33	
34	This file is the **iter-175 Lane J** file-skeleton: the single pinned
35	declaration carries the intended substantive type signature (matching the
36	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in
37	`chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` line 50) with a `sorry`
38	body. The body is iter-176+ work, gated on the sibling chapters
39	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (A.4.a) and
40	`Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b) landing their substantive
41	statements.
42	
43	The 1 pinned declaration is:
44	
45	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
46	   — Milne Theorem 3.2: for `X` a smooth (= nonsingular) variety over an
47	   algebraically closed field `k̄`, `A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, and
48	   `f : X ⇢ A` a rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism
49	   `g : X ⟶ A` whose induced rational map equals `f`.
50	
51	## Note on type expressivity
52	
53	The signature is encoded against Mathlib's existing
54	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` (a partial-map equivalence class on
55	dense opens) and `Scheme.Hom.toRationalMap` (the canonical "regular morphism
56	yields a rational map" coercion). The substantive claim is the existence
57	and uniqueness of a `g : X.left ⟶ A.left` with `g.toRationalMap = f`. The
58	type is non-tautological: the existence is the content of Milne 3.2;
59	uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
60	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`).
61	
62	## Abelian-variety conventions
63	
64	Throughout the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`),
65	an **abelian variety over `k̄`** is encoded as an object
66	`A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying the four instances:
67	
68	- `[GrpObj A]` (group-object structure on the over-category),
69	- `[IsProper A.hom]` (complete),
70	- `[Smooth A.hom]` (nonsingular),
71	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` (geometrically irreducible).
72	
73	A **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** (in the convention of
74	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` §1) is an
75	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
76	
77	- `[Smooth X.hom]`,
78	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
79	- `[IsSeparated X.hom]`,
80	- `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
81	- `[IsIntegral X.left]`,
82	- `[IsReduced X.left]`.
83	
84	These four-plus-two instances will normally be supplied by `inferInstance` at
85	the call site.
86	
87	## References
88	
89	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex`
90	(250 LOC, 1 pin). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §I.3, Theorem 3.2,
91	p. 17 (`references/abelian-varieties.pdf`, PDF page 23).
92	-/

94	set_option autoImplicit false
95	
96	universe u
97	
98	open CategoryTheory Limits
99	
100	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
101	
102	namespace Scheme
103	
104	namespace RationalMap
105	
106	/-! ## Milne Theorem 3.2 — rational maps to an abelian variety extend
107	
108	Let `k̄` be an algebraically closed field. Let `X` be a nonsingular variety
109	over `k̄` (smooth, geometrically irreducible, separated, locally of finite
110	type, integral, reduced). Let `A` be an abelian variety over `k̄`
111	(a group object that is proper, smooth, and geometrically irreducible). For
112	every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` there is a *unique* regular morphism
113	`g : X ⟶ A` whose induced rational map equals `f`.
114	
115	Milne's argument (`Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` §1.4, Theorem 3.2):
116	let `U ⊆ X` be a maximal open of definition of `f` and `Z := X ∖ U`.
117	By the codim-$\geq 2$ extension theorem (Theorem 3.1, project-side
118	`thm:codim_one_extension`) the closed subset `Z` has codimension `≥ 2`.
119	By the pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy lemma for maps into a group variety
120	(Lemma 3.3, project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy`), `Z` is either
121	empty or of pure codimension `1`. Both conditions force `Z = ∅`, so `U = X`
122	and `f` admits a regular representative on all of `X`. Uniqueness is the
123	standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle (cf.
124	`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two regular morphisms `X ⟶ A` that
125	agree on a dense open of `X` agree everywhere.
126	
127	Blueprint reference: `thm:rational_map_to_av_extends` (Milne, *Abelian
128	Varieties*, Theorem 3.2, §I.3, p. 17). The pin in the chapter is at
129	`Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` line 50.
130	
131	iter-176+ proof skeleton:
132	1. Pick a `PartialMap` representative `φ : X.PartialMap A` of `f` (using
133	   `Scheme.RationalMap.toPartialMap`, which exists for `X` reduced and `A`
134	   separated; both hold from the abelian-variety + variety instances).
135	2. Show `φ.domain = ⊤` by combining `thm:codim_one_extension` (codim `≥ 2`
136	   complement) with `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (complement empty or
137	   pure codim `1`).
138	3. Read off `g := φ.hom` (the morphism on the full domain) and verify
139	   `g.toRationalMap = f`.
140	4. Uniqueness: two `g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ A` with `g₁.toRationalMap = g₂.toRationalMap`
141	   agree on a dense open of `X`, so by
142	   `AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant` and reducedness of `X` they agree
143	   everywhere. -/
144	
145	/-- **Iter-180 Lane G split (1/2): integrality of the abelian variety.**
146	
147	For an abelian variety `A` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` —
148	encoded in the project as `A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying
149	`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` —
150	the underlying scheme `A.left` is integral.
151	
152	**Derivation.** The proof has three steps:
153	
154	1. `Spec k̄` is a singleton scheme (Mathlib instance
155	   `Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf` provides `Unique`,
156	   which yields both `Subsingleton` and `Nonempty`).
157	2. `GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom + Spec k̄ a single point ⟹ IrreducibleSpace A.left`
158	   via Mathlib's `GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton`.
159	3. `IrreducibleSpace + IsReduced ⟹ IsIntegral` via Mathlib's
160	   `isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`.
161	
162	The substantive Mathlib gap is the implication `Smooth A.hom ⟹ IsReduced A.left`
163	(at the project's pinned commit, Mathlib's `Smooth` morphism class does not
164	ship a `IsReduced` consequence for the source over a reduced base). The
165	gap is isolated to the single `IsReduced A.left` hypothesis in the proof,
166	which is left as a `sorry`; all other steps close axiom-clean.
167	
168	When Mathlib ships the bridge (Stacks `034V` / `02G4` over a field
169	direction), the single `sorry` discharges and this helper becomes
170	axiom-clean automatically.
171	
172	This is the iter-180 Lane G refactor of the iter-179 helper
173	`av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`, splitting its `IsIntegral A.left`
174	conjunct into a self-contained named lemma that does not depend on the
175	rational map `f`. -/
176	private theorem av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred
177	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
178	    (A : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
179	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
180	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] :
181	    IsIntegral A.left := by
182	  -- Step 1: `Spec k̄` has a unique point (Mathlib instance
183	  -- `Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf`); both
184	  -- `Subsingleton` and `Nonempty` follow.
185	  haveI : Subsingleton (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
186	  haveI : Nonempty (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
187	  -- Step 2: geometric irreducibility over a singleton base gives `IrreducibleSpace`.
188	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace A.left :=
189	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton A.hom
190	  -- Step 3 (Mathlib gap): smoothness over a field forces reducedness of the
191	  -- source. This is Stacks `034V`/`02G4` over a (reduced) base, but the
192	  -- scheme-level packaging does not ship in Mathlib at the project's pinned
193	  -- commit.
194	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := sorry
195	  exact isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced A.left
196	
197	/-- **Iter-180 Lane G split (2/2): codim-1-indeterminacy-freeness from
198	Milne's two-input argument.**
199	
200	For a rational map `f : X ⇢ A` from a nonsingular variety `X` to an
201	abelian variety `A` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every
202	codim-1 point of `X` lies in `f.domain` (`CodimOneFree f`).
203	
204	**Derivation (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, proof of Theorem 3.2, §I.3, p. 17).**
205	Two project-side inputs combine to force the indeterminacy locus empty:
206	
207	1. `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3 in
208	   `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): for a rational map from a smooth
209	   integral variety to a *group* variety, the indeterminacy locus is
210	   either empty or pure codim 1.
211	2. The *codim-≥2 conclusion* of Milne Theorem 3.1 (the unbundled half of
212	   `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`):
213	   for a rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety,
214	   the indeterminacy locus has codim `≥ 2`.
215	
216	Combining (1) ∨ (2): pure codim 1 contradicts codim `≥ 2`, so the
217	indeterminacy locus is empty; trivially every point — and in particular
218	every codim-1 point — lies in `f.domain`, so `CodimOneFree f`.
219	
220	**Status of the substantive content.** The codim-≥2 conclusion of
221	Milne 3.1 is not currently exposed by the project as a standalone lemma;
222	it is encapsulated inside `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s body (which
223	is itself `sorry` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`). Once the project
224	exposes a lemma `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` separating
225	out the codim-≥2 conclusion, this helper closes via the two-line
226	combination above. Until then it is left as a named `sorry` whose
227	docstring exhaustively records the combination strategy, replacing the
228	iter-179 conjunction-level `sorry` with a more targeted one.
229	
230	This is the iter-180 Lane G refactor's split: the `CodimOneFree f`
231	conjunct is split off into a self-contained named lemma with the
232	substantive missing content localised here. -/
233	private theorem av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy
234	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
235	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
236	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
237	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
238	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
239	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
240	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
241	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
242	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
243	    CodimOneFree f := by
244	  -- **Iter-182 structural refactor.** Materialise the variety-package
245	  -- instances on `A.left` needed by Milne Lemma 3.3
246	  -- (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): `IsIntegral` from the
247	  -- split helper `av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred`; `IsReduced` from
248	  -- `isReduced_of_isIntegral`; `IsSeparated` from `IsProper`;
249	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType` from `Smooth`.
250	  haveI : IsIntegral A.left := av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred A
251	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := inferInstance
252	  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
253	  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom := inferInstance
254	  -- Apply Milne Lemma 3.3: the indeterminacy locus is empty or every point
255	  -- of it lies in the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
256	  have hdisj := indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme f
257	  intro x hx
258	  rcases hdisj with hempty | hpure
259	  · -- **Branch 1 (CLOSED).** Empty indeterminacy locus: every point of `X`
260	    -- — in particular `x` — lies in `f.domain` (no codim-1 obstruction).
261	    by_contra hnotin
262	    have hxZ : x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f := hnotin
263	    rw [hempty] at hxZ
264	    exact hxZ.elim
265	  · -- **Branch 2 (PROJECT GAP).** The disjunct gives, for any `x ∈ Z(f)`,
266	    -- a codim-1 generic point `z` with `x ∈ closure {z}`. To prove
267	    -- `x ∈ f.domain` for our codim-1 `x`, contrapositive: assume
268	    -- `x ∈ Z(f)`. The disjunct produces a `z` with `coheight z = 1` and
269	    -- `x ∈ closure {z}` — but for a codim-1 `x`, sobriety of the scheme
270	    -- forces `z = x` (chains of length 1 starting at `z` and ending at a
271	    -- specialisation `x` of equal coheight collapse in a sober T₀ space),
272	    -- so `x = z` is a codim-1 point of `Z(f)`. The contradiction needs
273	    -- the **codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1** (project-side
274	    -- `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s unbundled half), which is
275	    -- currently encapsulated inside that theorem's `sorry`'d body in
276	    -- `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` rather than exposed as a separate
277	    -- lemma. Once a lemma
278	    -- `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete : ∀ z ∈ indeterminacyLocus f, coheight z ≥ 2`
279	    -- lands in `CodimOneExtension.lean`, branch 2 closes via:
280	    --   `by_contra hnotin; obtain ⟨z, hz1, hxz⟩ := hpure x hnotin;`
281	    --   `obtain rfl := sober_eq_of_specializes_of_coheight_eq hxz hx hz1;`
282	    --   `exact absurd hx (lt_irrefl 1 ∘ ... ∘ codimGe2 hnotin)`.
283	    sorry
284	
285	/-- **Iter-182 documentation helper: branch-1 of `av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy`.**
286	The empty-indeterminacy branch of Milne Lemma 3.3 closes `CodimOneFree`
287	without depending on the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1. Exposed as a
288	standalone lemma so the structural split survives any future refactor of
289	the parent helper. Axiom-clean. -/
290	private theorem codimOneFree_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty
291	    {X Y : Scheme.{u}} {f : X.RationalMap Y}
292	    (h : indeterminacyLocus f = ∅) : CodimOneFree f := by
293	  intro x _
294	  by_contra hnotin
295	  have hxZ : x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f := hnotin
296	  rw [h] at hxZ
297	  exact hxZ.elim
298	
299	/-- **Iter-179 Lane E + iter-180 Lane G: the two missing project inputs
300	for `extend_to_av`.**
301	
302	The conjunction of the two named iter-180 Lane G splits:
303	`av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred` (the `IsIntegral A.left` conjunct, with
304	its `IsReduced A.left` Mathlib gap isolated) and
305	`av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy` (the `CodimOneFree f` conjunct, with its
306	codim-≥2-from-Milne-3.1 gap isolated).
307	
308	This wrapper preserves the iter-179 call-site API (`extend_to_av` invokes
309	the conjunction in one `obtain`) while routing the proof to the two
310	narrower lemmas. The body itself is axiom-clean — all substantive content
311	is localised in the two split helpers above. -/
312	private theorem av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree
313	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
314	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
315	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
316	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
317	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
318	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
319	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
320	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
321	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
322	    IsIntegral A.left ∧ CodimOneFree f :=
323	  ⟨av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred A,
324	    av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy f⟩
325	
326	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.2 — a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an
327	abelian variety extends, uniquely, to a regular morphism.**
328	
329	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, and
330	`A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` has a
331	*unique* regular extension `g : X ⟶ A` (i.e. `g.toRationalMap = f`).
332	
333	This is the input the Albanese universal-property build
334	(`Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`, A.4.d) consumes when promoting the
335	symmetric-product rational map `C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism
336	`J → A`.
337	
338	**Iter-179 Lane E body.** The proof is the standard one-line reduction of
339	Milne's two-line argument:
340	
341	1. Apply `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (Milne Theorem 3.1, project-side
342	   `thm:codim_one_extension` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): given a
343	   `CodimOneFree` rational map from a smooth integral variety to a complete
344	   variety, the map extends uniquely to a regular morphism. This requires
345	   the variety-package instances on `A.left` (separated, locally of finite
346	   type, integral, reduced) in addition to the four AV instances supplied
347	   by the caller.
348	
349	2. Discharge `CodimOneFree f` by invoking the helper
350	   `av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`, which combines
351	   `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure
352	   codim 1 or empty indeterminacy for grpScheme targets) with the codim-≥2
353	   half of Milne 3.1. Both pieces are documented in that helper's
354	   docstring; the helper carries the single `sorry` that captures the
355	   remaining math content the project spec does not yet expose.
356	
357	3. Materialize the remaining variety-package instances on `A.left`:
358	   `IsSeparated A.hom` from `IsProper A.hom` (Mathlib instance),
359	   `LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom` from `Smooth A.hom` (Mathlib instance),
360	   `IsReduced A.left` from `IsIntegral A.left` (Mathlib instance
361	   `isReduced_of_isIntegral`).
362	
363	The proof body therefore introduces NO new inline `sorry`: all missing
364	content is concentrated in the single named helper above. -/
365	theorem extend_to_av
366	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
367	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
368	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
369	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
370	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
371	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
372	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
373	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
374	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
375	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ A.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
376	  -- Step 3: materialise the missing variety-package instances on A.left.
377	  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
378	  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom := inferInstance
379	  obtain ⟨hint, hcod⟩ := av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree f
380	  haveI := hint
381	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := inferInstance
382	  -- Steps 1–2: apply Milne Theorem 3.1 with the `CodimOneFree` discharged.
383	  exact extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth f hcod
384	
385	end RationalMap
386	
387	end Scheme
388	
389	end AlgebraicGeometry

10	/-!
11	# Codimension-1 indeterminacy extension (A.4.a)
12	
13	This file is the **A.4.a** sub-build chapter for the positive-genus arm of the
14	project's `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the two extension inputs to
15	Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3 Theorem 3.2, plus the Weil-divisor
16	reformulation of the codim-`1` obstruction:
17	
18	* **Milne Theorem 3.1** (`extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`): a rational map
19	  from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has indeterminacy locus
20	  of codimension `≥ 2`; if additionally the map is already
21	  codim-`1`-indeterminacy-free, then it extends to a regular morphism.
22	* **Milne Lemma 3.3** (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): for a
23	  rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety, the
24	  indeterminacy locus is either empty or of pure codimension `1`.
25	* **Weil-divisor obstruction** (`mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`):
26	  `f` is defined at the generic point of a prime divisor `W` iff some
27	  `PartialMap` representative of `f` is regular at `W.point`. This is a thin
28	  re-shuffle of Mathlib's `RationalMap.mem_domain`; the substantive
29	  Hartshorne-II.6 "regular ⇔ order ≥ 0 at every DVR pullback" reformulation
30	  (the original Weil-divisor obstruction) is deferred to a future lemma
31	  that builds genuine pullback machinery (`Scheme.RationalMap.order`-along-`f`),
32	  outside this iter's scope.
33	
34	These outputs feed Milne Theorem 3.2 in the sibling
35	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean` (A.4.c) and consume
36	`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b)
37	at Step 2 of `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`.
38	
39	## Status (iter-177 Lane 6 file-skeleton)
40	
41	Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended*
42	substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
43	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`) with a `sorry` body.
44	Bodies are iter-178+ work, gated on the local-cohomology / depth-≥2
45	extension lemma (Step 2 of `thm:codim_one_extension`) being supplied either
46	from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (the `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`
47	input) or from a Mathlib upstream once the local-cohomology vanishing
48	`H¹_{x}(V, 𝒪_X) = 0` at a depth-≥2 point lands.
49	
50	The 6 pinned declarations are:
51	
52	1. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus` (def, ~3 LOC) — the closed
53	   complement of `RationalMap.domain` as a `Set X`.
54	2. `Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree` (def, ~3 LOC) — predicate: every
55	   point `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in `f.domain`.
56	3. `Scheme.localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` (theorem, ~3 LOC) — for a smooth
57	   integral variety, the stalk at a codim-1 point is a DVR.
58	4. `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (theorem, ~10 LOC) —
59	   Milne 3.1 specialised to smooth source + complete target +
60	   codim-1-indeterminacy-free.
61	5. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`
62	   (theorem, ~8 LOC) — Milne Lemma 3.3.
63	6. `Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`
64	   (theorem, ~2 LOC) — definitional re-shuffle of `RationalMap.mem_domain`
65	   exposing a `PartialMap` representative whose domain contains `W.point`.
66	   The substantive Weil-divisor `order ≥ 0` reformulation is deferred (see
67	   header).
68	
69	## Note on type expressivity
70	
71	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
72	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
73	
74	* `indeterminacyLocus f : Set X` returns the carrier of the closed
75	  complement of `f.domain` — not `∅` or `Set.univ`.
76	* `CodimOneFree f : Prop` is the genuine ∀-statement over codim-1 points,
77	  not `True`.
78	* `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` produces a Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing`
79	  instance — not a trivial proposition.
80	* `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` asserts `∃! g, g.toRationalMap = f`
81	  — the existence of a unique regular extension.
82	* `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` asserts a disjunction:
83	  either the locus is empty or every component has coheight `1`.
84	* `mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point` asserts the (truthful)
85	  iff between definedness at `W.point` and the existence of a `PartialMap`
86	  representative containing `W.point` in its domain — a definitional unfold
87	  of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` with the
88	  conjunction swapped for downstream ergonomics.
89	
90	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
91	(the carrier set complement, the `∀ η, ...` statement, the DVR instance,
92	the unique regular extension, …); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content
93	`proof_wanted` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.
94	
95	## Variety conventions
96	
97	Following the project (cf. `Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`,
98	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`), a **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** is an
99	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
100	
101	* `[Smooth X.hom]`, `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
102	* `[IsSeparated X.hom]`, `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
103	* `[IsIntegral X.left]`, `[IsReduced X.left]`.
104	
105	A **complete variety** adds `[IsProper Y.hom]`. A **group variety** over
106	`k̄` adds a `[GrpObj Y]` instance to the variety package.
107	
108	## References
109	
110	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex` (~750 LOC,
111	6 pins). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §I.3, Theorem 3.1 (p. 16) and
112	Lemma 3.3 (p. 17); Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, II.6 pp. 130–131.
113	-/
