115	set_option autoImplicit false
116	
117	universe u
118	
119	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
120	
121	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
122	
123	namespace Scheme
124	
125	namespace RationalMap
126	
127	/-! ## §1. The indeterminacy locus of a rational map
128	
129	The indeterminacy locus `Z(f) ⊆ X` of a rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is the closed
130	complement of its domain of definition `Dom(f) := f.domain` (a Mathlib-shipped
131	open subset of `X`).
132	
133	Blueprint pin: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne §I.3 p. 16). -/
134	
135	/-- **Indeterminacy locus of a rational map.** The closed complement of the
136	domain of definition `f.domain` (an `X.Opens`, exposed by Mathlib at
137	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap`):
138	
139	`Z(f) := X ∖ Dom(f) ⊆ X`.
140	
141	`f` is everywhere defined (i.e. extends to a regular morphism) iff
142	`indeterminacyLocus f = ∅`.
143	
144	Blueprint reference: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
145	§I.3, p. 16). -/
146	def indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Set X :=
147	  (f.domain : Set X)ᶜ
148	
149	/-- The indeterminacy locus of a rational map is closed (it is the complement
150	of an open). -/
151	lemma isClosed_indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
152	    IsClosed (indeterminacyLocus f) := by
153	  unfold indeterminacyLocus
154	  exact (f.domain.isOpen).isClosed_compl
155	
156	/-! ## §2. Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps
157	
158	A rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is **codim-1-indeterminacy-free** if its
159	indeterminacy locus contains no prime divisor of `X`, i.e. every codim-1
160	generic point of `X` already lies in `f.domain`. Equivalently (under the
161	project's standing `Order.coheight` convention on the specialisation
162	preorder of `X.carrier`), every `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in
163	`f.domain`.
164	
165	Blueprint pin: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1, the
166	conclusion). -/
167	
168	/-- **Codim-1-indeterminacy-free rational map.** A rational map
169	`f : X ⇢ Y` is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if every codimension-one point
170	of `X` lies in its domain of definition. Equivalently: no prime divisor of
171	`X` (i.e. closure of a codim-1 generic point) is contained in
172	`indeterminacyLocus f`.
173	
174	The codim-1 condition is encoded via `Order.coheight η = 1` on the
175	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier`, matching the project's standing
176	convention in `Scheme.PrimeDivisor` (`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`).
177	
178	Blueprint reference: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
179	Varieties*, Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
180	def CodimOneFree {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Prop :=
181	  ∀ (x : X), Order.coheight x = 1 → x ∈ f.domain
182	
183	end RationalMap
184	
185	/-! ## §3. Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point
186	
187	On a nonsingular variety `X` over `k̄`, the local ring at the generic point
188	of a prime divisor is a discrete valuation ring with fraction field equal to
189	the function field `K(X) = X.functionField`. This is Hartshorne II.6's
190	"regular in codimension one" property combined with the regular-local-ring-of-dim-1
191	$\Leftrightarrow$ DVR equivalence (Atiyah–Macdonald 9.2; Stacks `00PD`).
192	
193	Blueprint pin: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130). -/
194	
195	/-- **Helper for `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`.** On a smooth integral variety
196	over an algebraically closed field, the stalk at a codimension-`1` point has a
197	*principal nonzero* maximal ideal. Encodes the substantive geometric content
198	of "regular in codimension one": smoothness ⟹ `IsRegularLocalRing` at the
199	stalk (Stacks `00PD`/`00TT`), and a Noetherian regular local ring with
200	`ringKrullDim = 1` has principal nonzero maximal ideal (Stacks `02IZ`),
201	via the cotangent-space `finrank = 1` characterisation.
202	
203	**Iter-179 Lane D body.** The Mathlib bridges
204	- `Algebra.Smooth k A → IsRegularLocalRing A` at every point (Stacks `00TT`
205	  Jacobian criterion side, scheme-level packaging), and
206	- `Order.coheight z = 1 → ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 1`
207	  (the topological-coheight-to-ring-Krull-dim bridge that
208	  `Scheme.RationalMap.order`'s `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1]` instance is gated on,
209	  see `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean` L148-151)
210	
211	do not ship in Mathlib at commit `b80f227`. We package both as a single
212	internal `sorry` (the `hreg_dim` hypothesis below) and discharge the
213	remaining principal-nonzero conclusion via Mathlib's
214	`IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace` +
215	`IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff` chain. The internal sorry
216	documents exactly the bridge needed: once the smooth-scheme regularity
217	and coheight-Krull-dim instances land in Mathlib (or a project upstream),
218	they discharge `hreg_dim` and the helper closes axiom-clean.
219	
220	The previous body was a bare `sorry`; this Lane-D refactor preserves the
221	warning count at 1 (one sorry per declaration) while exposing the proof
222	skeleton for the next prover. -/
223	private theorem smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot
224	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
225	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
226	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
227	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
228	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
229	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
230	    Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) ∧
231	      IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ := by
232	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk (needed for the cotangent-space API):
233	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the automatic `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
234	  -- through to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk picks up
235	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance).
236	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
237	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
238	  -- The Mathlib-gap content, packaged as a single hypothesis: smoothness +
239	  -- codim-1-witness ⟹ the stalk is a regular local ring of Krull dimension 1.
240	  -- Stacks `00TT` (Jacobian criterion for smoothness ⟹ regular local) and
241	  -- the topological-coheight-to-ring-Krull-dim bridge (Stacks `02IZ`/`005X`)
242	  -- together discharge this; neither half ships in Mathlib at b80f227.
243	  -- iter-184 Lane M downstream: split the conjunction. The Krull-dim half
244	  -- closes axiom-clean via `Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` (the
245	  -- iter-183 `Albanese/CoheightBridge.lean` bridge) + `_hz`. The
246	  -- `IsRegularLocalRing` half is the Stacks `00TT` Jacobian-criterion
247	  -- gap (smoothness over `k̄` ⟹ regular at the stalk): not shipped by
248	  -- Mathlib at b80f227; iter-185+ blueprint expansion. See the file
249	  -- header and `Albanese/CoheightBridge.lean` header for status.
250	  have hreg_dim : IsRegularLocalRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ∧
251	                    ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 1 := by
252	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
253	    · -- `IsRegularLocalRing` half — Stacks `00TT` gap (iter-185+).
254	      sorry
255	    · -- `ringKrullDim = 1` half — closes via the iter-183 axiom-clean
256	      -- CoheightBridge equality + the codim-1 witness `_hz`.
257	      rw [Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight]
258	      exact_mod_cast _hz
259	  obtain ⟨hreg, hdim⟩ := hreg_dim
260	  -- From regularity + dim = 1, the cotangent space has `finrank = 1`
261	  -- (Mathlib `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`).
262	  have hfin :
263	      Module.finrank
264	        (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z))
265	        (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) = 1 := by
266	    have h := (IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace _).mp hreg
267	    rw [hdim] at h
268	    exact_mod_cast h
269	  -- Principal maximal ideal: `finrank ≤ 1 ↔ IsPrincipal maximalIdeal`
270	  -- (Mathlib `IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff`).
271	  have hprin : Submodule.IsPrincipal
272	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) :=
273	    IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff.mp hfin.le
274	  -- `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`: equivalent to "not a field" by Mathlib
275	  -- `IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq`, and a field has Krull dim 0,
276	  -- contradicting `hdim : ringKrullDim = 1` via `ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField`.
277	  have hne : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ := by
278	    intro hbot
279	    have hF : IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) :=
280	      IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq.mpr hbot
281	    have h0 : ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 0 :=
282	      ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField hF
283	    -- Contradiction: `1 = 0` in `WithBot ℕ∞`.
284	    rw [hdim] at h0
285	    exact_mod_cast h0
286	  exact ⟨hprin, hne⟩
287	
288	/-- **Smooth + codim-1 ⇒ DVR.** For a nonsingular integral variety `X` over
289	an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a point `η : X.left` with
290	`Order.coheight η = 1`, the stalk `X.left.presheaf.stalk η` is a discrete
291	valuation ring (with fraction field the function field `K(X)`).
292	
293	The proof reduces to "regular local ring of Krull dimension `1` is a DVR"
294	(Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing.isDiscreteValuationRing_iff` or analogous)
295	plus the smooth-variety fact that every local ring at a codim-1 point is
296	regular of dimension `1`. The geometric step is packaged in the private helper
297	`smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot`; the rest assembles the
298	DVR instance via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE`.
299	
300	Blueprint reference: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130;
301	Stacks `00PD` for the regular-of-dim-1 ⇔ DVR equivalence). -/
302	theorem localRing_dvr_of_codim_one
303	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
304	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
305	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
306	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
307	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
308	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
309	    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
310	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk:
311	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the (automatic) `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
312	  -- to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk inherits
313	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance). The stalk of an integral scheme is
314	  -- a domain (Mathlib instance), and stalks of schemes are always local (Mathlib
315	  -- instance); these are picked up automatically by typeclass search.
316	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
317	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
318	  -- Extract the principal+nonzero-maximal-ideal helper (the Mathlib-gap content).
319	  obtain ⟨hprin, hne⟩ :=
320	    smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot X z _hz
321	  -- The stalk is a local Noetherian domain with principal maximal ideal:
322	  -- promote `IsPrincipal (maximalIdeal)` to a `IsPrincipalIdealRing` instance
323	  -- and conclude DVR via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.mk`.
324	  -- The TFAE entry "principal maximal ideal ⇔ DVR" handles the conversion
325	  -- once we know `¬ IsField` (which follows from `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`).
326	  have hfield : ¬ IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
327	    intro hF
328	    exact hne ((IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq).mp hF)
329	  have tfae := IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) hfield
330	  -- TFAE entry index 4 is `Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)`.
331	  exact ((tfae.out 0 4).mpr hprin)
332	
333	namespace RationalMap
334	
335	/-! ## §4. Milne Theorem 3.1 — codim-≥ 2 extension to a complete target
336	
337	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has
338	indeterminacy locus of codimension `≥ 2`. Specialising further: if `f` is
339	already codim-1-indeterminacy-free (by hypothesis), then `Dom(f) = X` and
340	`f` extends to a unique regular morphism `X ⟶ Y`.
341	
342	Blueprint pin: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1 p. 16). -/
343	
344	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.1 (specialised) — codim-1-indeterminacy-free + smooth
345	source + complete target ⇒ extension.**
346	
347	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
348	`Y` a complete variety over `k̄`, and `f : X ⇢ Y` a codim-1-indeterminacy-free
349	rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism `g : X ⟶ Y`
350	whose induced rational map equals `f`.
351	
352	The proof combines two steps:
353	* **Step 1** (codim-1 ruling-out): the indeterminacy locus has no codim-1
354	  components, because at the generic point of any candidate prime divisor
355	  `W ⊆ Z(f)` the DVR property of the stalk (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`)
356	  feeds the valuative criterion of properness on the complete target `Y`.
357	* **Step 2** (codim-≥2 extension): at a remaining codim-≥2 point `x`, the
358	  depth-≥2 property of the regular local ring `O_{X,x}` (Cohen–Macaulay
359	  from `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` of `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`)
360	  forces `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` and the pulled-back coordinates extend
361	  uniquely. By the `CodimOneFree` hypothesis, Step 1 alone is already
362	  enough on the dim-≤2 cases the project consumes; Step 2 is what extends
363	  through the remaining higher-codim points to obtain `Dom(f) = X`.
364	
365	Uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
366	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two morphisms `X ⟶ Y` that agree
367	on a dense open of the reduced scheme `X` agree everywhere.
368	
369	Blueprint reference: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
370	Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
371	theorem extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth
372	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
373	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
374	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
375	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
376	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
377	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
378	    [IsProper Y.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]
379	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
380	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
381	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (_hf : CodimOneFree f) :
382	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ Y.left), g.toRationalMap = f :=
383	  sorry
384	
385	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
386	
387	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
388	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
389	`thm:codim_one_extension`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus to
390	be empty when the target is an abelian variety.
391	
392	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
393	
394	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
395	codim 1.**
396	
397	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
398	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
399	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
400	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or every point of `Z(f)` lies in
401	the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
402	
403	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
404	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
405	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
406	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
407	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
408	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
409	
410	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
411	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
412	theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
413	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
414	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
415	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
416	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
417	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
418	    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
419	    [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
420	    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
421	    [IsIntegral G.left] [IsReduced G.left]
422	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left) :
423	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
424	      ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
425	        ∃ (z : X.left), Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left) :=
426	  sorry
427	
428	/-! ## §6. Definedness at a prime-divisor generic point
429	
430	A truthful, lightweight reformulation of definedness at a prime-divisor
431	generic point: `f` is defined at `W.point` iff `f` admits a `PartialMap`
432	representative whose domain contains `W.point`. This is a definitional
433	re-shuffle of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`
434	swapping the order of the two conjuncts under the existential for downstream
435	ergonomics (so the `toRationalMap = f` conjunct lands first, the way later
436	witnesses are constructed in this project).
437	
438	### Status note (iter-179 Lane D, Path D2)
439	
440	The blueprint pin `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` (Hartshorne II.6
441	pp. 130–131) calls for the genuinely substantive statement
442	
443	> `f` is defined at `W.point` iff every regular function on every affine
444	> open `V ⊆ Y` around `f(W.point)` pulls back to an element of
445	> `O_{X,W.point}` ⊂ `K(X)` of `ord_W`-value `≥ 0`,
446	
447	which requires the pullback machinery
448	`Scheme.RationalMap → K(Y) → K(X)` and the project's existing
449	`Scheme.RationalMap.order : X.functionField → ℤ` valuation from
450	`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`. The Lean-level construction of the
451	`RationalMap`-to-function-field ring map is **not** shipped by Mathlib
452	at commit `b80f227`, and building it inside this file would constitute
453	a substantive new sub-build (cf. `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
454	on the pullback of a sheaf-of-algebras along a morphism).
455	
456	iter-178's `extend_iff_order_nonneg` claimed the substantive statement
457	in its docstring but only proved this reshuffle; the lemma's name and
458	documentation are corrected here (auditor 178B's Path D2 honest fallback)
459	to match the proven content. The `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1]` and
460	`[IsLocallyNoetherian X.left]` typeclass binders that the substantive
461	statement would have required are dropped along with the renaming, since
462	the reshuffle does not use them. The substantive statement is deferred to
463	a future lemma (working title: `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg`) once
464	the pullback machinery lands.
465	
466	Blueprint reference (in its current weakened form): a definitional unfold
467	of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`. -/
468	
469	/-- **Definedness at the generic point of a prime divisor, repackaged.**
470	
471	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `Y` a variety over `k̄`,
472	`f : X ⇢ Y` a rational map, and `W : X.left.PrimeDivisor`, the following
473	are equivalent:
474	
475	1. `W.point ∈ f.domain` (i.e.\ `f` is defined at the generic point of `W`).
476	2. There exists a `PartialMap` representative `g` of `f` (a regular morphism
477	   `g.hom : g.domain ⟶ Y.left` from a dense open `g.domain ⊆ X.left` with
478	   `g.toRationalMap = f`) whose domain contains `W.point`.
479	
480	This is the canonical existential characterisation of `f.domain` membership
481	via `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`, with the two
482	conjuncts under the existential reordered so the `toRationalMap = f`
483	component lands first (matching the way later witnesses in this project
484	construct partial-map representatives).
485	
486	**Iter-179 Lane D scope.** This statement is the auditor-178B Path D2 honest
487	fallback: the substantive Hartshorne-II.6 "regular = no pole" Weil-divisor
488	reformulation (Stacks `02ME`), which would tighten the right-hand side to
489	an `ord_W ≥ 0` condition on every pulled-back regular function, is deferred
490	to a future lemma that builds the missing `RationalMap → function-field`
491	pullback machinery. See the section-level docstring above. -/
492	theorem mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point
493	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
494	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
495	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
496	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
497	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
498	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
499	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
500	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
501	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (W : X.left.PrimeDivisor) :
502	    W.point ∈ f.domain ↔
503	      ∃ (g : X.left.PartialMap Y.left),
504	        g.toRationalMap = f ∧ W.point ∈ (g.domain : Set X.left) := by
505	  rw [Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain]
506	  exact ⟨fun ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩ => ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩, fun ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩ => ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩⟩
507	
508	end RationalMap
509	
510	end Scheme
511	
512	end AlgebraicGeometry

9	/-!
10	# The Albanese universal property of `Pic⁰_{C/k}` (A.4.d)
11	
12	This file is the **A.4.d** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
13	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages Milne's
14	*Abelian Varieties* §III.6 Proposition 6.1: for a complete nonsingular curve
15	`C` of genus `g > 0` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a marked
16	`k̄`-point `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, the Abel–Jacobi morphism
17	`ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` is the **Albanese morphism** of the pointed curve
18	`(C, P₀)`: any pointed morphism `φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety with
19	`φ(P₀) = η_A` factors uniquely as `φ = ψ ∘ ι_{P₀}` for a homomorphism of
20	group schemes `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`.
21	
22	The chapter commits to the **symmetric-power route** (cube-free): the
23	symmetric assignment `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` factors through
24	`Sym^g C`, the resulting morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` descends through
25	the birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` to a rational map
26	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`, and Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (`extend_to_av` in the sibling
27	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`, A.4.c) promotes it to a regular
28	morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`. The group-homomorphism property of `ψ` then
29	follows from `ψ(η_J) = η_A` together with Milne Corollary I.1.2 (a
30	consequence of `thm:rigidity_lemma`).
31	
32	## Status (iter-177 Lane 7 file-skeleton)
33	
34	This file is the **iter-177 Lane 7** file-skeleton. Each of the six
35	blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended* substantive type
36	signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
37	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AlbaneseUP.tex`) with a `sorry` body.
38	
39	The 6 pinned declarations are:
40	
41	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.abelJacobi` (noncomputable def, ~6 LOC) — the
42	   regular morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` of
43	   `lem:abel_jacobi_morphism`. Encoded against the file-internal
44	   placeholder `Pic0.jacobianScheme` for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (the A.3 upstream
45	   has not yet been split out; see §0 below).
46	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.SymmetricPower` (noncomputable def, ~3 LOC) —
47	   the `g`-th symmetric power `Sym^g C` of the curve `C`, as a `k̄`-scheme.
48	   Body gated on iter-178+ `SymmetricPower.lean` substrate (Milne III.3
49	   Proposition 3.1; Mumford 1970 II.7 / III.11 affine-and-glue recipe).
50	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.symmetricPowerAVMap` (noncomputable def, ~5 LOC) —
51	   the symmetrised morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` produced by
52	   `lem:symmetric_product_av_map` from an input `φ : C ⟶ A`.
53	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.symmetricPowerToJacobian` (noncomputable def,
54	   ~5 LOC) — the birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` of
55	   `lem:symmetric_product_to_jacobian`.
56	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.descentThroughBirationalSigma` (theorem,
57	   ~10 LOC) — the descent of `Sym^g φ` to a regular morphism
58	   `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` via Milne Theorem 3.2.
59	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.Pic0.albanese_universal_property` (theorem, ~12 LOC) —
60	   the headline UP theorem (Milne Proposition III.6.1).
61	
62	## File-internal placeholder for `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`
63	
64	The A.3 row of Route A (the identity-component refinement of the Picard
65	scheme together with its degree map) has not yet been split out into a
66	dedicated chapter or `.lean` file at the time of this file-skeleton
67	landing. We supply a **single helper** here: a `Pic0.Bundle` structure
68	bundling the underlying scheme together with its four abelian-variety
69	instances (`GrpObj`, `IsProper`, `Smooth`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`),
70	plus a typed-`sorry` carrier `Pic0.bundle C` and a derived definition
71	`Pic0.jacobianScheme C := (bundle C).scheme`. The four instances on
72	`jacobianScheme C` are derived from the bundle's fields (no extra
73	`sorry`). Once the A.3 chapter materialises this placeholder collapses to
74	a one-line re-export.
75	
76	Helper budget = 1: the single helper carrying a `sorry` is `Pic0.bundle`.
77	
78	## Note on type expressivity
79	
80	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
81	each pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
82	
83	- `abelJacobi C P₀ : C ⟶ jacobianScheme C` — a genuine morphism into the
84	  Pic⁰ scheme (not `𝟙 C` or any tautology).
85	- `SymmetricPower C g : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` — a `k̄`-scheme, not `C`
86	  itself or `𝟙_`.
87	- `symmetricPowerAVMap C g φ : SymmetricPower C g ⟶ A` — a morphism into
88	  the abelian variety `A`.
89	- `symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀ g : SymmetricPower C g ⟶ jacobianScheme C`
90	  — a morphism into Pic⁰.
91	- `descentThroughBirationalSigma` — `∃!` existence-and-uniqueness of
92	  `ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A` satisfying
93	  `symmetricPowerAVMap C g φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀ g ≫ ψ`.
94	- `albanese_universal_property` — `∃!` existence-and-uniqueness of
95	  `ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A` satisfying `φ = abelJacobi C P₀ ≫ ψ`.
96	
97	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
98	(a morphism into the Pic⁰ scheme, a unique factorisation through the
99	Abel–Jacobi map, …); no `Iso.refl _` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` /
100	empty-content `proof_wanted` placeholders are used.
101	
102	## Abelian-variety conventions
103	
104	Following the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`),
105	an **abelian variety over `k̄`** is encoded as an object
106	`A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying the four instances:
107	
108	- `[GrpObj A]` (group-object structure on the over-category),
109	- `[IsProper A.hom]` (complete),
110	- `[Smooth A.hom]` (nonsingular),
111	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` (geometrically irreducible).
112	
113	A **smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over `k̄`** carries:
114	
115	- `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]`,
116	- `[IsProper C.hom]`,
117	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]`.
118	
119	These instances are supplied by `inferInstance` at the call site.
120	
121	## References
122	
123	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AlbaneseUP.tex` (~830 LOC,
124	6 pins). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §III.6 Proposition 6.1, p. 104;
125	§III.3 Proposition 3.1 (symmetric power), p. 94; §III.5 Theorem 5.1(a)
126	(birationality of `f^{(g)}`), p. 101 (`references/abelian-varieties.pdf`).

180	`sorry` pending the A.3 row chapter. iter-178+: replace with the
181	`Bundle` produced by `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean` (A.2.c) after
182	its identity-component / degree-map refinement (A.3) lands. -/
183	noncomputable def bundle : Bundle C := sorry
184	
185	/-- The underlying `k̄`-scheme `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` of a smooth proper
186	geometrically irreducible curve `C` over an algebraically closed field
187	`k̄`. Read off from the `Bundle` placeholder. -/
188	noncomputable def jacobianScheme : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) := (bundle C).scheme
189	
190	/-- Group-object structure on `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. -/
191	noncomputable instance instGrpObj : GrpObj (jacobianScheme C) := (bundle C).grpObj
192	
193	/-- Properness of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` over `k̄`. -/
194	instance instIsProper : IsProper (jacobianScheme C).hom := (bundle C).proper
195	
196	/-- Smoothness of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` over `k̄`. -/
197	instance instSmooth : Smooth (jacobianScheme C).hom := (bundle C).smooth
198	
199	/-- Geometric irreducibility of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` over `k̄`. -/

201	  (bundle C).geomIrred

203	/-! ## §1. The Abel–Jacobi morphism at a marked point
204	
205	The first sub-lemma packages the construction and elementary regularity
206	of the Abel–Jacobi morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` at the basepoint
207	`P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`. On `k̄`-points,
208	`ι_{P₀}(Q) = [𝓞_C(Q − P₀)] ∈ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}(k̄)`; at the basepoint
209	itself `ι_{P₀}(P₀) = η_J`.
210	
211	This is the content gated on A.3 (existence of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` as a
212	representable group scheme); the morphism is the moduli classifier of
213	the rigidified diagonal correspondence
214	`𝓛^{P₀} = 𝓞_{C × C}(Δ − {P₀} × C − C × {P₀})` on `C × C`, taken as a
215	relative degree-zero line bundle on `C × C` over the second factor.
216	
217	Blueprint reference: `lem:abel_jacobi_morphism` (Milne, *Abelian
218	Varieties*, Proposition III.6.1 and Summary III.6.11). -/
219	
220	/-- **The Abel–Jacobi morphism at the marked basepoint `P₀`.**
221	
222	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over an
223	algebraically closed field `k̄` and a marked `k̄`-point
224	`P₀ : 𝟙_ ⟶ C`, the **Abel–Jacobi morphism** is the unique regular
225	morphism `ι_{P₀} : C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` characterised on `T`-points by the
226	formula `(1 × ι_{P₀})^* 𝓜^{P₀} ≈ 𝓛^{P₀}`, where `𝓜^{P₀}` is the
227	Poincaré–Picard correspondence on `C × Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` and
228	`𝓛^{P₀} = 𝓞_{C × C}(Δ − {P₀} × C − C × {P₀})`.
229	
230	iter-178+: body constructed by applying the moduli interpretation of
231	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (`def:pic_scheme` together with the identity-component
232	refinement A.3) to the relative degree-zero line bundle `𝓛^{P₀}` on
233	`C × C → C` along the second projection. Yoneda then produces the
234	unique classifying morphism `C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. The basepoint
235	condition `P₀ ≫ abelJacobi C P₀ = η[Pic⁰_{C/k̄}]` (`lem:abel_jacobi_morphism`)
236	holds because restriction of `𝓛^{P₀}` to `C × {P₀}` is the trivial
237	line bundle `𝓞_C(P₀ − P₀) ≅ 𝓞_C` whose moduli class is `η_J`.
238	
239	For the iter-177 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
240	noncomputable def abelJacobi (P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C) :
241	    C ⟶ jacobianScheme C :=
242	  sorry
243	
244	/-! ## §2. The `g`-th symmetric power `Sym^g C`
245	
246	The `g`-th symmetric power of `C` is the quotient of the `g`-fold
247	Cartesian power `C^g` by the natural permutation action of the
248	symmetric group `S_g`. Mathlib `b80f227` has no formalised symmetric
249	power of a scheme — only `Sym` / `SymmetricPower` for types and
250	modules — so the scheme-theoretic construction must be supplied
251	project-side, following Milne's recipe of §III.3 Proposition 3.1: on
252	each open affine `U = Spec A ⊆ C` form `Spec(A^{⊗ g})^{S_g}`, then glue
253	along the standard open-cover patching (Mumford 1970 §II.7 / §III.11).
254	
255	This sub-build is a non-trivial dependency of A.4.d that the prover
256	must absorb before the body of `symmetricPowerAVMap` /
257	`symmetricPowerToJacobian` closes; the carrier here records the
258	substantive *target* `Sym^g C : Over (Spec k̄)` with a typed `sorry`
259	body, deferring the affine-and-glue construction to iter-178+
260	`Albanese/SymmetricPower.lean`.
261	
262	Blueprint reference: `def:symmetric_power_curve` (Milne, *Abelian
263	Varieties*, §III.3, Proposition 3.1, p. 94). -/
264	
265	/-- **The `g`-th symmetric power `Sym^g C`** of a complete nonsingular
266	curve `C` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, as a `k̄`-scheme.
267	
268	Milne III.3 Proposition 3.1 constructs `Sym^g C` as the quotient of
269	`C^g` by the natural permutation action of the symmetric group `S_g`:
270	on each open affine `U = Spec A ⊆ C`, form `Spec(A^{⊗ g})^{S_g}`, then
271	glue along the standard open-cover patching. The construction comes
272	equipped with a canonical finite surjective symmetric morphism
273	`π : C^g ⟶ Sym^g C` characterised by the universal property that
274	every `S_g`-symmetric morphism `C^g ⟶ T` factors uniquely through `π`.
275	
276	This pin records the substantive *target* scheme `Sym^g C`; the
277	projection `π` and its universal property are recorded as helper API
278	in the iter-178+ `Albanese/SymmetricPower.lean` substrate file.
279	
280	iter-178+: body is the gluing-of-affines `Spec(A^{⊗ g})^{S_g}`
281	construction over an open affine cover of `C`. For the iter-177
282	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`.
283	
284	The signature mentions `C` explicitly (not via the file's `variable`
285	block) so that the declaration's first explicit argument is the curve
286	`C`, matching the call sites `SymmetricPower C g`. -/
287	noncomputable def SymmetricPower
288	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
289	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
290	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
291	    (_g : ℕ) : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
292	  sorry
293	
294	/-! ## §3. The morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A`
295	
296	The second sub-lemma is the elementary fact that the symmetric
297	assignment `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` from `C^g` to `A`
298	descends through `π : C^g ⟶ Sym^g C`. The key point is that the
299	`g`-fold addition morphism `add_A : A^g ⟶ A` is itself `S_g`-symmetric
300	because the underlying group object `A` is commutative as a group
301	scheme; combined with the universal property of `Sym^g C`, this yields
302	the unique factorisation.
303	
304	Blueprint reference: `lem:symmetric_product_av_map` (Milne's proof of
305	III.6.1, p. 104, "Clearly this is symmetric, and so it factors through
306	`C^{(g)}`"). -/
307	
308	/-- **The symmetrised morphism `Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A`.**
309	
310	Let `φ : C ⟶ A` be a morphism into an abelian variety `A` over `k̄`.
311	The composition `add_A ∘ φ^{×g} : C^g ⟶ A^g ⟶ A` sending
312	`(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` is `S_g`-equivariant (where `S_g`
313	permutes the factors of `C^g` and acts trivially on `A`), because
314	addition in a commutative group object is order-independent. By the
315	universal property of `Sym^g C` (`def:symmetric_power_curve`) it
316	factors uniquely as `Sym^g φ ∘ π = add_A ∘ φ^{×g}`.
317	
318	iter-178+: body is the universal-property invocation on the
319	symmetric composition `add_A ∘ φ^{×g}` once the iter-178+
320	`Albanese/SymmetricPower.lean` substrate exposes the projection `π`
321	and its UP. For the iter-177 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
322	noncomputable def symmetricPowerAVMap (g : ℕ)
323	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
324	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
325	    (_φ : C ⟶ A) :
326	    SymmetricPower C g ⟶ A :=
327	  sorry
328	
329	/-! ## §4. The birational morphism `Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`
330	
331	The third sub-lemma is the birational identification of `Sym^g C` with
332	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`. After choosing the basepoint `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, the cycle
333	map `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ [𝓞_C(P₁ + ⋯ + P_g − g P₀)]` defines a regular
334	morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` which is birational by
335	Milne III Theorem 5.1(a) (the proof uses dimension count plus the
336	Riemann–Roch identification of the generic fibre as a single point on
337	a curve of genus `g`).
338	
339	Blueprint reference: `lem:symmetric_product_to_jacobian` (Milne,
340	*Abelian Varieties*, Theorem III.5.1(a), p. 101). -/
341	
342	/-- **The birational morphism `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`.**
343	
344	Given a basepoint `P₀ : 𝟙_ ⟶ C`, the symmetrisation of the Abel–Jacobi
345	morphism `add_J ∘ ι_{P₀}^{×g} : C^g ⟶ J^g ⟶ J` descends through
346	`π : C^g ⟶ Sym^g C` to a regular morphism
347	`f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` sending an effective divisor class
348	`[P₁ + ⋯ + P_g]` to `[𝓞_C(P₁ + ⋯ + P_g − g P₀)] ∈ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}(k̄)`.
349	By Milne III Theorem 5.1(a) this morphism is *birational*: it is
350	dominant with one-dimensional fibres, and its generic fibre is a single
351	`k̄`-point (the linear system `|D|` of a general degree-`g` divisor `D`
352	on a curve of genus `g` is `0`-dimensional by Riemann–Roch).
353	
354	The birationality data (a dense open subset `U ⊆ Sym^g C` mapping
355	isomorphically onto a dense open `V ⊆ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`) is recorded as
356	helper API in the iter-178+ proof body of
357	`descentThroughBirationalSigma`.
358	
359	iter-178+: body is the specialisation of `symmetricPowerAVMap` to
360	`A := jacobianScheme C` and `φ := abelJacobi C P₀`. For the iter-177
361	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
362	noncomputable def symmetricPowerToJacobian
363	    (_P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C) (g : ℕ) :
364	    SymmetricPower C g ⟶ jacobianScheme C :=
365	  sorry
366	
367	/-! ## §5. Descent through the birational quotient
368	
369	The final auxiliary lemma packages the two-step descent that promotes
370	`Sym^g φ : Sym^g C ⟶ A` (after passing through the birational morphism
371	`f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`) to a regular morphism
372	`ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`. The descent is a rational map
373	`Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A` on the dense open of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` on which
374	`f^{(g)}` is an isomorphism, then promoted to a regular morphism via
375	Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (the sibling `Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`).
376	This is the unique step of the proof that consumes A.4.c.
377	
378	Blueprint reference: `lem:descent_through_birational_sigma` (Milne's
379	proof of III.6.1, p. 104, "It therefore defines a rational map
380	`ψ : J → A`, which (I 3.2) shows to be a regular map"). -/
381	
382	/-- **Descent of `Sym^g φ` to a regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`.**
383	
384	There is a *unique* regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` such that
385	`symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀ (genus C) ≫ ψ`
386	(equivalently, `ψ` is the unique regular extension of the rational map
387	`Sym^g φ ∘ (f^{(g)})^{-1} : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`).
388	
389	The rational map is constructed on the dense open `V ⊆ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}`
390	where `f^{(g)}` is an isomorphism, then promoted to a regular morphism
391	on all of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` via Milne's Theorem I.3.2 (sibling
392	`Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` in
393	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`, A.4.c). Uniqueness is the
394	standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle: two regular
395	morphisms `Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` agreeing on a dense open are equal.
396	
397	iter-178+: body invokes `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` on the
398	rational map produced by inverting `f^{(g)}` on its dense open of
399	isomorphism. For the iter-177 file-skeleton the body is a typed
400	`sorry`. -/
401	theorem descentThroughBirationalSigma
402	    (P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
403	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
404	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
405	    (φ : C ⟶ A) (_hφ : P0 ≫ φ = η[A]) :
406	    ∃! (ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A),
407	      symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ
408	        = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P0 (genus C) ≫ ψ := by
409	  sorry
410	
411	/-! ## §5.5. Albanese ⇔ symmetric-power equation (connecting biconditional)
412	
413	The iter-182 structural decomposition extracts the precise *connecting
414	identity* between the Albanese-form factorisation
415	`φ = abelJacobi C P₀ ≫ ψ` and the symmetric-power-form factorisation
416	`symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P₀
417	(genus C) ≫ ψ` as a named typed-`sorry` biconditional helper. With this
418	biconditional in hand the body of `albanese_universal_property` reduces
419	to sorry-free assembly from `descentThroughBirationalSigma`. -/
420	
421	/-- **Connecting biconditional: Albanese-form equation ⇔ symmetric-power-form
422	equation.**
423	
424	For every candidate descent morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`, the Albanese-form
425	factorisation `φ = abelJacobi C P₀ ≫ ψ` holds iff the symmetric-power-form
426	factorisation `symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ = symmetricPowerToJacobian
427	C P₀ (genus C) ≫ ψ` holds. The forward direction precomposes both sides of
428	the Albanese equation with the symmetric `g`-fold sum, then descends through
429	`π : C^g ⟶ Sym^g C` and uses the additivity of `ψ` (a homomorphism of group
430	schemes). The reverse direction restricts the symmetric-power-form equation
431	along the diagonal embedding `Q ↦ (Q, P₀, …, P₀)` and uses
432	`φ(P₀) = η_A`.
433	
434	iter-200+: body uses the `Sym^g` projection π and its UP from
435	`Albanese/SymmetricPower.lean` (substrate file not yet opened),
436	together with the explicit Milne III §6 identification of `ι_{P₀}` with
437	the composite of `Q ↦ Q + (g - 1) P₀` followed by `f^{(g)}`. For the
438	iter-182 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry` — but the named
439	substantive helper exposes the precise connecting identity, allowing
440	`albanese_universal_property` to be assembled sorry-free from it. -/
441	theorem albanese_eq_iff_symmetricPower_eq
442	    (P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
443	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
444	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
445	    (φ : C ⟶ A) (_hφ : P0 ≫ φ = η[A]) :
446	    ∀ (ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A),
447	      (φ = abelJacobi C P0 ≫ ψ) ↔
448	      (symmetricPowerAVMap C (genus C) φ
449	        = symmetricPowerToJacobian C P0 (genus C) ≫ ψ) :=
450	  sorry
451	
452	/-! ## §6. The Albanese universal property — headline theorem
453	
454	The headline result of the chapter: for every pointed morphism
455	`φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety `A` with `φ(P₀) = η_A`, there
456	exists a unique regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` such that
457	`φ = ψ ∘ ι_{P₀}`. The proof follows Milne Proposition III.6.1
458	verbatim, using the four sub-lemmas above plus Milne Theorem I.3.2
459	(`extend_to_av`) and Corollary I.1.2 (a `thm:rigidity_lemma`
460	consequence).
461	
462	Blueprint reference: `thm:albanese_universal_property` (Milne,
463	*Abelian Varieties*, Proposition III.6.1, p. 104). -/
464	
465	/-- **Albanese universal property of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` — Milne III §6
466	Proposition 6.1.**
467	
468	For a complete nonsingular curve `C` of genus `g > 0` over an
469	algebraically closed field `k̄`, a marked `k̄`-point `P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`, and
470	every pointed morphism `φ : C ⟶ A` into an abelian variety `A` with
471	`φ(P₀) = η_A`, there exists a *unique* regular morphism
472	`ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` such that `φ = ι_{P₀} ≫ ψ`.
473	
474	Proof sketch (Milne III.6.1):
475	1. **Symmetrisation.** `(P₁,…,P_g) ↦ φ(P₁) + ⋯ + φ(P_g)` is
476	   `S_g`-symmetric (`A` commutative), so factors through
477	   `Sym^g C` as `Sym^g φ` (`lem:symmetric_product_av_map`).
478	2. **Descent to a regular morphism on `J`.** The birational morphism
479	   `f^{(g)} : Sym^g C ⟶ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (`lem:symmetric_product_to_jacobian`)
480	   exhibits `Sym^g φ ∘ (f^{(g)})^{-1}` as a rational map
481	   `Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⇢ A`, which by Milne Theorem I.3.2 extends uniquely to
482	   a regular morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A`
483	   (`lem:descent_through_birational_sigma`).
484	3. **Factorisation `ψ ∘ ι_{P₀} = φ`.** Use Milne's identification of
485	   `ι_{P₀}` with the composite `Q ↦ Q + (g − 1) P₀` followed by
486	   `f^{(g)}`; together with `φ(P₀) = η_A` and the additive structure
487	   of `A`, this gives `ψ(ι_{P₀}(Q)) = φ(Q)` on `k̄`-points, hence
488	   globally by the reduced-and-separated agreement principle.
489	4. **Homomorphism property.** `ψ` sends `η_J = ι_{P₀}(P₀)` to
490	   `φ(P₀) = η_A`, so by Milne Corollary I.1.2 (a `thm:rigidity_lemma`
491	   consequence) it is a homomorphism of group schemes.
492	5. **Uniqueness.** Two homomorphisms `Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` with the same
493	   restriction to `ι_{P₀}(C)` agree on the `g`-fold sum
494	   `ι_{P₀}(C) + ⋯ + ι_{P₀}(C) = Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` (since `f^{(g)}` is
495	   surjective), hence are equal.
496	
497	The iter-177 file-skeleton encodes only the existence-and-uniqueness
498	of the factoring morphism `ψ : Pic⁰_{C/k̄} ⟶ A` (matching the
499	project's `IsAlbanese` precedent in `Jacobian.lean`); the
500	group-homomorphism property is implicit in the construction. The body
501	consumes its four sub-lemma exports plus
502	`Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` (A.4.c) and Milne Corollary I.1.2
503	(registered in `chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
504	
505	iter-182 update: structural decomposition lands the body as
506	sorry-free assembly from `descentThroughBirationalSigma` (§5)
507	combined with the named typed-`sorry` biconditional helper
508	`albanese_eq_iff_symmetricPower_eq` (§5.5). The remaining substantive
509	gaps (substrate files `Pic⁰` representability, `Sym^g C` construction,
510	`extend_to_av` rational-map extension, and the connecting biconditional
511	body itself) remain blocking and are deferred to iter-200+. -/
512	theorem albanese_universal_property
513	    (_hg : 0 < genus C)
514	    (P0 : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
515	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
516	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
517	    (φ : C ⟶ A) (_hφ : P0 ≫ φ = η[A]) :
518	    ∃! (ψ : jacobianScheme C ⟶ A), φ = abelJacobi C P0 ≫ ψ := by
519	  -- Structural decomposition (iter-182): reduce to the symmetric-power
520	  -- descent `descentThroughBirationalSigma` plus the connecting
521	  -- biconditional `albanese_eq_iff_symmetricPower_eq`. The remaining
522	  -- substantive gap is the biconditional helper (named typed-sorry); the
523	  -- proof body below is sorry-free assembly.
524	  have key := albanese_eq_iff_symmetricPower_eq C P0 φ _hφ
525	  obtain ⟨ψ, hψ_sym, huniq_sym⟩ := descentThroughBirationalSigma C P0 φ _hφ
526	  exact ⟨ψ, (key ψ).mpr hψ_sym,
527	    fun ψ' hψ' => huniq_sym ψ' ((key ψ').mp hψ')⟩
528	
529	end Pic0
530	
531	end AlgebraicGeometry

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK
8	
9	/-!
10	# Genus of a smooth proper curve
11	
12	The genus of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field.
13	
14	## Status (closed iteration 011 — `genus`)
15	
16	The iter-011 refactor added the `noncomputable` modifier (user-authorised
17	`2026-05-07`) and an import of `AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK`
18	so the body uses the project's `ModuleCat k`-flavoured cohomology
19	`Scheme.HModule` (iter-009) of the structure sheaf
20	`Scheme.toModuleKSheaf` (iter-006). The body is the probe-confirmed
21	one-liner
22	
23	    Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
24	
25	which is the honest mathematical definition `dim_k H^1(C, O_C)`.
26	
27	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus.tex` for the informal proof sketch and
28	`.archon/STRATEGY.md` for the multi-phase build-out plan.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	/-- The genus of a smooth proper curve. -/
40	noncomputable def genus {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
41	    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
42	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : ℕ :=
43	  Module.finrank k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 1)
44	
45	end AlgebraicGeometry

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian
7	
8	/-!
9	# The Abel-Jacobi map
10	
11	The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian, and the universal property
12	of the Jacobian as the Albanese variety.
13	
14	## Status (iteration 073 — Phase E closes by reduction)
15	
16	After the iter-073 refactor of `Jacobian C := (jacobianWitness C).J` (no genus-0 dite),
17	all three protected declarations reduce uniformly to the Albanese universal property
18	carried by `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P : IsAlbanese C P (jacobianWitness C).J`:
19	
20	* `ofCurve P := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve`.
21	* `comp_ofCurve P := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve`.
22	* `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp P f hf := ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf`.
23	
24	The genus-0 rigidity content previously sitting at the existence step of
25	`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp` (classical rigidity `Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`) is now absorbed
26	into the single deferred existence claim `nonempty_jacobianWitness` in
27	`Jacobian.lean`: the witness's `isAlbaneseFor P` field must verify the Albanese property
28	for genus-0 curves too, which is precisely the rigidity content.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
38	
39	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
40	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
41	  [IsProper C.hom]
42	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
43	
44	namespace Jacobian
45	
46	variable {C} in
47	-- data
48	/-- The Abel-Jacobi map from a smooth, proper curve to its Jacobian associated
49	to a `k`-rational point of `C`. Projects the Albanese universal morphism carried
50	by the witness `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. -/
51	noncomputable def ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) : C ⟶ Jacobian C := by
52	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
53	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
54	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
55	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
56	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).ofCurve
57	
58	variable {C} in
59	/-- The Abel-Jacobi map sends the `k`-rational point `P` to `0`, where `0` (denoted by `η` below) is
60	the neutral element of the group scheme `Jacobian C`. Projects the pointed-property
61	of the Albanese witness `(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. -/
62	lemma comp_ofCurve (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C) :
63	    P ≫ ofCurve P = η[Jacobian C] := by
64	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
65	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
66	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
67	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
68	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).comp_ofCurve
69	
70	variable {C} in
71	/--
72	The universal property of the Jacobian variety: For any abelian variety `A`,
73	any morphism `f : C ⟶ A` such that `f(P) = 0` factors uniquely through the
74	Jacobian of `C`.
75	In other words, `Jacobian C` is the Albanese variety of `C`.
76	
77	The existence-and-uniqueness data is projected unconditionally from the witness
78	`(jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P`. The genus-0 rigidity content (`Hom(ℙ¹, A) = A(k)`,
79	Mumford §4) is absorbed into the deferred existence claim `nonempty_jacobianWitness`,
80	since the witness must verify the Albanese property for genus-0 curves too.
81	-/
82	theorem exists_unique_ofCurve_comp (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
83	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [Smooth A.hom] [IsProper A.hom] [GrpObj A]
84	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
85	    ∃! (g : Jacobian C ⟶ A), f = ofCurve P ≫ g := by
86	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
87	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).proper
88	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).smooth
89	  letI := (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
90	  exact ((jacobianWitness C).isAlbaneseFor P).exists_unique_ofCurve_comp f hf
91	
92	end Jacobian
93	
94	end AlgebraicGeometry
95

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian
7	
8	/-!
9	# Rigidity for morphisms of schemes (scheme-level form)
10	
11	A self-contained Phase E helper: two morphisms `g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ Y` between
12	schemes over `Spec k` whose restrictions to a non-empty open `U ⊆ X` agree
13	as scheme morphisms — for `X` reduced and geometrically irreducible over
14	`Spec k` and `Y` separated over `Spec k` — agree everywhere. Independent of
15	`AlgebraicGeometry.Jacobian C` and provable from current Mathlib alone
16	(Stacks 0BFD plus standard separatedness/irreducibility lemmas).
17	
18	The historical motivation is Mumford's §4 rigidity for morphisms of group
19	schemes; the formalized statement is the minimal-hypothesis scheme-level
20	form, which the Mumford group-scheme rigidity follows from by specialization.
21	
22	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Rigidity.tex`.
23	
24	## Status
25	
26	`Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen` — rigidity of morphisms agreeing as scheme
27	maps on a non-empty open of a reduced (geometrically) irreducible source
28	with separated target — is closed. See the "Hypothesis history" block below
29	for the discussion of why the original point-wise hypothesis was
30	strengthened to scheme-level equality on `U`.
31	-/
32	
33	set_option autoImplicit false
34	
35	universe u
36	
37	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
38	
39	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
40	
41	variable {k : Type u} [Field k]
42	
43	/-!
44	## Hypothesis history
45	
46	### Scheme-level hypothesis (iter 003)
47	
48	The original scaffold from iter 002 had hypothesis
49	`h : ∀ x ∈ U, g₁.left.base x = g₂.left.base x`
50	(point-wise topological equality on `U`). This is too weak: in characteristic
51	`p`, the absolute Frobenius `F : X → X` of an elliptic curve over `𝔽_p` agrees
52	topologically with the identity on all of `X` (the identity on the underlying
53	topological space) but `F ≠ id` as scheme morphisms — they differ on stalks
54	(`a ↦ a` vs `a ↦ a^p`). Hence the symmetric-form rigidity statement
55	`g₁.base = g₂.base on U ⇒ g₁ = g₂` is mathematically false.
56	
57	We strengthen the hypothesis to scheme-level equality on `U`:
58	`h : U.ι ≫ g₁.left = U.ι ≫ g₂.left`. This is the version that is actually
59	needed at the use site (uniqueness half of `exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`):
60	the application produces *scheme-level* agreement on a non-empty open
61	(through the symmetric-power surjection), not merely topological agreement.
62	
63	We also add `[IsReduced X.left]` because Mathlib `b80f227` lacks the lemma
64	`Smooth ⇒ IsReduced` for schemes over a field (the closest is the perfect-base
65	case `Scheme.Hom.dense_smoothLocus_of_perfectField`). This is a small Mathlib
66	gap that the downstream consumer must satisfy via the smoothness + irreducibility
67	of the curve / Jacobian (e.g. via `IsIntegral`).
68	
69	### Unused-hypothesis cleanup (iter 125)
70	
71	Eight decorative hypotheses (`{n m : ℕ}`, `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension n X.hom]`,
72	`[IsProper X.hom]`, `[GrpObj X]`, `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension m Y.hom]`,
73	`[GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]`, `[GrpObj Y]`, and the strengthening of
74	`[IsSeparated Y.hom]` to `[IsProper Y.hom]`) were dropped per
75	`analogies/rigidity-refactor.md`. The declaration was also renamed from
76	`GrpObj.eq_of_eqOnOpen` to `Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen`, mirroring
77	Mathlib's `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'` naming idiom
78	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Separated:319–322`).
79	-/
80	
81	/-- Rigidity for morphisms of schemes (scheme-level form): two morphisms
82	`g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ Y` between schemes over `Spec k` whose restrictions to a
83	non-empty open `U ⊆ X` agree as scheme morphisms — for `X` reduced and
84	geometrically irreducible over `Spec k` and `Y` separated over `Spec k` —
85	agree everywhere.
86	
87	This is the `Over (Spec (.of k))`-bundled specialization of Mathlib's
88	`ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'`. The Mumford group-scheme rigidity
89	statement (a morphism vanishing on a non-empty open is zero) is
90	interderivable with this form using the group law on the target. -/
91	theorem Scheme.Over.ext_of_eqOnOpen
92	    {X Y : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    [IsSeparated Y.hom]
94	    [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
95	    [IsReduced X.left]
96	    (g₁ g₂ : X ⟶ Y) (U : X.left.Opens) (hU : (U : Set X.left).Nonempty)
97	    (h : (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) ≫ g₁.left =
98	      (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) ≫ g₂.left) :
99	    g₁ = g₂ := by
100	  -- (1) Provide the separatedness instance under the `OverClass.fromOver`
101	  --     form `Y.left ↘ Spec (.of k)` so that the downstream typeclass
102	  --     search at `ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated'` finds it.
103	  haveI : IsSeparated (Y.left ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)) :=
104	    (‹IsSeparated Y.hom› : IsSeparated Y.hom)
105	  -- (2) `X.left` is irreducible: `Spec k` is a single point (`Unique`,
106	  --     hence `Subsingleton + Nonempty`), and `GeometricallyIrreducible`
107	  --     over a subsingleton base implies `IrreducibleSpace`.
108	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace X.left :=
109	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton X.hom
110	  -- (3) `U.ι : U.toScheme ⟶ X.left` is dominant: a non-empty open in an
111	  --     irreducible space is dense, and a dense open immersion is dominant.
112	  haveI : IsDominant (U.ι : (U : X.left.Opens).toScheme ⟶ X.left) :=
113	    Scheme.PartialMap.Opens.isDominant_ι (IsOpen.dense U.isOpen hU)
114	  -- (4) Promote `g₁ = g₂` from the underlying scheme equality.
115	  --     The `Scheme.Over` and `IsOver` instances for `X.left`, `Y.left`,
116	  --     `g₁.left`, `g₂.left` over `Spec (.of k)` are inferred globally
117	  --     from the `Over (Spec (.of k))` category structure
118	  --     (`OverClass.fromOver`).
119	  refine Over.OverMorphism.ext ?_
120	  exact ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated' (S := Spec (.of k))
121	    (X := X.left) (Y := Y.left) (f := g₁.left) (g := g₂.left) U.ι h
122	
123	end AlgebraicGeometry
124

6	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Kaehler.Basic
7	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Unramified.Basic
8	import Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Basic
9	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme
10	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.FinitePresentation
11	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth
12	import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Differentials.Presheaf
13	import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Presheaf
14	
15	/-! # Relative Kähler differentials for schemes (presheaf form)
16	
17	This file constructs the **presheaf** of relative Kähler differentials
18	`Ω_{X/S}` of a morphism of schemes `f : X → S` and states the
19	characterisation of smoothness in terms of pointwise local freeness of
20	`Ω` on affine opens.
21	
22	The construction builds on Mathlib's ring-theoretic `KaehlerDifferential`
23	and the presheaf-of-modules differential construction
24	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`.
25	
26	## References
27	
28	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex`.
29	-/
30	
31	set_option autoImplicit false
32	
33	universe u
34	
35	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
38	
39	variable {X S : Scheme.{u}}
40	
41	/-- The relative cotangent **presheaf** of a morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ S`.
42	
43	On each open `U ⊆ X`, the sections are the Kähler differential module of
44	the ring map `O_S(f(U)) → O_X(U)` induced by `f`. More precisely, we use
45	the inverse-image presheaf `f⁻¹ O_S` on `X` and the canonical map to `O_X`;
46	the Kähler differential construction then gives a presheaf of `O_X`-modules.
47	
48	Mathlib leverage: `TopCat.Presheaf.pullback` for `f⁻¹`,
49	`PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials'`
50	for the presheaf of Kähler differentials. -/
51	noncomputable def relativeDifferentialsPresheaf (f : X ⟶ S) : X.PresheafOfModules :=
52	  let φ' := ((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm
53	    f.c
54	  PresheafOfModules.DifferentialsConstruction.relativeDifferentials' φ'
55	
56	/-- Localisation-compatibility identity: on each open `V ⊆ X`, the sections
57	of `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f` over `V` are *definitionally* the
58	Kähler differential module of the ring map
59	`(f⁻¹ O_S)(V) → O_X(V)`. -/
60	theorem relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_obj_kaehler (f : X ⟶ S)
61	    (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
62	    ((relativeDifferentialsPresheaf f).presheaf.obj V : Type _) =
63	      CommRingCat.KaehlerDifferential
64	        (((TopCat.Presheaf.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction CommRingCat
65	          f.base).homEquiv _ _).symm f.c |>.app V) :=
66	  rfl
67	
68	/-- The Kähler module of a localization is subsingleton.
69	This is project-local Lemma `lem:kaehler_localization_subsingleton`. -/
70	theorem kaehler_localization_subsingleton
71	    {A L : Type u} [CommRing A] [CommRing L] [Algebra A L]
72	    (M : Submonoid A) [IsLocalization M L] :
73	    Subsingleton (Ω[L⁄A]) :=
74	  letI : Algebra.FormallyUnramified A L :=
75	    Algebra.FormallyUnramified.of_isLocalization (R := A) (Rₘ := L) M
76	  Algebra.FormallyUnramified.subsingleton_kaehlerDifferential
77	
78	/-- Tower-cancellation `LinearEquiv` for the Kähler module under a localization:
79	if `A → L` is a localization at `M ⊆ A` and `L → B` is a ring map forming a
80	scalar tower `A → L → B`, then the canonical map `Ω[B/A] → Ω[B/L]` is a
81	`B`-linear equivalence.
82	
83	This is project-local Lemma `lem:kaehler_quotient_localization_iso` and the most
84	extractable Mathlib contribution candidate of milestone M1 (candidate name
85	`KaehlerDifferential.equivOfFormallyUnramified`). -/
86	noncomputable def kaehler_quotient_localization_iso
87	    {A L B : Type u} [CommRing A] [CommRing L] [CommRing B]
88	    [Algebra A L] [Algebra A B] [Algebra L B] [IsScalarTower A L B]
89	    (M : Submonoid A) [IsLocalization M L] :
90	    Ω[B⁄A] ≃ₗ[B] Ω[B⁄L] := by
91	  haveI : Subsingleton (Ω[L⁄A]) := kaehler_localization_subsingleton M
92	  refine LinearEquiv.ofBijective (KaehlerDifferential.map A L B B) ⟨?_, ?_⟩
93	  · rw [injective_iff_map_eq_zero]
94	    intro x hx
95	    obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ :=
96	      (KaehlerDifferential.exact_mapBaseChange_map A L B x).mp hx
97	    -- y : B ⊗[L] Ω[L⁄A]; since Ω[L⁄A] is subsingleton, the tensor product is too
98	    haveI : Subsingleton (TensorProduct L B (Ω[L⁄A])) := by
99	      refine ⟨fun a b => ?_⟩
100	      have hzero : ∀ z : TensorProduct L B (Ω[L⁄A]), z = 0 := by
101	        intro z
102	        induction z with
103	        | zero => rfl
104	        | tmul x y =>
105	          rw [show y = (0 : Ω[L⁄A]) from Subsingleton.elim _ _, TensorProduct.tmul_zero]
106	        | add x y hx hy => rw [hx, hy, add_zero]
107	      rw [hzero a, hzero b]
108	    rw [Subsingleton.elim y 0, map_zero]
109	  · exact KaehlerDifferential.map_surjective _ _ _
110	
111	/-- Forward direction of the Jacobian criterion (algebra-Kähler form).
112	If `f : X → S` is smooth of relative dimension `n`, then for every
113	point `x ∈ X` there exist affine opens `U ⊆ S` and `V ⊆ X` with
114	`f V ⊆ U` and `x ∈ V`, on which the Kähler differential module
115	`Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]` (over the appLE algebra structure) is a free
116	module of rank `n` over `Γ(X, V)`.
117	
118	The reverse direction (locally free Ω of rank `n` implies smooth of
119	relative dimension `n`) is **mathematically false** as stated without
120	additional deformation-theoretic input (vanishing of
121	`Algebra.H1Cotangent A B` on each chart); see the chapter
122	`Differentials.tex` for the counterexample (`Spec k → Spec k[t]`,
123	`t ↦ 0`) and the converse-direction disclosure. -/
124	theorem smooth_locally_free_omega {n : ℕ} (f : X ⟶ S)
125	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension n f] :
126	    ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : S.Opens) (V : X.Opens) (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
127	        x ∈ V ∧ IsAffineOpen U ∧ IsAffineOpen V ∧
128	          letI : Algebra Γ(S, U) Γ(X, V) :=
129	            (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e).hom.toAlgebra
130	          (Module.Free Γ(X, V) (Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]) ∧
131	           Module.rank Γ(X, V) (Ω[Γ(X, V) ⁄ Γ(S, U)]) = n) := by
132	  intro x
133	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hxV, e, hRing⟩ :=
134	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension.exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension (n := n) (f := f) x
135	  refine ⟨U, V, e, hxV, hU, hV, ?_, ?_⟩ <;>
136	    · algebraize [CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appLE f U V e)]
137	      haveI : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth Γ(S, U) Γ(X, V) :=
138	        Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth n
139	      haveI : Nonempty V := ⟨⟨x, hxV⟩⟩
140	      first
141	      | exact Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
142	      | exact Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential n
143	
144	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
145

6	import Mathlib
7	
8	/-!
9	# FGA representability of the Picard scheme (A.2.c)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.c** assembly file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
12	project's positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It wires the
13	Quot-scheme engine (A.2.b, sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`) and the
14	étale-sheafified relative Picard functor (A.1.c, sibling
15	`Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`) into the FGA representability statement for
16	`Pic_{C/k}`, following Kleiman, "The Picard scheme", §4 (cf. FGA Explained
17	Ch. 9 §9.4; arXiv:math/0504020).
18	
19	## Status (iter-176 Lane I file-skeleton)
20	
21	This file is the **iter-176 Lane I** file-skeleton (re-dispatch of the iter-175
22	Lane I that died to the Anthropic session-limit reset window without ever
23	calling `Write`). Each of the five blueprint-pinned declarations carries the
24	*intended* substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
25	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex`) with a `sorry`
26	body. The bodies are iter-177+ work, gated on the sibling chapters
27	`Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean` (A.1.c) and `Picard/QuotScheme.lean` (A.2.b)
28	landing their pinned declarations.
29	
30	The 5 pinned declarations are:
31	
32	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme` (def, ~5 LOC) — the **Picard scheme**
33	   `Pic_{C/k}` as an object of `Over (Spec k)`, the witness extracted from
34	   the `Functor.RepresentableBy` produced by `representable`.
35	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.abelMap` (def, ~6 LOC) — the
36	   **Abel map** `A_{C/k} : Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` as a natural
37	   transformation of presheaves on `(Sch/k)^op`.
38	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
39	   — the Altman–Kleiman **smooth-proper quotient lemma** (Kleiman §4
40	   Lem. `lm:qt`) packaging the descent from a flat-and-proper equivalence
41	   relation `R ⊆ Z ×_k Z` on a quasi-projective `k`-scheme `Z` to a
42	   representing quotient `Q`.
43	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.representable` (theorem, ~12 LOC) —
44	   the **FGA representability theorem** (Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main`,
45	   specialised via Cor. `cor:algsch` to `S = Spec k` and `X = C` a smooth
46	   proper geometrically integral curve): the étale-sheafified relative
47	   Picard functor is represented by `PicScheme`.
48	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.groupSchemeStructure` (instance,
49	   ~6 LOC) — the **group-scheme structure** on `PicScheme`: a `GrpObj`
50	   instance obtained by Yoneda transport of the abelian-group structure
51	   on `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`.
52	
53	## Note on type expressivity
54	
55	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
56	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
57	
58	- `PicScheme C : Over (Spec (.of k))` — a scheme over `k`, not a
59	  tautological `Spec k`.
60	- `abelMap C : divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C` — a genuine natural
61	  transformation of presheaves, not `𝟙 _` (it sends a relative effective
62	  divisor to its associated invertible sheaf).
63	- `smoothProperQuotient` carries the four Kleiman §4 Lem. `lm:qt`
64	  hypotheses (Z represented, R representable, smooth+proper first
65	  projection, étale surjection) and concludes `P.IsRepresentable`. The
66	  hypotheses are not vacuous: any one of (i)–(iv) can fail for a
67	  non-quotient morphism of presheaves.
68	- `representable C : (picSharp C).RepresentableBy (PicScheme C)` — the
69	  substantive Yoneda-bijection structure between `Hom(T, PicScheme C)` and
70	  `picSharp C |_T`.
71	- `groupSchemeStructure C : GrpObj (PicScheme C)` — a genuine group-object
72	  structure, with multiplication / inverse / unit morphisms satisfying the
73	  group axioms.
74	
75	The two file-internal auxiliary defs `picSharp` and `divFunctor` (placeholders

154	/-! ## §1. The Picard scheme
155	
156	The Picard scheme `Pic_{C/k}` of a smooth proper geometrically integral curve
157	`C/k` is the `k`-scheme representing the étale-sheafified relative Picard
158	functor `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`. It is supplied by Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main`
159	(specialised to `S = Spec k`, `X = C` via Cor. `cor:algsch`); the
160	representability theorem itself is the next declaration (`representable`),
161	and `PicScheme` is the witness scheme extracted from it.
162	
163	The scheme is separated, locally of finite type over `k`, and a disjoint
164	union of open quasi-projective `k`-subschemes; the abelian-group-scheme
165	structure is the content of `groupSchemeStructure`.
166	
167	Blueprint reference: `def:pic_scheme` (Kleiman §4 Def. `df:Psch`). -/
168	
169	/-- The **Picard scheme** `Pic_{C/k}` of a smooth proper geometrically
170	integral curve `C/k`.

173	substantive identity "this is the scheme representing `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`"
174	via the `RepresentableBy` witness produced by
175	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.representable`. The structural
176	properties (separated, locally of finite type, disjoint union of open
177	quasi-projectives) live on the underlying scheme via the standard
178	instances of `Over (Spec (.of k))`.
179	
180	iter-177+: the body extracts the representing object from

182	`(representable C).choose` modulo a `Functor.RepresentableBy` witness; or

189	  sorry

193	/-! ## §2. The Abel map — line-bundle / Quot correspondence
194	
195	The bridge from line bundles on `C ×_k T` to the Quot scheme is the **Abel
196	map**: a relative effective divisor `D ⊆ C ×_k T` over `T` (an `S`-flat
197	closed subscheme whose ideal is invertible) determines a `T`-point of the
198	Hilbert functor `Hilb_{C/k}(T)` (hence of `Div_{C/k}(T)`), and the dual
199	ideal sheaf `O_{C ×_k T}(D) := I_D⁻¹` is an invertible sheaf on the
200	relative curve, representing a class in `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}(T)`.
201	
202	The assignment `D ↦ [O_{C ×_k T}(D)]` is functorial in `T` (pullback of an
203	ideal sheaf along `id_C ×_k g : C ×_k T' ⟶ C ×_k T` takes the ideal of `D`
204	to the ideal of `D_{T'}`, since `D` is flat over `T` and hence
205	`D_{T'} = D ×_T T'` is again a relative effective divisor by Kleiman §3
206	Lem. `lm:ctn`).
207	
208	Blueprint reference: `lem:line_bundle_quot_correspondence` (Kleiman §3
209	Def. `dfn:Abel` + Thm. `th:repDiv`). -/
210	
211	/-- The **Abel map** `A_{C/k} : Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`, sending a
212	relative effective Cartier divisor `D ⊆ C ×_k T` over `T` to its associated
213	invertible sheaf `[O_{C ×_k T}(D)] = [I_D⁻¹]`.
214	
215	A natural transformation of presheaves on `(Sch/k)^op`. The source
216	`divFunctor C` is the relative-divisor functor (the open subfunctor of the
217	Hilbert/Quot scheme cut out by the effective-divisor criterion); the target
218	`picSharp C` is the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor.
219	
220	iter-177+: the body constructs the natural transformation component-wise:
221	for each test `T`, send a relative effective Cartier divisor `D` on
222	`C ×_k T` to the dual of its ideal sheaf, then take the étale-sheafified
223	class. Naturality is the functoriality of the dual-of-ideal-sheaf
224	construction under pullback (Stacks 01HG + Kleiman §3 Lem. `lm:ctn`).
225	For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
226	noncomputable def abelMap {k : Type u} [Field k]
227	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
228	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
229	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
230	    divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C :=
231	  sorry
232	
233	/-! ## §3. The smooth-proper quotient lemma — Altman–Kleiman descent
234	
235	The structural lemma underlying Step 4 of Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main` is the
236	Altman–Kleiman **smooth-proper quotient lemma**: given a surjection
237	`α : Z ⟶ P` of étale sheaves with `R := Z ×_P Z` representable, `Z`
238	quasi-projective, and the first projection `R ⟶ Z` smooth and proper,
239	the quotient `P` is representable by a quasi-projective scheme.
240	
241	Kleiman's proof:
242	1. `R ↪ Z ×_k Z` is a flat (from (iv)) and proper monomorphism, hence a
243	   closed immersion by EGA IV 8.11.5.
244	2. By the theory of flat-and-proper effective equivalence relations on
245	   quasi-projective schemes (Altman–Kleiman), there exist a
246	   quasi-projective scheme `Q` and a faithfully flat, projective map
247	   `Z ⟶ Q` with `R = Z ×_Q Z`. The map `Z ⟶ Q` is smooth (its fibers
248	   coincide with those of `R ⟶ Z`, which are smooth).
249	3. In the category of étale sheaves, both `Q` and `P` are coequalisers of
250	   `R ⇒ Z`; uniqueness of the coequaliser gives `Q ≃ P`.
251	
252	Blueprint reference: `lem:smooth_proper_quotient` (Kleiman §4 Lem.
253	`lm:qt`). -/
254	
255	/-- **The smooth-proper quotient lemma — Altman–Kleiman descent of an
256	étale-sheaf surjection.**
257	
258	Let `Z, P : (Sch/k)^op ⥤ Type u` be presheaves on `(Sch/k)^op`, and let
259	`α : Z ⟶ P` be a natural transformation. Assume:
260	1. `Z` is representable by a `k`-scheme `Y` (encoded by the
261	   `Z.RepresentableBy Y` hypothesis),
262	2. the pullback presheaf `R := Z ×_P Z` is representable by a `k`-scheme `R`
263	   (encoded by the `(Limits.pullback α α).RepresentableBy R` hypothesis),
264	3. the first projection `π : R ⟶ Y` (after representation) is smooth and
265	   proper (encoded by the typeclass hypotheses `[Smooth π.left]` and
266	   `[IsProper π.left]`),
267	4. `α` is an étale surjection (encoded as the lift property: every
268	   `T`-point `p ∈ P(T)` lifts to a `T'`-point `z ∈ Z(T')` for some test
269	   morphism `T ⟶ T'`).
270	
271	Then `P` is representable (by a quasi-projective `k`-scheme `Q`).
272	
273	iter-177+: the body builds `Q` via the Altman–Kleiman effective quotient
274	of the flat-and-proper equivalence relation `R ↪ Y ×_k Y`, then exhibits
275	the coequaliser-uniqueness in the category of étale sheaves to identify
276	`Q` with the representing object of `P`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton
277	the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
278	theorem smoothProperQuotient {k : Type u} [Field k]
279	    {Z P : (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u}
280	    (α : Z ⟶ P)
281	    (Y : Over (Spec (.of k)))
282	    (_hZ : Z.RepresentableBy Y)
283	    (R : Over (Spec (.of k)))
284	    (_hR : (Limits.pullback α α).RepresentableBy R)
285	    (π : R ⟶ Y)
286	    [Smooth π.left] [IsProper π.left]
287	    (_hα : ∀ ⦃T : (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ⦄ (p : P.obj T),
288	        ∃ (T' : (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ) (e : T ⟶ T') (z : Z.obj T'),
289	          α.app T' z = P.map e p) :
290	    P.IsRepresentable := by
291	  sorry
292	
293	/-! ## §4. The FGA representability theorem
294	
295	Grothendieck's existence theorem for the Picard scheme (Kleiman §4 Thm.
296	`th:main`), specialised via Cor. `cor:algsch` to `S = Spec k`,
297	`X = C` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve: the étale-sheafified
298	relative Picard functor `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` is representable by a `k`-scheme,
299	separated and locally of finite type, which is a disjoint union of open
300	quasi-projective `k`-subschemes. The representing scheme is the **Picard
301	scheme** `Pic_{C/k}` of `PicScheme` above.
302	
303	Blueprint reference: `thm:fga_pic_representability` (Kleiman §4 Thm.
304	`th:main` + Cor. `cor:algsch`). -/
305	
306	/-- **FGA representability of the Picard scheme.**
307	
308	Let `k` be a field and `C` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve
309	over `k`. Then the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`
310	is representable by the Picard scheme `PicScheme C`.
311	
312	iter-177+: the body assembles the four-step Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main` proof:
313	1. Stratify `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` by Hilbert polynomial into a disjoint open
314	   cover `(P^φ)_φ`.
315	2. For each `φ`, bound twists by `m`-regularity to reduce to `P^{φ_0}_0`.
316	3. Factor through the Abel map `A_{C/k}` of `abelMap` to obtain
317	   `Z := P^{φ_0}_0 ×_{Pic^♯} Div_{C/k}`, a quasi-projective open of
318	   `Div_{C/k}`.
319	4. Apply `smoothProperQuotient` to the projection `Z ⟶ P^{φ_0}_0` to
320	   represent each `P^{φ_0}_0`; reassemble over `φ` for the full
321	   `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`.
322	
323	For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
324	noncomputable def representable {k : Type u} [Field k]
325	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
326	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
327	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
328	    (picSharp C).RepresentableBy (PicScheme C) :=
329	  sorry
330	
331	/-! ## §5. The abelian group-scheme structure
332	
333	The Picard scheme `Pic_{C/k}` inherits an abelian group-scheme structure
334	from the tensor product of invertible sheaves on `C ×_k T`. The relative
335	Picard presheaf `T ↦ Pic(C ×_k T)/π_T^* Pic(T)` is already an abelian
336	group at every `T` (the quotient of an abelian group by a subgroup),
337	functorially in `T`; this lifts to the étale sheafification (sheafification
338	of an abelian-presheaf is an abelian-sheaf, since it commutes with finite
339	products in the value category), and then to a group-scheme structure on
340	the representing scheme by Yoneda.
341	
342	Blueprint reference: `thm:pic_is_group_scheme` (Kleiman §2 Def. `df:Pfs` +
343	§4 Def. `df:Psch`). -/
344	
345	/-- **The Picard scheme is a `k`-group scheme.**
346	
347	The Picard scheme `PicScheme C` carries a canonical `GrpObj` structure (a
348	group-object structure in `Over (Spec (.of k))`), obtained by Yoneda
349	transport of the abelian-group structure on `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}(T)` (sum:
350	`[L] + [M] = [L ⊗ M]`; inverse: `-[L] = [L⁻¹]`; unit: `[O_{C ×_k T}]`).
351	
352	The full abelian-group-scheme refinement is the conjunction of this `GrpObj`
353	instance with the `IsCommMonObj (PicScheme C)` predicate (commutativity of
354	the multiplication), which holds because tensor product of invertible
355	sheaves is symmetric; `IsCommMonObj` is a separate instance threaded on top
356	of `GrpObj` in iter-177+.
357	
358	iter-177+: the body invokes the Yoneda lemma to transport the natural
359	abelian-group structure on `T ↦ picSharp C |_T` to the multiplication /
360	inverse / unit morphisms on `PicScheme C`; the group axioms hold at the
361	representable level functorially in `T`, hence on the representing scheme
362	by Yoneda. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
363	noncomputable instance groupSchemeStructure {k : Type u} [Field k]
364	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
365	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
366	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] :
367	    GrpObj (PicScheme C) :=
368	  sorry
369	
370	end PicScheme
371	
372	end Scheme
373	
374	end AlgebraicGeometry
