6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.BareScheme
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.ChartIso
8	
9	/-!
10	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 3): standard `k̄`-points on `ℙ¹`; `𝔾_a` and `𝔾_m`
11	
12	This file is **Stratum 3** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
13	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:
14	
15	* the three standard `k̄`-points `0 = [0 : 1]`, `1 = [1 : 1]`, `∞ = [1 : 0]` of
16	  `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, encoded as sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`;
17	* the additive group `Ga` over `Spec k̄` (= `AffineSpace (Fin 1)`) and its
18	  affine, finite-presentation, and reduced instances;
19	* the multiplicative group `Gm` over `Spec k̄` (= `Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`) and its
20	  affine, finite-presentation, reduced, domain, and irreducible-space instances,
21	  plus the scaffold `GrpObj Gm` and the resulting `Smooth Gm.hom`;
22	* the multiplicative identity `Gm.onePt = η[Gm kbar]`.
23	
24	Upstream strata: `BareScheme`, `ChartIso`. Downstream stratum: `GmScaling`.
25	-/
26	
27	set_option autoImplicit false
28	set_option linter.style.setOption false
29	
30	universe u
31	
32	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
33	
34	noncomputable section
35	
36	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
37	
38	/-! ### The standard `k̄`-points `0`, `1`, `∞` on `ℙ¹`
39	
40	The three distinguished `k̄`-points of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` are `[0 : 1]` (the affine origin), `[1 : 1]`
41	(the affine unit), and `[1 : 0]` (the point at infinity). They are encoded as morphisms
42	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, i.e. sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`.
43	
44	The construction goes through `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`: a `k̄`-point of `Proj 𝒜` is
45	specified by an evaluation ring map `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* k̄` (composed into
46	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) whose image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)`
47	generates the unit ideal — automatic whenever the evaluation vector has a unit coordinate. -/
48	
49	/-- **The underlying ring map of a `k̄`-point of `ℙ¹`.** Sends `X₀ ↦ v 0`, `X₁ ↦ v 1`,
50	then composes back into `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via the inverse of `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
51	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
52	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) :
53	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤) :=
54	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).inv.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.eval v)
55	
56	/-- **Irrelevant-ideal-maps-to-top condition** for `ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal`: if one
57	of the coordinates `v 0` or `v 1` is a unit, then the image of `(X₀, X₁)` generates the
58	unit ideal in `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`. -/
59	private lemma ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top
60	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
61	    Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
62	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤ := by
63	  have hX_mem : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
64	      (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
65	    HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_of_mem _ Nat.zero_lt_one
66	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
67	  have hImg_mem : ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i) ∈
68	      Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
69	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
70	    Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hX_mem
71	  have hImg_unit : IsUnit (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i)) := by
72	    unfold ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
73	    rw [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval_X]
74	    exact hi.map _
75	  rw [Ideal.eq_top_iff_one]
76	  obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hImg_unit
77	  rw [show (1 : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)) = ((u⁻¹ : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)ˣ) : _) * u from
78	    (Units.inv_mul _).symm]
79	  exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (hu.symm ▸ hImg_mem)
80	
81	/-- **Helper: construct a `k̄`-point of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`** from an evaluation vector
82	`v : Fin 2 → kbar` with at least one unit coordinate. The underlying scheme map is
83	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation; the section condition chases through
84	`fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` + `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) MvPoly` collapse to
85	`MvPolynomial.C` + `MvPolynomial.eval_C` + `toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv`. -/
86	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec
87	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
88	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
89	  Over.homMk
90	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
91	      (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
92	      (ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| by
93	    -- Section condition: fromOfGlobalSections ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = 𝟙.
94	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
95	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
96	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
97	    -- Expose the structure morphism's composition shape.
98	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
99	    -- Combine via `fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`, then unify the two Spec.maps.
100	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
101	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
102	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
103	    -- The inner composition `(evalIntoGlobal v) ∘ C` collapses to `(ΓSpecIso).inv.hom`.
104	    -- Build a CommRingCat-level equation, then apply `Spec.map` and finish.
105	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom
106	        ((ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v).comp MvPolynomial.C) =
107	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
108	      apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
109	      ext r
110	      change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
111	          ((MvPolynomial.eval v) (MvPolynomial.C r)) = _
112	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval_C]
113	    calc _ = (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)).toSpecΓ ≫
114	            Spec.map (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
115	            exact congrArg _ (congrArg Spec.map hcc)
116	      _ = _ := AlgebraicGeometry.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv _
117	
118	/-- The `k̄`-point `0 = [0 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` via
119	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation `X₀ ↦ 0`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
120	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
121	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
122	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else 1) 1 (by simp)
123	
124	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 = [1 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
125	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
126	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
127	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
128	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun _ => 1) 0 (by simp)
129	
130	/-- The `k̄`-point `∞ = [1 : 0] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
131	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 0`. -/
132	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.inftyPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
133	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
134	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (by simp)
135	
136	/-! ### (B) The additive group `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` -/
137	
138	/-- **The additive group `𝔾_a = 𝔸¹` over `Spec k̄` as an underlying scheme.** This is the
139	affine line `AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))`. It is affine, locally of finite
140	presentation, and reduced (its global sections are `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain). -/
141	def GaScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
142	  AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))
143	
144	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GaScheme` (via
145	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.over`). -/
146	instance gaScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
147	    (GaScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
148	  inferInstanceAs ((AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
149	
150	/-- **The additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
151	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
152	abbrev Ga (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
153	  (GaScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
154	
155	/-- **`𝔾_a` is an affine morphism over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
156	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.instIsAffineHomOverSchemeInferInstanceOverClass`. -/
157	instance ga_isAffineHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
158	    IsAffineHom (Ga kbar).hom :=
159	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffineHom (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘
160	    (Spec (.of kbar))))
161	
162	/-- **`𝔾_a` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
163	`AffineSpace.instLocallyOfFinitePresentation…OfFinite` (the index `Fin 1` is finite). -/
164	instance ga_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
165	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Ga kbar).hom :=
166	  inferInstanceAs (LocallyOfFinitePresentation
167	    (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘ Spec (.of kbar)))
168	
169	/-- **`𝔾_a`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** Since the global sections are
170	`MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain over a field, the affine scheme is reduced. The proof
171	transfers `IsReduced (Spec (.of (MvPolynomial _ _)))` (free for any reduced ring) across
172	`AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine`. -/
173	instance ga_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Ga kbar).left :=
174	  isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine (Fin 1) _).hom
175	
176	/-! ### (C) The multiplicative group `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` -/
177	
178	/-- **The ring `k̄[t, t⁻¹] = Localization.Away t`**, where `t = X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄`.
179	This is the global-sections ring of `𝔾_m`. -/
180	abbrev GmRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Type u :=
181	  Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
182	
183	/-- **The multiplicative group `𝔾_m = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` as an underlying scheme.** This is
184	the chosen affine encoding (the analogist `gm-scaling-p1` D2.b verdict: AFFINE `Spec`, NOT
185	the basic-open of `𝔸¹` — the latter loses `IsAffine`). -/
186	def GmScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
187	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))
188	
189	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GmScheme`. -/
190	instance gmScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
191	    (GmScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
192	  hom := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))
193	
194	/-- **The multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
195	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
196	abbrev Gm (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
197	  (GmScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
198	
199	/-- **`𝔾_m` is affine.** -/
200	instance gm_isAffine (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsAffine (Gm kbar).left :=
201	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffine (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
202	
203	/-- **`𝔾_m` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** Follows from
204	`Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` (a localization of a polynomial ring at a single
205	element is finitely presented), bridged through
206	`AlgebraicGeometry.HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` for the `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` /
207	`RingHom.FinitePresentation` correspondence. -/
208	instance gm_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
209	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Gm kbar).hom :=
210	  (HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation)).mpr
211	    ((RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap (B := GmRing kbar)).mpr
212	      inferInstance)
213	
214	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** `k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is a localization of a domain at
215	a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain, hence reduced. The `IsReduced (Spec _)` instance picks
216	up directly from `IsReduced (Localization.Away _)`. -/
217	instance gm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Gm kbar).left :=
218	  inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
219	
220	/-- **`k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain.** Localization of the integral polynomial ring
221	`MvPolynomial Unit k̄` at the powers of a nonzero element `X () ≠ 0` preserves the domain
222	property (`IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` applied to
223	`Submonoid.powers (X ()) ≤ nonZeroDivisors`, the latter from `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero`).
224	
225	Load-bearing for `gm_irreducibleSpace` and `gm_geomIrred`. -/
226	instance gmRing_isDomain (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsDomain (GmRing kbar) := by
227	  unfold GmRing
228	  exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
229	    (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
230	
231	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is irreducible.** Follows from the fact that the
232	global-sections ring `GmRing = k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain (`gmRing_isDomain`)
233	together with Mathlib's `Spec` of a domain being irreducible
234	(`PrimeSpectrum.irreducibleSpace`). -/
235	instance gm_irreducibleSpace (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
236	    IrreducibleSpace (Gm kbar).left := by
237	  change IrreducibleSpace (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)))
238	  infer_instance
239	
240	/-! ### `gm_grpObj` — `GrpObj` instance on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`
241	
242	The route is per the iter-179 `gm-grpobj-representable` analogy (8-step recipe).
243	
244	Step A: build the units-of-global-sections functor `gmHomFunctor : (Over (Spec k̄))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat`,
245	which sends `T ↦ Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. This is the presheaf of groups that `Gm` represents.
246	
247	Step B: build the `RepresentableBy` witness asserting `Hom(-, Gm) ≃ Γ(-, ⊤)ˣ` naturally.
248	The per-`T` bijection is the 3-step chain (over-cat unfold → Γ-Spec adjunction →
249	`IsLocalization.Away.lift` universal property of `k̄[t, t⁻¹]`).
250	
251	Step C: `gm_grpObj := GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (Gm kbar) gmHomFunctor _`. -/
252	
253	/-- The `(Over (Spec k̄))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{u}` functor of units of global sections.
254	This is the presheaf of groups that `Gm` represents. -/
255	private noncomputable def gmHomFunctor (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
256	    (Over (Spec (.of kbar)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{u} where
257	  obj T := GrpCat.of (Γ(T.unop.left, ⊤))ˣ
258	  map {_ _} φ := GrpCat.ofHom (Units.map (φ.unop.left.appTop).hom.toMonoidHom)
259	  map_id := by
260	    intro T
261	    apply GrpCat.hom_ext
262	    apply MonoidHom.ext
263	    intro u
264	    apply Units.ext
265	    change ((𝟙 T.unop : T.unop ⟶ T.unop).left.appTop).hom u.val = u.val
266	    rw [Over.id_left]
267	    simp
268	  map_comp := by
269	    intros T T' T'' φ ψ
270	    apply GrpCat.hom_ext
271	    apply MonoidHom.ext
272	    intro u
273	    apply Units.ext
274	    change ((φ ≫ ψ).unop.left.appTop).hom u.val
275	      = ((ψ.unop.left.appTop).hom (((φ.unop.left.appTop).hom) u.val))
276	    rw [show (φ ≫ ψ).unop = ψ.unop ≫ φ.unop from rfl, Over.comp_left]
277	    simp
278	
279	/-- **Forward** of the per-`T` bijection: a morphism `f : T ⟶ Gm kbar` in `Over (Spec k̄)`
280	gives the unit in `Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ` corresponding to the image of the standard generator
281	`t ∈ k̄[t, t⁻¹]` under the induced ring map.
282	
283	Concretely, `f.left.appTop : Γ(Spec(GmRing), ⊤) ⟶ Γ(T.left, ⊤)`; composing with
284	`ΓSpecIso.inv` gives a ring map `GmRing → Γ(T.left, ⊤)`. The image of the algebra-map
285	generator `t ∈ GmRing` (a unit via `IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit`) is a unit
286	in `Γ(T.left, ⊤)`. -/
287	private noncomputable def gmHomEquiv_toFun (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
288	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) (f : T ⟶ Gm kbar) : (Γ(T.left, ⊤))ˣ :=
289	  letI ringMap : (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)) ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
290	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop
291	  Units.map ringMap.hom.toMonoidHom
292	    (IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit
293	      (S := GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)).unit
294	
295	/-- Helper for `gmHomEquiv_invFun`: the underlying scheme morphism factors over `Spec k̄`.
296	Substantive content: reduces to `IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp` + `MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq`
297	+ `Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality` (the Γ⊣Spec unit triangle). -/
298	private lemma gmHomEquiv_invFun_isOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
299	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) (u : (Γ(T.left, ⊤))ˣ) :
300	    letI structureRingMap : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
301	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop
302	    letI polyMap : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
303	      MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom structureRingMap.hom (fun _ => u.val)
304	    haveI hg : IsUnit (polyMap (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
305	      change IsUnit (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()))
306	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
307	      exact u.isUnit
308	    letI liftedMap : GmRing kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
309	      IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := GmRing kbar)
310	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) hg
311	    (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap)) ≫ (Gm kbar).hom = T.hom := by
312	  -- Set up local abbreviations matching the letI's of the goal.
313	  set structureRingMap : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
314	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop
315	  set polyMap : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
316	    MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom structureRingMap.hom (fun _ => u.val) with polyMap_def
317	  have hg : IsUnit (polyMap (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
318	    rw [polyMap_def, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
319	    exact u.isUnit
320	  set liftedMap : GmRing kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
321	    IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := GmRing kbar)
322	      (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) hg with liftedMap_def
323	  -- Unfold `(Gm kbar).hom`.
324	  change T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap) ≫
325	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) = T.hom
326	  -- Combine the Spec.maps.
327	  rw [← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
328	  -- `liftedMap ∘ algebraMap kbar GmRing = structureRingMap.hom`.
329	  have halg : (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)) =
330	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)).comp
331	        (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
332	    ext r
333	    exact (IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) r).symm
334	  have hcomp : liftedMap.comp (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)) = structureRingMap.hom := by
335	    change (IsLocalization.Away.lift _ hg).comp _ = _
336	    rw [halg, ← RingHom.comp_assoc, IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp]
337	    ext r
338	    change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ ((algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) r) = _
339	    rw [MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
340	  rw [hcomp]
341	  -- Now: T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map structureRingMap.hom = T.hom
342	  -- structureRingMap = ΓSpecIso.inv ≫ T.hom.appTop  (as CommRingCat morphism)
343	  change T.left.toSpecΓ ≫
344	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫
345	          T.hom.appTop).hom)) = T.hom
346	  -- CommRingCat.ofHom (...).hom = the original morphism (since ofHom and .hom are inverses)
347	  rw [show CommRingCat.ofHom (((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫
348	        T.hom.appTop).hom) = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop from
349	        CommRingCat.ofHom_hom _]
350	  rw [Spec.map_comp, ← Category.assoc, ← Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality]
351	  -- Now: (T.hom ≫ (Spec _).toSpecΓ) ≫ Spec.map (ΓSpecIso.inv) = T.hom
352	  rw [Category.assoc, toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv, Category.comp_id]
353	
354	/-- **Backward** of the per-`T` bijection: a unit `u : Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ` gives the morphism
355	`T ⟶ Gm kbar` defined by the ring map `k̄[t, t⁻¹] → Γ(T.left, ⊤)` sending `t ↦ u.val`.
356	
357	Concretely: build the polynomial-ring map `MvPolynomial Unit k̄ →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤)` via
358	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` (sending `X() ↦ u.val`); lift through the localization via
359	`IsLocalization.Away.lift` (using that `u.val` is a unit); convert to a scheme morphism
360	via `Spec.map`-then-`toSpecΓ`; bundle with the `Over`-commutativity proof. -/
361	private noncomputable def gmHomEquiv_invFun (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
362	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) (u : (Γ(T.left, ⊤))ˣ) : T ⟶ Gm kbar :=
363	  -- structure ring map kbar → Γ(T.left, ⊤), via T.hom.appTop and ΓSpecIso
364	  letI structureRingMap : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
365	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop
366	  letI polyMap : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
367	    MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom structureRingMap.hom (fun _ => u.val)
368	  haveI hg : IsUnit (polyMap (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
369	    change IsUnit (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()))
370	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
371	    exact u.isUnit
372	  letI liftedMap : GmRing kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
373	    IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := GmRing kbar)
374	      (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) hg
375	  Over.homMk
376	    (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap))
377	    (gmHomEquiv_invFun_isOver kbar T u)
378	
379	/-- Round-trip identity 1: `invFun ∘ toFun = id`. Substantive content reduces to
380	the Γ⊣Spec adjunction injectivity (`ext_to_Spec`) + `IsLocalization.Away.lift` uniqueness
381	applied to the lifted ring map from the morphism's `appTop`. -/
382	private lemma gmHomEquiv_left_inv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
383	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) :
384	    Function.LeftInverse (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T) (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T) := by
385	  intro f
386	  apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
387	  -- Goal: (invFun (toFun f)).left = f.left as T.left ⟶ Spec(GmRing).
388	  -- (a) `Over.w f` transferred through `Spec.appTop` + `ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality`:
389	  --     `structureRingMap = algebraMap kbar GmRing ≫ g_f` (as CommRingCat morphisms),
390	  --     where `g_f := (ΓSpecIso (GmRing)).inv ≫ f.left.appTop`.
391	  have hw : (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop =
392	      CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)) ≫
393	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop) := by
394	    have hOver := Over.w f
395	    -- `(Gm kbar).hom = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))` by defeq.
396	    change f.left ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) = T.hom
397	      at hOver
398	    rw [← hOver, Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]
399	    set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
400	      rw [← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc]
401	  -- (b) Compare the two morphisms `T.left ⟶ Spec(GmRing)` via `ext_to_Spec`.
402	  apply AlgebraicGeometry.ext_to_Spec
403	  change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
404	      (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T f)).left.appTop
405	    = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop
406	  -- Reduce LHS to `CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap_{toFun f}` (same chain as in `right_inv`).
407	  unfold gmHomEquiv_invFun
408	  change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
409	      (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (IsLocalization.Away.lift
410	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) _))).appTop
411	    = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop
412	  rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, ← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc,
413	    Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
414	  -- Goal: `CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap_{toFun f} = g_f` as CommRingCat morphisms.
415	  apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
416	  simp only [CommRingCat.hom_ofHom, IsLocalization.Away.lift]
417	  -- Apply `IsLocalization.lift_unique`: it suffices to show
418	  -- `g_f.hom ∘ algebraMap_{MvPoly→GmRing} = polyMap` on the MvPoly side.
419	  refine IsLocalization.lift_unique _ ?_
420	  change ∀ (x : MvPolynomial Unit kbar),
421	    (((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop).hom.comp
422	        (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))) x = (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _) x
423	  apply DFunLike.congr_fun
424	  -- Both sides are RingHoms `MvPoly Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤)`. By `ringHom_ext`,
425	  -- it suffices to compare on `C r` (constants) and `X i` (variables).
426	  refine MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext (fun r => ?_) (fun i => ?_)
427	  · -- `C r`-case: by `Over.w f`, `g_f ∘ algebraMap_{kbar→GmRing} = structureRingMap`.
428	    have hwr := congrArg (fun (g : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ _) => g.hom r) hw
429	    simp only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, RingHom.comp_apply, CommRingCat.hom_ofHom] at hwr
430	    simp only [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
431	    -- Now LHS = g_f.hom (algebraMap kbar GmRing r), RHS = structureRingMap.hom r
432	    exact hwr.symm
433	  · -- `X i`-case: `i : Unit`, so `i = ()`; then both sides reduce to `(toFun f).val`.
434	    obtain ⟨⟩ := i
435	    simp [gmHomEquiv_toFun]
436	
437	/-- Round-trip identity 2: `toFun ∘ invFun = id`. Substantive content reduces to
438	`IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq` (image of the algebra-map generator is the unit)
439	plus `ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality` + `Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop` (canceling the unit triangle). -/
440	private lemma gmHomEquiv_right_inv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
441	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) :
442	    Function.RightInverse (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T) (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T) := by
443	  intro u
444	  apply Units.ext
445	  -- Stage the proof at the CommRingCat morphism level.
446	  change ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
447	        (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T u).left.appTop).hom
448	      ((algebraMap _ _) (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) = u.val
449	  -- Unfold `invFun` to expose `T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (ofHom liftedMap)` as the
450	  -- underlying scheme morphism.
451	  unfold gmHomEquiv_invFun
452	  change ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
453	      (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
454	        (IsLocalization.Away.lift
455	          (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) _))).appTop).hom
456	      ((algebraMap _ _) (MvPolynomial.X ())) = u.val
457	  -- Step (a): morphism equality `(ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫ invFun(u).left.appTop = ofHom liftedMap`
458	  -- via `Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop`, `← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc`,
459	  -- `Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop`, `Iso.inv_hom_id`, `Category.comp_id`.
460	  rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, ← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc,
461	    Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
462	  -- Step (b)+(c): conclude via `IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq` + `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X'`.
463	  simp [IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
464	
465	/-- Naturality of `homEquiv`: pre-composition by `φ : T' ⟶ T` in `Over (Spec k̄)` corresponds
466	to `Units.map` of `φ.left.appTop` on the units side. Reduces to `Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop`
467	and the unit-element transfer through `IsLocalization.Away.lift` and the functor's `map` action. -/
468	private lemma gmHomEquiv_homEquiv_comp (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
469	    {T T' : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} (f : T ⟶ T') (g : T' ⟶ Gm kbar) :
470	    gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T (f ≫ g) =
471	      (gmHomFunctor kbar ⋙ forget _).map f.op (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T' g) := by
472	  apply Units.ext
473	  simp only [gmHomEquiv_toFun, gmHomFunctor, Functor.comp_map, Units.coe_map,
474	    IsUnit.unit_spec, GrpCat.hom_ofHom, Over.comp_left, Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]
475	  rfl
476	
477	/-- The `RepresentableBy` witness exhibiting `Gm kbar` as a representing object for the
478	units-of-global-sections functor. Per-`T` bijection is `gmHomEquiv_toFun` / `gmHomEquiv_invFun`;
479	round-trip identities split into `gmHomEquiv_left_inv` / `gmHomEquiv_right_inv` (named
480	substantive sorries) and naturality `homEquiv_comp` (named substantive sorry). The deep
481	Mathlib-gap content per the iter-179 `gm-grpobj-representable` analogy — no
482	`Spec(Localization.Away)` analogue of `AffineSpace.homOverEquiv` in Mathlib. -/
483	private noncomputable def gmHomFunctor_representableBy (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
484	    (gmHomFunctor kbar ⋙ forget _).RepresentableBy (Gm kbar) where
485	  homEquiv {T} :=
486	    { toFun := gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T
487	      invFun := gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T
488	      left_inv := gmHomEquiv_left_inv kbar T
489	      right_inv := gmHomEquiv_right_inv kbar T }
490	  homEquiv_comp := gmHomEquiv_homEquiv_comp kbar
491	
492	/-- **`GrpObj`-structure on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`.**
493	
494	Installs the multiplicative-group structure on `Gm` using `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with
495	the units functor `T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. The representable-by witness exploits the
496	fact that morphisms into `Spec (Localization.Away t)` correspond exactly to units in the
497	global sections (Mathlib's `IsLocalization.Away`-Spec bijection).
498	
499	This `GrpObj Gm` is the LIVE consumer of the iter-166 `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`
500	proof body (the `𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut applies `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` with
501	`W = Gm`). -/
502	instance gm_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Gm kbar) :=
503	  GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (Gm kbar) (gmHomFunctor kbar) (gmHomFunctor_representableBy kbar)
504	
505	/-- **`𝔾_m` is smooth over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` once
506	`GrpObj`, `LocallyOfFinitePresentation`, and `IsReduced` are installed. -/
507	instance gm_smooth (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
508	    Smooth (Gm kbar).hom :=
509	  have : GrpObj (Over.mk (Gm kbar).hom) := gm_grpObj kbar
510	  smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed (Gm kbar).hom
511	
512	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 ∈ 𝔾_m` (the multiplicative identity), encoded as the group-object
513	unit `η[Gm kbar] : 𝟙_ ⟶ Gm kbar`. -/
514	def Gm.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
515	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Gm kbar :=
516	  η[Gm kbar]
517	
518	end AlgebraicGeometry
519	
520	end
521

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.StructureSheafModuleK.SheafProperty
7	
8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: finite-length carriers and producer instances
10	
11	Sub-file (3/3) of the `StructureSheafModuleK` split (refactor iter-174). This file
12	hosts the downstream finite-length carriers (`IsAffineHModuleVanishing`,
13	`IsHModuleHomFinite`), their algebraic bridges (the `HModule` / `HModule'`
14	abbrevs, the H⁰ `LinearEquiv` transport companions, the constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction
15	upgrade), and the curve-side producer instances. Upstream sub-files
16	`Presheaf.lean` and `SheafProperty.lean` provide the structure-sheaf object the
17	carriers depend on.
18	
19	Downstream finite-length carriers in this file:
20	
21	* H>0 affine cohomology vanishing is carried by iter-040's
22	  `IsAffineHModuleVanishing` (the producer instance for the curve case
23	  lives at iter-051).
24	* H⁰ Hom-finiteness is carried by iter-043's **wholespace**
25	  `IsHModuleHomFinite`; the producer instance on a proper geometrically
26	  integral $k$-curve is supplied at iter-046 from Stein finiteness
27	  (`module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper`).
28	* The iter-041 per-affine-open variant `IsAffineHModuleHomFinite` was an
29	  abandoned attempt (dead scaffolding — $\Gamma(U, O_C)$ is not finite
30	  over $k$ on a proper affine open of a non-trivial curve) and has been
31	  removed from this file.
32	
33	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
34	-/
35	
36	set_option autoImplicit false
37	
38	universe u v
39	
40	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
41	
42	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
43	
44	/-- Phase A step 5/6 bridge (iter-009 scaffold): the parallel `Sheaf.H` for
45	`ModuleCat k`-valued sheaves. Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.H` is
46	parameterised over `Sheaf J AddCommGrpCat` only, so closing `genus` honestly
47	requires this `ModuleCat k`-flavoured version. The result carries `Module k`
48	automatically via `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.instModule`, and
49	`Module.finrank k` is therefore well-defined on it. The declaration is a
50	`noncomputable abbrev` (rather than `def`) so that instance synthesis sees
51	through the wrapper to find `Module k` and `AddCommGroup` instances. -/
52	noncomputable abbrev HModule
53	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
54	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
55	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
56	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) : Type (u+1) :=
57	  Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n
58	
59	/-- Phase A step 6 algebraic bridge (iter-010 scaffold): the $k$-linear
60	identification of `HModule k F 0` with the Hom group from the constant
61	sheaf at `ModuleCat.of k k`. Mathlib provides
62	`CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀ : Ext X Y 0 ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ Y)` in any
63	`Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to the `Linear k`
64	enrichment of `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (auto-inferable from
65	`HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`), this collapses `HModule k F 0` to a
66	`k`-linear Hom group. The closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀`;
67	probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-010 plan-agent). Used downstream to
68	identify `H⁰(C, toModuleKSheaf C)` with `Γ(C, O_C)` viewed as a
69	`k`-module on a connected proper `k`-curve. -/
70	noncomputable def HModule_zero_linearEquiv
71	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
72	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
73	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
74	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
75	    HModule k F 0 ≃ₗ[k]
76	      ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F) :=
77	  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀
78	
79	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-013 scaffold): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
80	cohomology of an open `X : C` with values in a sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`.
81	Mirrors Mathlib's `Sheaf.H' F n X = (F.cohomologyPresheaf n).obj (op X)`
82	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Basic.lean` L105) for
83	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued sheaves, with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
84	
85	The codomain is `Type u` (not `ModuleCat.{u} k`): `Abelian.Ext` returns a bare
86	`Type u` carrying `Module k` via `Abelian.Ext.instModule` through the `Linear k`
87	enrichment. The `noncomputable abbrev` form (rather than `def`) is required so
88	instance synthesis sees through the wrapper to find `Module k (HModule' k F n X)`
89	and `AddCommGroup (HModule' k F n X)` — under `def`, `Module.finrank` would fail
90	to typecheck (cf. iter-009 design rationale on `HModule`).
91	
92	This is the prerequisite for the iter-014+ `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris
93	LES (mirror of `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`).
94	The iter-014+ work will state and prove the LES on a `MayerVietorisSquare`,
95	specialising to a 2-affine cover of a proper `k`-curve in iter-015+. The
96	comparison theorem `cechCohomology_OC C 𝒰 n ≅ HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) n`
97	for an acyclic cover (the technical heart of Path-2) is also queued for
98	iter-015+. -/
99	noncomputable abbrev HModule' (k : Type u) [Field k]
100	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
101	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
102	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
103	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) (X : C) : Type u :=
104	  Abelian.Ext ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
105	    ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X)) F n
106	
107	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-015): the `H⁰` algebraic bridge for `HModule'`,
108	mirroring iter-010's `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` for the iter-014 `HModule'`. The
109	$k$-linear identification of `HModule' k F 0 X` with the Hom group from the
110	sheafified representable `(presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ ModuleCat.free k).obj X)`.
111	Mathlib provides `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀ : Ext X Y 0 ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ Y)`
112	in any `Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to `R = k` and to the
113	`Linear k`-enriched abelian category `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (the `Linear k`
114	enrichment is auto-inferable from `HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` via Mathlib's
115	`Sheaf.linear`), this collapses `HModule' k F 0 X` to a `k`-linear Hom group. The
116	closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀`; probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-015
117	plan-agent). Used downstream as the algebraic prerequisite for Stein-factorization-
118	derived `H⁰(C, O_C) ≃ k` on a connected proper `k`-curve (multi-iteration; queued
119	iter-017+ alongside the Mayer-Vietoris LES of iter-016+). -/
120	noncomputable def HModule'_zero_linearEquiv
121	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
122	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
123	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
124	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
125	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (X : C) :
126	    HModule' k F 0 X ≃ₗ[k]
127	      ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
128	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X) ⟶ F) :=
129	  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀
130	
131	/-- Iter-038 `Module.Finite` H⁰ transport companion for `HModule k F 0`. The iter-010
132	H⁰ bridge `HModule_zero_linearEquiv : HModule k F 0 ≃ₗ[k] ((constantSheaf J _).obj
133	(ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)` identifies the degree-zero cohomology with a `k`-linear Hom
134	group; Mathlib's `Module.Finite.equiv` then transports `Module.Finite k`-ness across.
135	The `.symm` is required: iter-010's bridge has `HModule k F 0` on the LHS and the
136	Hom group on the RHS, but we want the Hom hypothesis on the LHS and the
137	`HModule` conclusion on the RHS, so we apply `.symm` first. Mirrors iter-037's
138	`module_finite_HModule_of_HModule'_X₄` pattern at degree $0$, with no Mayer-Vietoris
139	machinery required. Used downstream as a building block for `Module.Finite k
140	(HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` once the Hom-from-constant-sheaf finiteness
141	input is supplied for proper geometrically integral $k$-curves. -/
142	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero
143	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
144	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
145	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
146	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
147	    [Module.Finite k ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)] :
148	    Module.Finite k (HModule k F 0) :=
149	  Module.Finite.equiv (HModule_zero_linearEquiv k F).symm
150	
151	/-- Iter-038 `Module.Finite` H⁰ transport companion for `HModule' k F 0 X`. Parallel
152	of `Scheme.module_finite_HModule_zero` for the iter-014 sheaf-parameterised carrier
153	`HModule'`. The iter-015 H⁰ bridge `HModule'_zero_linearEquiv : HModule' k F 0 X ≃ₗ[k]
154	((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ ModuleCat.free k).obj X) ⟶ F)` identifies the
155	degree-zero open-evaluation cohomology with a `k`-linear Hom group from the sheafified
156	representable; Mathlib's `Module.Finite.equiv` then transports `Module.Finite k`-ness
157	across. The `.symm` is required for the same orientation reason as in
158	`Scheme.module_finite_HModule_zero`. -/
159	theorem module_finite_HModule'_zero
160	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
161	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
162	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
163	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
164	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (X : C)
165	    [Module.Finite k ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
166	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X) ⟶ F)] :
167	    Module.Finite k (HModule' k F 0 X) :=
168	  Module.Finite.equiv (HModule'_zero_linearEquiv k F X).symm
169	
170	/-- Iter-039 curve specialisation of iter-038's `module_finite_HModule_zero` to
171	the structure sheaf `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` of a `Spec k`-scheme `C`. The
172	Grothendieck topology `Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat` is auto-inferred
173	via the iter-005 instances `instHasSheafify_Opens_ModuleCatK` and
174	`instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_ModuleCatK`. The sheaf argument is inferred from the
175	result type. Mirrors iter-030 / iter-035 / iter-036 / iter-037's `_curve`
176	patterns. Used downstream as a building block for `Module.Finite k (HModule k
177	(toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` once the Hom-from-constant-sheaf finiteness input is
178	supplied for proper geometrically integral $k$-curves (typically the morally
179	trivial `H^0(C, O_C) ≃ k` from Stein factorization on a connected proper
180	curve). -/
181	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero_curve
182	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
183	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
184	    [Module.Finite k
185	      ((constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
186	        (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)] :
187	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 0) :=
188	  Scheme.module_finite_HModule_zero k _
189	
190	/-- Iter-039 curve specialisation of iter-038's `module_finite_HModule'_zero` to
191	the structure sheaf `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` of a `Spec k`-scheme `C`,
192	evaluated at an arbitrary open `U` of the underlying topological space. Parallel
193	of `module_finite_HModule_zero_curve` for the iter-014 sheaf-parameterised
194	carrier `HModule'`.  Same auto-inferred topology / instance setup. The open `U`
195	is an explicit parameter (unlike the implicit topology); the sheaf argument is
196	inferred. Used downstream in the cover-evaluation chain for proper geometrically
197	integral $k$-curves: for each affine corner $X_i$ of an `AffineCoverMVSquare`,
198	the iter-039 transport propagates `Module.Finite k`-ness from the Hom group
199	`((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ free_k).obj X_i) ⟶ toModuleKSheaf C)` —
200	morally `Module.Finite k (Γ(X_i, O_C))` for affine $X_i$ — to the H⁰ cohomology
201	piece `HModule' k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0 X_i`. -/
202	theorem module_finite_HModule'_zero_curve
203	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
204	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
205	    (U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)
206	    [Module.Finite k
207	      ((presheafToSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
208	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj U) ⟶
209	          Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)] :
210	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 0 U) :=
211	  Scheme.module_finite_HModule'_zero k _ U
212	
213	/-- Iter-040 affine cohomology vanishing carrier predicate. Packages the
214	geometric statement that for every affine open `U` of `C.left.toTopCat` and
215	every degree `i > 0`, the open-evaluation cohomology `Scheme.HModule' k F i U`
216	is the zero `k`-module (formulated as `Subsingleton`, since `HModule'` returns
217	a `Type u` rather than a `ModuleCat` object — see iter-014 abbrev). The class
218	is the affine-vanishing input the cover-evaluation chain consumes once the
219	producer instance is supplied (queued for iter-041+; multi-iteration
220	project-local construction expected since Mathlib does not yet provide
221	scheme-level Serre vanishing on affines for the `ModuleCat k`-flavour). -/
222	class IsAffineHModuleVanishing
223	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
224	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
225	    Prop where
226	  subsingleton_HModule' : ∀ {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat},
227	    AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen U → ∀ i, 0 < i →
228	      Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i U)
229	
230	/-- Iter-040 immediate consumer of `IsAffineHModuleVanishing`: given the
231	class hypothesis, the open-evaluation cohomology `HModule' k F i U` is
232	`Module.Finite k` for any affine open `U` and any `i > 0`. The proof unfolds
233	the class field to `Subsingleton (HModule' k F i U)` and then invokes
234	Mathlib's auto-derived `Subsingleton M → Module.Finite R M` instance (any
235	subsingleton module is finitely generated by the empty set, hence finite).
236	This consumer is the building block for the cover-evaluation chain: combined
237	with iter-039's `H^0` curve specialisations and iter-037's general-degree
238	corner-bridge transport, it closes the algebraic side of `Module.Finite k
239	(HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1)` once the producer instance for
240	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing` is supplied. -/
241	theorem module_finite_HModule'_of_isAffineHModuleVanishing
242	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
243	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
244	    [IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F]
245	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
246	    (hU : AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen U) (i : ℕ) (hi : 0 < i) :
247	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule' k F i U) :=
248	  have : Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i U) :=
249	    IsAffineHModuleVanishing.subsingleton_HModule' (F := F) hU i hi
250	  inferInstance
251	
252	/-- Iter-043 wholespace H⁰ Hom-finiteness carrier predicate. Packages the
253	algebraic statement that the global Hom group
254	`((constantSheaf _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)` is finite over `k`. Morally
255	`Γ(C.left, F)` (the **global** sections of `F`) being finite over `k`. On a
256	proper geometrically integral $k$-curve $C$ with $F = O_C$, this is
257	$\Gamma(C, O_C) \simeq k$ (Stein factorization on a proper geometrically
258	connected curve), so this class admits a producer instance from the
259	geometric content of $C$ (supplied at iter-046).
260	
261	**Historical note on the abandoned per-affine-open variant.** An earlier
262	iter-041 attempt packaged Hom-finiteness of
263	`((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ free k).obj U) ⟶ F)` **for every
264	affine open** `U`. By Yoneda + free-functor + sheafify adjunctions this
265	Hom group is `≃ₗ[k] Γ(U, F)`, and on a proper smooth $k$-curve $C$ with
266	$F = O_C$, $\Gamma(U, O_C)$ is **NOT finite over $k$** for $U$ a proper
267	affine open — e.g. for the standard cover of $\mathbb{P}^1_k$ by
268	$U_0 = U_1 = \mathbb{A}^1_k$, $\Gamma(U_i, O_{\mathbb{P}^1}) = k[t]$ is
269	infinite over $k$. That per-open class therefore admits no producer
270	instance on a non-trivial proper curve and has been deleted as dead
271	scaffolding.
272	
273	This wholespace version captures only the **global** Hom group, which on
274	a proper curve is finite via Stein. Iter-044+ LES finite-length transport
275	uses this wholespace class (in conjunction with iter-040's H>0 affine
276	vanishing). -/
277	class IsHModuleHomFinite
278	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
279	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) : Prop where
280	  module_finite_hom : Module.Finite k
281	    ((constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
282	        (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)
283	
284	/-- Iter-043 immediate consumer: with the wholespace H⁰ Hom-finiteness class
285	hypothesis in scope, transport via iter-038's `module_finite_HModule_zero`
286	(which transports `Module.Finite k`-ness across iter-010's H⁰ algebraic bridge
287	`HModule_zero_linearEquiv : HModule k F 0 ≃ₗ[k] ((constantSheaf _).obj _ ⟶ F)`)
288	to obtain `Module.Finite k (HModule k F 0)`. Works at the global level
289	(one Hom-finiteness instance, not one per affine open). -/
290	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite
291	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
292	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
293	    [IsHModuleHomFinite k C F] :
294	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k F 0) :=
295	  have := IsHModuleHomFinite.module_finite_hom (k := k) (C := C) (F := F)
296	  module_finite_HModule_zero k F
297	
298	/-- Iter-043 curve specialisation: direct dot-notation wrapper for
299	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`. Saves call sites in the curve setting from
300	re-typing the sheaf argument when chaining into the LES finite-length
301	transport (queued for iter-044+). Mirrors the iter-039/iter-042 `_curve`
302	patterns. -/
303	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite_curve
304	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
305	    [IsHModuleHomFinite k C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)] :
306	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 0) :=
307	  module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite k C _
308	
309	/-- Iter-044 geometric Stein input for the iter-043 wholespace H⁰
310	Hom-finiteness carrier. For `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))` an
311	integral $k$-scheme with proper structure morphism, the global sections
312	$\Gamma(C, O_C)$ form a finite-dimensional $k$-vector space.
313	
314	This is Stein's classical statement, packaged via Mathlib's
315	`AlgebraicGeometry.finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`
316	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Proper.lean`):
317	for $X$ integral and $f \colon X \to \Spec K$ universally closed and
318	locally of finite type, the structure-morphism ring map `f.appTop`
319	is module-finite. `IsProper f` packages both `UniversallyClosed f` and
320	`LocallyOfFiniteType f`.
321	
322	The bridge from `RingHom.Finite (C.hom.appTop.hom)` to
323	`Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (op ⊤))` (where the algebra
324	structure on `Γ(C, ⊤)` is iter-006's `kToSection`-derived) uses
325	`RingHom.finite_algebraMap` plus `Module.Finite.of_equiv_equiv` to
326	transport the base ring from intermediate `Γ(Spec k, ⊤)` to `k` along
327	the ring iso `Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)`. The compatibility
328	of algebra maps reduces to showing
329	`kToSection C (op ⊤).hom = (C.hom.appTop.hom).comp (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom`,
330	which collapses via `Subsingleton.elim` on the `⊤ ⟶ ⊤` hom-set +
331	`Functor.map_id`.
332	
333	Iter-045+ consumes this input to assemble the producer instance
334	`IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` via lifting Mathlib's
335	`constantSheafΓAdj.homEquiv` to a `LinearEquiv` + identification of
336	`Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C)` with the underlying global sections
337	+ transport via `Module.Finite.equiv`. -/
338	theorem module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper
339	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
340	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
341	    Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) := by
342	  have hf : (C.hom.appTop.hom).Finite :=
343	    AlgebraicGeometry.finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed k C.hom
344	  letI alg2 : Algebra ((Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
345	               (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
346	    := RingHom.toAlgebra C.hom.appTop.hom
347	  have hM_inter :
348	      Module.Finite ((Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
349	        (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) := by
350	    rw [← RingHom.finite_algebraMap]; exact hf
351	  refine Module.Finite.of_equiv_equiv
352	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv
353	    (RingEquiv.refl _) ?_
354	  ext x
355	  simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, RingEquiv.coe_toRingHom, RingEquiv.refl_apply,
356	    Function.comp_apply, RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra]
357	  have h_kts : (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C (Opposite.op ⊤)).hom =
358	                (C.hom.appTop.hom).comp ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv.hom) := by
359	    ext y
360	    simp only [Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection, CommRingCat.hom_comp,
361	      RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
362	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_id (Opposite.op (⊤ :
363	                TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)))) _
364	  calc (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C (Opposite.op ⊤)))
365	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv x)
366	       = (C.hom.appTop.hom).comp ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv.hom)
367	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv x) :=
368	              congrFun (congrArg DFunLike.coe h_kts) _
369	    _  = C.hom.appTop.hom x := by
370	        simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
371	        congr 1
372	        change ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).hom ≫
373	              (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv).hom x = x
374	        rw [Iso.hom_inv_id]; rfl
375	
376	/-- Iter-045: LinearEquiv between the global-sections module
377	`(Sheaf.Γ J _).obj F` (an object of `ModuleCat k`) and the underlying carrier
378	of `F.obj.obj (op ⊤)` for any sheaf `F` on a topological space `X`.
379	
380	The underlying iso comes from `Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections` (Mathlib
381	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/GlobalSections.lean`): on a site with terminal
382	`T`, the global-sections functor is naturally iso to evaluation at `T`. For
383	the topology of opens `Opens.grothendieckTopology X`, the terminal in
384	`TopologicalSpace.Opens X` is the top open `⊤` (this is `Preorder.isTerminalTop`
385	for any preorder with a top element). The categorical iso in `ModuleCat k` is
386	converted to a `LinearEquiv` via `Iso.toLinearEquiv` (Mathlib's standard
387	upgrading of `ModuleCat`-isos to LinearEquivs).
388	
389	Iter-046+ uses this `LinearEquiv` together with the linearised constant-sheaf
390	/ global-sections adjunction (multi-iteration; project-local lift of
391	Mathlib's `Adjunction.homAddEquiv` to `≃ₗ[k]`) to construct the producer
392	instance `IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`. -/
393	noncomputable def SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top
394	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : TopCat.{u}}
395	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
396	    (Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj F
397	      ≃ₗ[k] F.obj.obj (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)) :=
398	  ((Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
399	      (ModuleCat.{u} k) (T := ⊤) (Preorder.isTerminalTop _)).app F).toLinearEquiv
400	
401	/-- Iter-045 immediate consumer: combining iter-044's geometric Stein input
402	`module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper` with `SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top`
403	yields `Module.Finite k ((Sheaf.Γ).obj (toModuleKSheaf C))` for a proper
404	integral `Spec k`-scheme `C`.
405	
406	The `haveI` is necessary because `Module.Finite.equiv` does not
407	auto-synthesise the `[Module.Finite k]` hypothesis on the source: the iter-044
408	declaration's conclusion is `Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (op ⊤))`,
409	but the source of `(SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top _ _).symm` is
410	`(toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj (op ⊤)` — these are *the same Module* (via the
411	iter-006 `toModuleKPresheaf_obj` simp lemma) but Lean needs the `haveI` to
412	register the typeclass under the new spelling.
413	
414	This is the algebraic input for the iter-046+ producer-instance assembly:
415	bridging from `Sheaf.Γ.obj` to the Hom group `((constantSheaf @ unit) ⟶ -)`
416	requires the linearised constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction, which is multi-iteration
417	project-local infrastructure. -/
418	theorem module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper
419	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
420	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
421	    Module.Finite k
422	      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
423	        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)) := by
424	  haveI : Module.Finite k
425	      ((Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj
426	        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)) : ModuleCat k) :=
427	    module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper k C
428	  exact Module.Finite.equiv
429	    (SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)).symm
430	
431	/-- Iter-046: applied LinearEquiv from the constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction. For any
432	sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)` and `X : ModuleCat k`, gives a `k`-LinearEquiv
433	between the Hom group `((constantSheaf).obj X ⟶ F)` (sheaf morphisms from the
434	constant sheaf at `X`) and `(X ⟶ (Sheaf.Γ).obj F)` (module morphisms into the
435	global sections of `F`).
436	
437	Built from `(constantSheafΓAdj).homLinearEquiv` (iter-046 Mathlib gap-fill).
438	The five `haveI` lines establish the typeclass scaffolding required to invoke
439	the gap-fill: `presheafToSheaf` Linear (via `sheafificationAdjunction`),
440	`constantSheaf` Additive + Linear (via composition), and `Sheaf.Γ` Additive +
441	Linear (via the `right_adjoint_*` propagators along `constantSheafΓAdj`).
442	
443	Used in iter-046's producer instance `instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf`
444	to bridge from `Sheaf.Γ.obj`-finiteness (iter-045) to Hom-from-constantSheaf-
445	finiteness (the `IsHModuleHomFinite` carrier). -/
446	noncomputable def constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv
447	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C]
448	    (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
449	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
450	    (X : ModuleCat.{u} k) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
451	    ((constantSheaf J _).obj X ⟶ F) ≃ₗ[k] (X ⟶ (Sheaf.Γ J _).obj F) :=
452	  haveI : (presheafToSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
453	    (sheafificationAdjunction J _).left_adjoint_linear k
454	  haveI : (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive := by
455	    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
456	  haveI : (Sheaf.Γ J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive :=
457	    (constantSheafΓAdj J _).right_adjoint_additive
458	  haveI : (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k := by
459	    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
460	  haveI : (Sheaf.Γ J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
461	    (constantSheafΓAdj J _).right_adjoint_linear k
462	  (constantSheafΓAdj J _).homLinearEquiv k X F
463	
464	/-- Iter-046: Hom-from-`k` upgrade. The Hom group `(ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M)` for
465	`M : ModuleCat k` is canonically `k`-LinearEquivalent to `M` via `f ↦ f 1`.
466	Direct one-liner combining `ModuleCat.homLinearEquiv` (Mathlib's
467	LinearEquiv-version of the underlying-LinearMap correspondence) with
468	`LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf` (the standard `(R →ₗ[R] M) ≃ₗ[S] M` evaluation). -/
469	noncomputable def homFromOne_linearEquiv (k : Type u) [Field k] (M : ModuleCat.{u} k) :
470	    (ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M) ≃ₗ[k] M :=
471	  (ModuleCat.homLinearEquiv (M := ModuleCat.of k k) (N := M) (S := k)).trans
472	    (LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf k k M)
473	
474	/-- Iter-046: **the producer instance** for `IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`
475	on a proper integral `Spec k`-scheme `C`. Closes the four-step chain:
476	
477	  (1) `constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv` (iter-046 step 1) bridges the Hom group
478	      `((constantSheaf).obj k ⟶ toModuleKSheaf C)` to `(k ⟶ Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))`.
479	  (2) `homFromOne_linearEquiv` (iter-046 step 3) identifies `(k ⟶ M)` with `M`
480	      as `k`-modules.
481	  (3) Combined LinearEquiv from (1)+(2): `((constantSheaf).obj k ⟶ toModuleKSheaf C)
482	      ≃ₗ[k] Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C)`.
483	  (4) `module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper` (iter-045 step 2) provides
484	      `Module.Finite k (Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))` from `[IsIntegral C.left]
485	      [IsProper C.hom]` (iter-044 geometric Stein input).
486	  (5) Transport via `Module.Finite.equiv (.symm)` of the combined LinearEquiv.
487	
488	Once landed, iter-043's curve consumer `module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite_curve`
489	fires automatically on `Module.Finite k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` queries,
490	closing the H⁰ side of the genus-finrank Module.Finite ladder.
491	
492	Marked `instance` (not `theorem`) — this is a *producer* of a typeclass instance,
493	to be picked up by typeclass synthesis when the consumer asks for
494	`IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`. Hypotheses `[IsIntegral C.left]`,
495	`[IsProper C.hom]` are class arguments propagated via instance synthesis from
496	the use site (where `C` is concretely a proper integral `Spec k`-scheme, e.g.\ a
497	smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve). -/
498	noncomputable instance instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf
499	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
500	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
501	    IsHModuleHomFinite k C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) where
502	  module_finite_hom := by
503	    haveI := Scheme.module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper k C
504	    let LE1 := constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv k
505	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.of k k)
506	      (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)
507	    let LE2 := homFromOne_linearEquiv k
508	      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
509	        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
510	    exact Module.Finite.equiv (LE1.trans LE2).symm
511	
512	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
513	
514	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
515	
516	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the Čech cochain complex of
517	the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))`,
518	with respect to an arbitrary indexed family of opens `𝒰 : ι → Opens C.left.toTopCat`.
519	Built from Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor` (file
520	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`) applied to the
521	underlying presheaf of `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` (iter-006). The result is a
522	cochain complex valued in `ModuleCat.{u} k`, indexed by `ℕ`.
523	
524	The cohomology of this complex is `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` below. The
525	downstream comparison theorem (Čech cohomology = derived-functor cohomology
526	= `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)` for an acyclic cover) is
527	queued for iter-013+; iter-012 only establishes the Čech-side carrier. -/
528	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain_OC
529	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
530	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
531	    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ℕ :=
532	  (cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
533	
534	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the `n`-th Čech cohomology
535	of the structure sheaf for an arbitrary indexed open cover. Defined as the
536	`n`-th homology of the Čech cochain complex `Scheme.cechCochain_OC`. The
537	result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k` and therefore carries a `Module k`
538	structure for free; the iter-013+ comparison theorem will identify it
539	with `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n` when the cover is
540	acyclic. -/
541	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology_OC
542	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
543	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
544	    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
545	  (Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰).homology n
546	
547	/-- Iter-047: parameterised Čech cochain complex generalising iter-012's
548	`Scheme.cechCochain_OC` to any sheaf of `k`-modules `F`, not just the structure
549	sheaf. Built from the same Mathlib `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor`
550	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`) applied to the
551	underlying presheaf of `F`. The result is a cochain complex valued in
552	`ModuleCat.{u} k`, indexed by `ℕ`. Iter-012's specialisation
553	`Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰` is recovered by setting `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`
554	(see `Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq` below).
555	
556	This generalisation is the foundational scaffolding the iter-048+ Čech-vs-derived
557	comparison theorem will build on: the comparison map
558	`Scheme.cechCohomology k C F 𝒰 n →ₗ[k] Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ᵢ 𝒰 i)`
559	(queued for iter-048) is naturally parameterised over the sheaf `F`, not just
560	the structure sheaf, so the parameterised carrier is the right level of
561	generality. -/
562	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain
563	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
564	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
565	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
566	    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ℕ :=
567	  (cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F)
568	
569	/-- Iter-047: parameterised Čech cohomology generalising iter-012's
570	`Scheme.cechCohomology_OC`. The `n`-th cohomology of the parameterised Čech
571	cochain complex. The result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k`. Iter-012's
572	specialisation is recovered by `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq` below. -/
573	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology
574	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
575	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
576	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
577	    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
578	  (Scheme.cechCochain C F 𝒰).homology n
579	
580	/-- Iter-047 bridge: iter-012's `Scheme.cechCochain_OC` is definitionally the
581	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation of `Scheme.cechCochain`. The
582	proof is `rfl` since iter-012's body is the same `(cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj
583	((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))` term. Used by downstream
584	consumers (iter-048+) to switch between the iter-012 structure-sheaf-specific
585	form and the iter-047 parameterised form without semantic loss. -/
586	theorem Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq
587	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
588	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
589	    Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰 = Scheme.cechCochain C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 :=
590	  rfl
591	
592	/-- Iter-047 bridge: iter-012's `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` is definitionally the
593	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation of `Scheme.cechCohomology`. -/
594	theorem Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq
595	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
596	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
597	    Scheme.cechCohomology_OC C 𝒰 n =
598	      Scheme.cechCohomology C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 n :=
599	  rfl
600	
601	/-- Iter-048: Čech-side acyclicity carrier predicate. The cover `𝒰` is
602	*Čech-acyclic for the sheaf `F` of `k`-modules* on `C` if positive-degree Čech
603	cohomology vanishes (in the `Subsingleton` sense — `Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n`
604	has type `ModuleCat.{u} k`, but `Subsingleton` on the underlying type is the
605	natural and more chainable form). Mirrors the iter-040 / iter-043 carrier-
606	predicate pattern: a single-field `Prop` class capturing a combinatorial
607	vanishing condition that downstream consumers receive as an instance argument.
608	
609	This is the foundational Čech-side input that the iter-051 producer instance for
610	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` will consume, in conjunction
611	with the iter-049+ Čech-vs-derived comparison theorem and iter-048's consumer. -/
612	class Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover
613	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
614	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
615	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) : Prop where
616	  subsingleton_cechCohomology :
617	    ∀ (n : ℕ), 0 < n → Subsingleton (Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n)
618	
619	/-- Iter-048: subsingleton transport via Čech acyclicity + comparison.
620	
621	Given the iter-048 carrier hypothesis `[IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰]` AND an explicit
622	comparison iso `compIso n : cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n ≃ₗ[k] HModule' k F n (⨆ 𝒰 i)`
623	(the Čech-vs-derived comparison, queued for iter-049+ to construct as a
624	theorem), conclude that `Subsingleton (HModule' k F n (⨆ 𝒰 i))` for `n ≥ 1`.
625	
626	The `compIso` is taken as an *explicit argument*, not a class field. The
627	comparison itself is a `LinearEquiv` (data), so it cannot be a field of a
628	`Prop`-valued class; more importantly, decoupling the Čech-side combinatorial
629	vanishing (iter-048) from the substantive comparison theorem (iter-049+) lets
630	each step land as a single iteration. Iter-049+ will provide the comparison as
631	a theorem, which iter-048's consumer accepts directly via this argument.
632	
633	The consumer body extracts the class field (the `Subsingleton` on
634	`cechCohomology n`) and transports along `(compIso n).symm.toEquiv.subsingleton`. -/
635	theorem Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover
636	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
637	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
638	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
639	    [Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰]
640	    (compIso : ∀ (n : ℕ),
641	      Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n ≃ₗ[k]
642	        Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i))
643	    (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) :
644	    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i)) := by
645	  haveI := Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover.subsingleton_cechCohomology
646	    (F := F) (𝒰 := 𝒰) n hn
647	  exact (compIso n).symm.toEquiv.subsingleton
648	
649	/-- Iter-048: curve specialisation at `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`.
650	
651	Mirrors the iter-039 / iter-042 / iter-043 `_curve` pattern: a thin dot-notation
652	wrapper that saves call sites in the curve setting (where `F` is the structure
653	sheaf) from re-typing `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` whenever the iter-048 consumer
654	is chained through. Used by the iter-051 `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`
655	producer instance. -/
656	theorem Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover_curve
657	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
658	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
659	    [Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰]
660	    (compIso : ∀ (n : ℕ),
661	      Scheme.cechCohomology C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 n ≃ₗ[k]
662	        Scheme.HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i))
663	    (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) :
664	    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i)) :=
665	  Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover (𝒰 := 𝒰) compIso n hn
666	
667	end AlgebraicGeometry

10	/-!
11	# The line bundle `𝒪_C(P)` of a closed point and its global sections (RR.3)
12	
13	This file is the **RR.3** sub-build chapter for the project's headline
14	`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically irreducible
15	genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma in
16	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
17	
18	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain for the genus-`0` ↦ `ℙ¹` classification routes
19	through:
20	
21	- `RR.1` (`WeilDivisor.lean`): the Weil divisor group `Div(C)` and the
22	  degree map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ`.
23	- `RR.2` (`RRFormula.lean`): the Euler-characteristic identity
24	  `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` and the Riemann–Roch dimension formula
25	  `ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1` in genus `0`.
26	- **`RR.3` (this file)**: the invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(P)` of a closed point
27	  `P ∈ C`, its `k̄`-vector space of global sections as the
28	  Riemann–Roch space `L([P])`, the `H¹`-vanishing
29	  `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 0` on a genus-`0` curve, the dimension formula
30	  `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2`, and the existence of a non-constant
31	  rational function `f ∈ K(C)` with at most a simple pole at `P`.
32	- `RR.4` (`RationalCurveIso.lean`, future): the "two-section
33	  ⇒ `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` ⇒ `≅ ℙ¹`" classification.
34	
35	## Status (iter-183 Lane A — sig amend + carrierSet scaffold)
36	
37	Iter-183 Lane A (re-dispatch from iter-182 deferral) landed:
38	
39	1. **Sig amend** `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` and `toFunctionField` now take
40	   the codimension-one witness `(hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)` explicitly,
41	   so the subsheaf-of-`K_C` carrier can read off the order at `P` via the
42	   prime divisor `⟨P, hPcoh⟩`. The amend matches the blueprint chapter prose
43	   for the Hartshorne subsheaf-of-`K_C` direct construction (per analogist
44	   `ocofp-sheaf-internalhom.md`, Decision 3 + Decision 4 verdict
45	   `ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB`).
46	2. **Scaffold** `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet` (concrete `Set`-valued
47	   substantive carrier — the set of rational functions with the order
48	   conditions on a given open). This is iter-183's substantive
49	   contribution beyond the sig amend; no new `sorry` introduced.
50	3. The bodies of `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` (L140) and `toFunctionField`
51	   (L154) remain typed `sorry` for iter-184+ (the full chain
52	   `carrierSet → carrierSubmodule (Submodule) → presheaf (Functor) →
53	   isSheaf (typed sorry) → Sheaf` is ~230-360 LOC; iter-183's helper
54	   budget = 5 and `sorry` ceiling = 7 forced PARTIAL).
55	
56	The 5 pinned declarations are:
57	
58	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint` — the invertible
59	   sheaf `𝒪_C(P)` associated to a closed point `P` on a smooth proper
60	   curve `C / k̄`.
61	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.globalSections_iff`
62	   — the identification of global sections of `𝒪_C(P)` with the
63	   Riemann–Roch space
64	   `L([P]) = {f ∈ K(C)^× | div(f) + [P] ≥ 0} ∪ {0}`,
65	   expressed as an `Iff`-style characterisation of the order conditions
66	   `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`.
67	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.h1_vanishing_genusZero`
68	   — the cohomological vanishing `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 0` on a smooth proper
69	   geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄` with `g(C) = 0`, via the long
70	   exact sequence of the closed-point short exact sequence
71	   `0 → 𝒪_C → 𝒪_C(P) → k(P) → 0`.
72	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.dim_eq_two_of_genusZero`
73	   — the dimension formula `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2` in genus `0`,
74	   specialising the χ-identity `RR.2` to `D = [P]` and consuming the
75	   `H¹`-vanishing of pin 3.
76	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.lineBundleAtClosedPoint.exists_nonconstant_genusZero`
77	   — the corollary: a non-constant rational function `f ∈ K(C)` regular
78	   on `C \ {P}` with at most a simple pole at `P`, obtained as a lift of
79	   any non-zero element of the quotient
80	   `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) / 𝓀̄ · 1`.
81	
82	## Notation reminders
83	
84	The line bundle `𝒪_C(P)` is realised as a
85	`Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)`,
86	the same `ModuleCat k̄`-flavoured sheaf category used by the project's
87	`Scheme.HModule` cohomology pipeline (cf. `AlgebraicJacobian.Genus`).
88	Its `H⁰` and `H¹` are computed via `Scheme.HModule kbar (·) 0` and
89	`Scheme.HModule kbar (·) 1`, both of which carry a canonical `Module k̄`
90	instance.
