110	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
111	
112	/-! ## §1. The line bundle of a closed point on a smooth proper curve
113	
114	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄` and a closed
115	point `P ∈ C`, the local ring `𝒪_{C,P}` is a DVR with maximal ideal
116	generated by a uniformiser `f_P`. Hartshorne's construction `ℒ(D)`
117	(II §6 p. 144) applied to the Cartier divisor `[P]` (locally cut out by
118	`f_P` near `P`, by `1` elsewhere) produces the invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(P)`:
119	the sub-`𝒪_C`-module of the function-field constant sheaf `𝒦_C ≅ K(C)`
120	generated locally by `f_P^{-1}` near `P` and by `1` on `C \ {P}`. -/
121	
122	namespace Scheme
123	
124	/-! ### Hartshorne subsheaf-of-`K_C` carrier (iter-183 Lane A scaffold)
125	
126	The substantive iter-183 contribution: a concrete per-open `Set`-valued
127	carrier of the line bundle `𝒪_C(P)`, realised directly as Hartshorne's
128	subsheaf of the function-field constant sheaf `K_C` (Hartshorne II §6
129	p. 144; analogist `ocofp-sheaf-internalhom.md` Decision 3
130	`ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB`).
131	
132	A section of `𝒪_C(P)` over an open `U` is a rational function `f ∈ K(C)`
133	satisfying the order conditions `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor
134	`Q ≠ P` with `Q.point ∈ U` (regularity on the complement of `P` inside
135	`U`) and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` when `P ∈ U` (at most a simple pole at `P`).
136	The construction is independent of the choice of uniformiser at `P`:
137	any two uniformisers differ by a unit, so the order-`≥ −1` condition at
138	`P` is intrinsic.
139	
140	iter-184+ will upgrade `carrierSet` to a `Submodule kbar K(C)` via the
141	closure proofs (zero / addition / kbar-scalar), bundle the result as a
142	presheaf functor (identity-on-`K(C)` restrictions, monotone in `U` via
143	`carrierSet_mono`), and discharge the sheaf property via gluing-by-
144	stalks (stalk-locality of the order conditions at each prime divisor). -/
145	
146	/-- **Carrier set** of `𝒪_C(P)` over an open `U : (Opens C.left)ᵒᵖ`: the
147	set of rational functions `f ∈ K(C)` satisfying the order conditions
148	`ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q` with `Q.point ∈ U.unop`,
149	`Q.point ≠ P`, and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` when `P ∈ U.unop`.
150	
151	iter-183 Lane A landed this as a concrete substantive `Set` definition;
152	no sorry. -/
153	private noncomputable def lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet
154	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
155	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsIntegral C.left]
156	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
157	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
158	    (P : C.left) (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
159	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
160	    Set C.left.functionField := by
161	  let Phat : C.left.PrimeDivisor := ⟨P, hPcoh⟩
162	  haveI : Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (C.left.presheaf.stalk Phat.point) :=
163	    Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.out Phat
164	  exact { f | (∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Q.point ∈ U.unop.1 → Q.point ≠ P →
165	              0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f) ∧
166	              (P ∈ U.unop.1 → (-1 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Phat f) }
167	
168	/-- **Monotonicity of `carrierSet` in `U`**: when `V.unop ⊆ U.unop` (i.e.
169	the open `V` is contained in the open `U`), the carrier on `U` is
170	INCLUDED in the carrier on `V` (the order conditions over the smaller
171	open `V` involve fewer prime divisors, hence are easier to satisfy).
172	
173	This is the substantive monotonicity that drives the (identity-on-`K(C)`)
174	restriction map of the would-be `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.presheaf` functor:
175	in `(Opens C.left)ᵒᵖ`, an arrow `U ⟶ V` corresponds to `V.unop ⊆ U.unop`,
176	and the restriction map `carrierSet U → carrierSet V` is the inclusion
177	delivered by this lemma.
178	
179	iter-183 Lane A landed this as the substantive monotonicity proof; no
180	sorry. -/
181	private lemma lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet_mono
182	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
183	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsIntegral C.left]
184	    [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
185	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
186	    (P : C.left) (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
187	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ}
188	    (hUV : V.unop.1 ⊆ U.unop.1) :
189	    lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet P hPcoh U
190	      ⊆ lineBundleAtClosedPoint.carrierSet P hPcoh V := by
191	  intro f hf
192	  refine ⟨fun Q hQV hQP => hf.1 Q (hUV hQV) hQP, fun hPV => hf.2 (hUV hPV)⟩
193	
194	/-- **The line bundle `𝒪_C(P)` of a closed point `P` on a smooth proper
195	curve `C / k̄`** (Hartshorne II §6, p. 144, Proposition 6.13(a)).
196	
197	The invertible sheaf cut out (in the dual / `𝒦_C`-subsheaf packaging of
198	Hartshorne `ℒ(D)`) by `f_P^{-1}` near `P` and by `1` on the complement,
199	where `f_P ∈ 𝔪_P ∖ 𝔪_P²` is any uniformiser of the DVR `𝒪_{C,P}`. The
200	result is independent of the choice of uniformiser (two uniformisers
201	differ by a unit) and is an invertible `𝒪_C`-module of rank one.
202	
203	The signature returns a `Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
204	(ModuleCat.{u} kbar)`: the same `ModuleCat k̄`-flavoured sheaf
205	carrier used by the project's `Scheme.HModule` cohomology pipeline (so
206	that `H⁰` and `H¹` of `𝒪_C(P)` are accessible via
207	`Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint P hP) 0/1`).
208	
209	iter-183+ body recipe (per analogist `ocofp-sheaf-internalhom.md` Decision 3
210	`ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB`): use the Hartshorne subsheaf-of-`K_C` direct
211	construction. Concretely, define the per-open submodule
212	`carrierSet U ⊆ K(C)` of rational functions `f` satisfying the order
213	conditions `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q ≠ P` with
214	`Q.point ∈ U.unop` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` when `P ∈ U.unop`; bundle as a
215	`Submodule kbar K(C)` (the closure proofs reduce to the
216	`Ring.ordFrac`-multiplicativity + non-archimedean inequality of the DVR
217	valuation at each prime divisor); upgrade to a presheaf functor via the
218	identity-on-`K(C)` restriction; close the sheaf property via the

265	/-! ## §2. Global sections as the Riemann–Roch space `L([P])`
266	
267	Hartshorne II §7 Proposition 7.7 identifies the global sections of
268	`ℒ(D_0)` with the rational functions `f ∈ K(X)^×` satisfying
269	`div(f) ≥ −D_0` (plus the zero section). Specialised to `D_0 = [P]` on a
270	curve, the condition `div(f) + [P] ≥ 0` rewrites coordinate-wise as
271	`ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`. -/
272	
273	/-- **Forward direction of `globalSections_iff` (Hartshorne II.7.7(b)).**
274	
275	Given `f ∈ K(C)^×` with `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q ≠ P`
276	and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`, the rational function `f` lifts to a global section
277	`s ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` whose image under the canonical inclusion
278	`𝒪_C(P) ↪ 𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` equals `f`. Hartshorne's construction inside the
279	proof of Proposition 7.7(b) (p. 157) defines this section locally on the
280	affine cover witnessing the Cartier-divisor structure of `[P]`: on a
281	neighbourhood `U_i` of `P` the section is `f · f_P` (in
282	`𝒪_C(P)(U_i) = f_P⁻¹ · 𝒪_C(U_i)`, this is `(f · f_P) · f_P⁻¹ = f`);
283	on the complement `C ∖ {P}` the section is `f` directly (the order
284	conditions `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P` guarantee `f ∈ 𝒪_C(U) = 𝒪_C(P)(U)`
285	on any affine open `U ⊆ C ∖ {P}`).
286

318	/-- **Backward direction of `globalSections_iff` (Hartshorne II.7.7(a)).**
319	
320	Given a global section `s ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` whose image under
321	`𝒪_C(P) ↪ 𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` equals `f`, the order conditions on `f` follow by
322	reading off the stalk-by-stalk DVR identification. Concretely:
323	
324	* At a prime divisor `Q ≠ P`, the stalk `𝒪_C(P)_Q = 𝒪_{C, Q}` agrees with
325	  the structure sheaf (since `𝒪_C(P)` equals `𝒪_C` on the open
326	  complement `C ∖ {P}`); the germ of `s` at `Q` lies in `𝒪_{C, Q}`, so
327	  the image `f = ι(s) ∈ K(C)` has valuation `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0`.
328	* At `P`, the stalk `𝒪_C(P)_P = f_P⁻¹ · 𝒪_{C, P}` (where `f_P` is a
329	  uniformiser of the DVR `𝒪_{C, P}`); the germ of `s` at `P` lies in
330	  this stalk, so `f = ι(s)` satisfies `f_P · f ∈ 𝒪_{C, P}`, i.e.
331	  `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`.

364	/-- **Global sections of `𝒪_C(P)` as rational functions with controlled
365	pole at `P`** (Hartshorne II §7 Proposition 7.7, p. 157).
366	
367	For a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(C)^×`, the following are
368	equivalent:
369	
370	* there exists a global section `s ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` whose image under
371	  the canonical inclusion `𝒪_C(P) ↪ 𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` equals `f` (formally,
372	  `lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField P hP s = f`);
373	* the order conditions hold: `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor
374	  `Q ∈ C.PrimeDivisor` whose generic point is not `P`, and
375	  `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` (where the latter is read off the prime divisor
376	  `⟨P, h⟩` with `h : Order.coheight P = 1` the codimension-one witness
377	  automatic for a closed point on a one-dimensional integral scheme).
378	
379	The iff is the substantive content of Hartshorne's Proposition 7.7(b) /
380	its proof, specialised to `D_0 = [P]`.
381	
382	**iter-181 Lane A PARTIAL — directional split landed**: the iff is now
383	proved by combining the two directional helpers
384	`globalSections_iff_mp` (Hartshorne II.7.7(b), forward) and
385	`globalSections_iff_mpr` (Hartshorne II.7.7(a), backward), both of

400	(Hartshorne II.7 Proposition 7.7(b), p. 157). -/
401	lemma globalSections_iff
402	    [∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor,
403	        Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point)]
404	    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
405	    (f : C.left.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0)
406	    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1) :
407	    (∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Q.point ≠ P →
408	        0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f) ∧

412	        (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP hPcoh) 0,

415	  ⟨globalSections_iff_mp P hP f hf hPcoh,
416	   globalSections_iff_mpr P hP f hf hPcoh⟩

418	/-! ## §3. Cohomological vanishing in genus zero
419	
420	Specialise Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3's inductive step at `D = 0`. The
421	standard short exact sequence
422	`0 → 𝒪_C(−[P]) → 𝒪_C → k(P) → 0` (Hartshorne II.6.18: the ideal sheaf
423	of the locally principal closed subscheme `P` is `𝒪_C(−[P])`; the
424	quotient is the skyscraper `k(P) ≅ k̄` at `P`) tensored by the locally
425	free rank-`1` sheaf `𝒪_C([P])` (left rigid, so preserves exactness and
426	leaves the skyscraper invariant) becomes
427	`0 → 𝒪_C → 𝒪_C(P) → k(P) → 0` in `Coh(C)`. The associated long exact
428	sequence of sheaf cohomology, combined with `H¹(C, 𝒪_C) = 0`
429	(genus-`0` hypothesis: `g(C) = dim_{k̄} H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`) and
430	`H¹(C, k(P)) = 0` (skyscraper / flasque), kills `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P))`. -/
431	
432	/-- **Vanishing of `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P))` on a smooth proper geometrically
433	irreducible curve of genus `0`** (Hartshorne IV §1 p. 296, the
434	inductive step of Theorem 1.3 specialised to `D = 0`).
435	
436	Concretely, the finite-dimensional `k̄`-vector space
437	`Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint P hP) 1` has dimension
438	`0`, i.e. is the trivial vector space.
439	
440	iter-177+ body: assemble the closed-point short exact sequence
441	`0 → 𝒪_C → 𝒪_C(P) → k(P) → 0`, feed it to the long exact sequence of
442	`Module k̄`-flavoured cohomology (the project's
443	`Scheme.HModule k̄`-bridge inherits the LES by forget-functor
444	naturality from
445	`CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariantSequence_exact`), substitute
446	`H¹(C, 𝒪_C) = 0` (the genus-`0` hypothesis, unfolding
447	`AlgebraicGeometry.genus`) and `H¹(C, k(P)) = 0` (skyscraper sheaf /
448	flasque cohomology, Hartshorne III.2.5), and collapse the segment to
449	`0 → H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P)) → 0`.
450	
451	Blueprint reference: `lem:H1_vanishing_lineBundleAtClosedPoint_genusZero`
452	(Hartshorne IV.1 p. 296). -/

462	/-! ## §4. The dimension formula `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2` in genus zero
463	
464	Specialise the Euler-characteristic identity
465	`χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` of `RR.2`
466	(`Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus`) to

468	`1` over `k̄`) and `g(C) = 0`, this gives `χ(𝒪_C(P)) = 2`. Unfolding
469	`χ` as `dim H⁰ − dim H¹` and substituting the `H¹`-vanishing of §3
470	yields `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2`. -/
471

499	/-! ## §5. A non-constant rational function with at most a simple pole at `P`
500	
501	The two-dimensionality of `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` and the one-dimensional
502	constant subspace `k̄ · 1` give a non-zero quotient `H⁰/k̄`. Any lift
503	of a non-zero element of the quotient is, under the identification of
504	`globalSections_iff`, a non-constant rational function `f ∈ K(C)` with
505	the order conditions `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`.
506	This is the seed of `RR.4` (the morphism `C → ℙ¹` produced by
507	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` from the basis `(1, f)`). -/
508	
509	/-- **Existence of a non-constant rational function regular on `C ∖ {P}`
510	with at most a simple pole at `P`** (Hartshorne IV §1 Exercise 1.1,
511	p. 297, the genus-`0` specialisation).
512	
513	Concretely, there exists `f ∈ K(C)` such that:
514	
515	* `f ≠ 0`;
516	* `f ∉ k̄` (i.e. `f` is non-constant — for instance, it does not lie
517	  in the image of the structural inclusion of constants);
518	* `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q ∈ C.PrimeDivisor` whose
519	  generic point is not `P`;
520	* `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` (at most a simple pole at `P`).
521	
522	iter-177+ body: use `dim_eq_two_of_genusZero` to get
523	`dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2`. The image of `1 ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) ≅ k̄`
524	under the structural inclusion `𝒪_C ↪ 𝒪_C(P)` spans a one-dimensional
525	subspace of `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))`; choose any section `s ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))`
526	not in this constant subspace (non-empty because `dim H⁰ = 2 > 1`),
527	then take `f := lineBundleAtClosedPoint.toFunctionField P hP s` and
528	verify the four bullets via `globalSections_iff` applied to `f`
529	(the forward direction supplies the order conditions from the existence
530	witness `⟨s, rfl⟩`). The chosen `f` is non-constant because `s` is not
531	in the constant subspace and `toFunctionField` is `k̄`-linear and
532	injective on global sections.
533	
534	The principal-divisor-non-zero formulation `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal
535	f hf ≠ 0` follows from non-constancy plus the fact that constant
536	functions have principal divisor zero (the converse — `div(f) = 0`
537	⇒ `f` constant — uses the integrality of `C` and is the Stacks 02P0
538	"functions with no zeros and poles are constant" type statement).
539	
540	Blueprint reference:
541	`cor:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_exists_nonconstant_genusZero` (alias
542	`cor:nonconstant_function_genus_zero` consumed by `RR.4`)
543	(Hartshorne IV.1 Exercise 1.1, p. 297). -/
544	theorem exists_nonconstant_genusZero
545	    [∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor,
546	        Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point)]
547	    (P : C.left) (_hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
548	    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
549	    (_hg : AlgebraicGeometry.genus C = 0) :
550	    ∃ (f : C.left.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0),
551	      (∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Q.point ≠ P →
552	          0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f) ∧
553	      (-1 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ f ∧
554	      Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal (X := C.left) f hf ≠ 0 := by
555	  sorry

10	/-!
11	# The rational-curve isomorphism `C ≅ ℙ¹` (RR.4)
12	
13	This file is the **RR.4** sub-build chapter for the project's headline
14	`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically irreducible
15	genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma pinned in
16	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity:290`).
17	
18	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain for the genus-`0` ⇒ `ℙ¹` classification routes
19	through the four sub-build chapters:
20	
21	- `RR.1` (`WeilDivisor.lean`): the Weil divisor group `Div(C)`, the degree
22	  map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ`, and the degree-zero principal-divisor identity.
23	- `RR.2` (`RRFormula.lean`): the Euler-characteristic identity
24	  `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` and Riemann–Roch in genus zero.
25	- `RR.3` (`OCofP.lean`): the line bundle `𝒪_C([P])` of a closed point and
26	  the existence of a non-constant rational function
27	  `f ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C([P])) ∖ k̄ · 1`.
28	- **`RR.4` (this file)**: the global classification — the linear-system
29	  morphism `C ⟶ ℙ¹` produced from `(1, f)` (Pin 1), its degree identified
30	  via the pole divisor (Pin 2), and the degree-`1` ⇒ isomorphism step
31	  (Pin 3). The headline target `AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
32	  (Pin 4) is the existing pinned declaration at
33	  `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:290` — see §0 below.
34

71	## Note on type expressivity
72	
73	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
74	each pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
75	
76	* `morphismToP1OfGlobalSections` returns a *concrete* morphism
77	  `X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` of `Over (Spec k̄)`, not `Iso.refl _` or a
78	  reflexive placeholder; the input data is the exact pair
79	  `(f, hf)` consumed by Mathlib's `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`.
80	* `morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` asserts the existence of a
81	  positive-integer degree `d` together with a Weil divisor `D` on `C`
82	  realising it — the *named substantive content* of the degree-via-pole
83	  identification. Unfolds to `∃ d D, 0 < d ∧ D.degree = d`, not `True`.
84	* `iso_of_degree_one` asserts `Nonempty (C ≅ C')` from the
85	  non-constant hypothesis together with the *substantive* degree-`1`
86	  hypothesis `Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField = 1`
87	  (paired with an `[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]` typeclass
88	  binder for the canonical `φ`-induced function-field map). The
89	  `Nonempty`-wrapped iso is the exact downstream-consumer shape,
90	  matching the `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` headline signature in AVR.lean.
91	  This signature was refined in iter-181 Lane I from the iter-177
92	  file-skeleton placeholder `Nonempty (C'.ff ≃+* C.ff)` to make the
93	  degree-`1`-induced-by-`φ` hypothesis the substantive content; see
94	  `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Decision 2.
95	
96	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
97	(the concrete `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`-derived morphism, the
98	existence-of-positive-degree-divisor statement, the existence of a
99	scheme isomorphism); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content `proof_wanted` /
100	`Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.
101	
102	## Curve conventions
103	
104	Following the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`,
105	`Jacobian.lean`, `OCofP.lean`), a **smooth proper geometrically
106	irreducible curve over `k̄`** carries:
107	
108	- `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]`,
109	- `[IsProper C.hom]`,
110	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]`.
111	
112	These instances are supplied by `inferInstance` at the call site. The
113	`[IsIntegral C.left]` / `[IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]` instances
114	required by the Weil-divisor / `RationalMap.order` API are threaded
115	explicitly per-lemma where consumed.

165	/-! ## §1. Pin 1 — the morphism `X ⟶ ℙ¹` from a globally-generating pair
166	(`morphismToP1OfGlobalSections`)
167	
168	Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1, specialised to `ℙ¹` over `k̄`. Given a graded
169	`k̄`-algebra homomorphism `f : k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)` whose image of the
170	irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)` generates the unit ideal of `Γ(X, ⊤)`, the
171	construction `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of Mathlib's
172	`AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic` produces a morphism
173	`X.left ⟶ Proj 𝒜 = ProjectiveLineBarScheme k̄`. Lifting to the slice
174	category over `Spec k̄` (via `Over.homMk` with a section-condition
175	proof) yields the pinned morphism
176	`X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄ : Over (Spec (.of kbar))`. -/
177	
178	/-- **The morphism `X ⟶ ℙ¹_{k̄}` produced from a globally-generating pair of
179	homogeneous sections** (Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1; Mathlib's
180	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` specialised to the standard
181	ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`).
182	
183	Given a graded `k̄`-algebra homomorphism `f : k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)`, a
184	`k̄`-algebra compatibility witness `halg` (recording that the composite
185	`kbar → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar → Γ(X.left, ⊤)` agrees with the natural
186	algebra map `kbar → Γ(X.left, ⊤)` induced by `X.hom`), and a proof `hf`
187	that the image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁) ⊆ k̄[X₀, X₁]` generates
188	the unit ideal of `Γ(X, ⊤)`, this constructs the unique morphism
189	`φ : X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` characterised on basic-opens by
190	`φ ⁻¹ᵁ D₊(X_i) = X.basicOpen (f X_i)` (the "no-common-zero" expression of
191	Hartshorne's morphism condition). The composite
192	`φ.left ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ = X.hom`, witnessing the `Over (Spec k̄)`
193	section condition.
194	
195	The input data `(f, halg, hf)` is the standard Hartshorne II §7 packaging
196	of "a pair of global sections `s₀, s₁ ∈ H⁰(X, ℒ)` of an invertible sheaf
197	`ℒ` without common zeros": `f` is the graded `k̄`-algebra map sending
198	`X_i ↦ s_i ∈ Γ(X, ℒ) ⊂ Γ(X, ⊤)` (after a choice of trivialisation
199	identifying `H⁰(X, ℒ)` with a submodule of `Γ(X, ⊤)` over the affine
200	cover `X = D(s₀) ∪ D(s₁)`); `halg` is the `kbar`-algebra-compatibility
201	condition (without which the section condition over `Spec kbar` cannot
202	be derived); `hf` is the no-common-zero condition.
203	
204	The construction invokes `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` on the standard
205	grading `projectiveLineBarGrading k̄` (the `ℕ`-grading on
206	`MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` by total degree, with `GradedRing` instance
207	via `MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra`), then wraps in `Over.homMk` with the
208	section condition proof. The section condition chases through
209	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` plus
210	`IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)` to reduce to
211	`X.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C)) = X.hom`,
212	which the `halg` hypothesis closes via `Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality` and
213	`toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv` (cf. the analogous chain in
214	`Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean:pointOfVec`).
215	
216	Blueprint reference: `lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections`
217	(Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1, II §7). -/
218	noncomputable def morphismToP1OfGlobalSections
219	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
220	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
221	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(X.left, ⊤))
222	    (_halg :
223	      f.comp (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) =
224	        X.hom.appTop.hom.comp (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom)
225	    (_hf :
226	      Ideal.map f
227	          (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤) :
228	    X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
229	  -- Underlying scheme morphism: invoke Mathlib's
230	  -- `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` on `projectiveLineBarGrading kbar`.
231	  -- The result `X.left ⟶ Proj 𝒜 = ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar` is
232	  -- then wrapped via `Over.homMk` with the section condition
233	  -- `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections … ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = X.hom`.
234	  Over.homMk
235	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f _hf) <| by
236	    -- Section condition (`Over (Spec k̄)`-compatibility):
237	    --   `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections … ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = X.hom`
238	    -- where `ProjectiveLineBar.hom = Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map (algebraMap kbar ↥(𝒜 0))`.
239	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
240	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
241	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq
242	        (R := kbar) (S := ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0))
243	        (A := MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (fun _ => rfl)
244	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
245	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
246	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
247	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
248	    -- Goal: `X.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C)) = X.hom`.
249	    -- Step 1: convert the `_halg` RingHom equation to a CommRingCat-level equation.
250	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C) =
251	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ X.hom.appTop := by
252	      rw [← MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq, _halg, CommRingCat.ofHom_comp,
253	        CommRingCat.ofHom_hom, CommRingCat.ofHom_hom]
254	    -- Step 2: rewrite, distribute `Spec.map`, and apply naturality of `toSpecΓ` plus
255	    -- the `toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (ΓSpecIso).inv = 𝟙` identity on `Spec (.of kbar)`.
256	    rw [hcc, Spec.map_comp, ← Category.assoc, ← Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality,
257	      Category.assoc, toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv, Category.comp_id]
258	
259	/-! ## §2. Pin 2 — the degree of a non-constant `C ⟶ ℙ¹` via its pole divisor
260	(`morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor`)
261	
262	Hartshorne II §6 + IV §2 opening: for a non-constant morphism
263	`φ : C ⟶ ℙ¹` from a smooth proper curve, the function-field extension
264	`k̄(ℙ¹) ↪ K(C)` is finite, and the degree
265	`[K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)] = deg(φ)` is recovered as the degree of the pullback
266	of any closed point of `ℙ¹` to `C`. Specialising to `Q = ∞`, this
267	identifies `deg(φ)` with the degree of the **pole divisor** of the
268	pulled-back affine coordinate `φ^* u`.
269	
270	For the iter-177 file-skeleton the substantive content is the
271	existence of a positive-degree Weil divisor on `C` realising
272	`deg(φ)`; the body in iter-178+ produces this divisor as the pole
273	divisor of `φ^* u` via the Hartshorne II.6.9 multiplicativity-of-degree
274	identity.
275	
276	This is a *separable* sub-build pin (the lemma is stated independently
277	of the headline `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` and does not require `g(C) = 0`).
278	-/
279	
280	/-- **The pole divisor of a morphism `φ : C → ℙ¹` of smooth proper curves
281	over `k̄`.** As a Weil divisor on `C`, it is `φ^*[∞]` — the pullback of the
282	divisor `[∞]` on `ℙ¹` along `φ`.
283	
284	The body is iter-183+ work; see `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin2.md` for the
285	construction route via affine-chart-localised `Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia`
286	(Stacks tag `02RW` / `0AX5`). The substantive content — the assignment from
287	a morphism `φ` to its `[∞]`-pullback Weil divisor — is the named target of
288	`morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor`: the latter pins the existential to this
289	specific divisor (not just any positive-degree divisor on `C`). -/
290	noncomputable def Hom.poleDivisor
291	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
292	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
293	    [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
294	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
295	    (_φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar) :
296	    C.left.WeilDivisor := sorry
297	
298	/-- **Degree identity for the pole divisor of a non-constant `C ⟶ ℙ¹`**
299	(Hartshorne IV §2 p. 299 opening + II §6.9 multiplicativity of degree under
300	finite pullback). The Weil-divisor degree of the pole divisor
301	`Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor φ = φ^*[∞]` equals the function-field-extension
302	degree `[K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)] = Module.finrank K(ℙ¹) K(C)`, where the
303	`K(ℙ¹)`-algebra structure on `K(C)` is the canonical `φ`-induced one (the
304	typeclass binder pins this; per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Decision 2
305	convention).
306	
307	**Tier-3 honest sorry (iter-183).** The substantive body is iter-184+ work
308	via the affine-chart-localised `Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia`
309	(Stacks `02RW` / `0AX5`) per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin2.md` Decision 2.
310	The strategy: pick an affine open `Spec A ⊂ ℙ¹` containing `∞`; the
311	preimage `Spec B ⊂ C` is finite over `Spec A` (non-constancy + smooth
312	proper curves ⟹ finite); both `A → B` are Dedekind extensions and
313	`Σ_{Q above P} e(Q|P) · f(Q|P) = [Frac B : Frac A] = [K(C) : K(ℙ¹)]`
314	identifies the pole-divisor degree with the function-field degree. The
315	non-constancy hypothesis is consumed in the finiteness reduction.
316	
317	This helper is the **single substantive gap** of
318	`morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor` after the iter-182 plan-phase
319	signature strengthening: the wrapper body below is sorry-free assembly
320	that reduces to this helper. -/
321	theorem Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank
322	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
323	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
324	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
325	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left]
326	    (φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar)
327	    (_hφ_non_const :
328	      ∀ Q : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar,
329	        φ ≠ toUnit C ≫ Q)
330	    [Algebra (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField C.left.functionField] :
331	    Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree (Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor φ) =
332	      (Module.finrank
333	        (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
334	        C.left.functionField : ℤ) := sorry
335	
336	/-- **Degree of a non-constant morphism to `ℙ¹` via its pole divisor**
337	(Hartshorne IV §2 p. 299 opening + II §6.9 multiplicativity of degree
338	under finite pullback).
339	
340	For a non-constant morphism `φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` from a smooth
341	proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over an algebraically closed
342	field `k̄`, there exists a positive integer `d` (= `deg(φ)`) together
343	with a Weil divisor `D` on `C` (= the pole divisor `φ^*[∞]` of the
344	pulled-back affine coordinate) of total degree `d`.
345	
346	The non-constancy hypothesis is encoded as `∀ Q, φ ≠ toUnit C ≫ Q`
347	(no constant morphism `C ⟶ ℙ¹` agrees with `φ`); this is equivalent to
348	`φ` being surjective onto `ℙ¹_{k̄}` since the image is a closed
349	irreducible subset of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` of dimension ≥ 1, and the only such is
350	`ℙ¹_{k̄}` itself.
351	
352	iter-178+ body: a non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves
353	over `k̄` is automatically finite (proper + quasi-finite). The
354	function-field extension `k̄(ℙ¹) ↪ K(C)` is finite of some degree
355	`d = [K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)] ≥ 1`. Take `D := φ^*[∞] ∈ Div(C)` via the standard
356	"pullback of a Weil divisor along a finite morphism" operation; by
357	Hartshorne II.6.9, `deg(D) = deg(φ) · deg([∞]) = d · 1 = d`. The
358	positivity `d ≥ 1` follows from non-constancy (else `deg(φ) = 0` would
359	force `φ` constant). Both the finite-pullback operation and the
360	multiplicativity-of-degree identity are project-bespoke sub-lemmas
361	threaded inside the iter-178+ body (cf. the closely related
362	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero` of `RR.1`).
363	
364	Blueprint reference: `lem:degree_via_pole_divisor` (Hartshorne IV §2
365	opening, p. 299; II §6.9 p. 137 for multiplicativity).
366	
367	**Signature strengthening (iter-182 Pin 2).** The iter-177 file-skeleton
368	signature output existential `∃ d D, 0 < d ∧ D.degree = (d : ℤ)` was
369	flagged `weakened-wrong` by the iter-181 `lean-vs-blueprint-checker`:
370	the existential is discharged by ANY positive-degree divisor on `C` and
371	does not reference `φ`. iter-182 strengthens the output to pin `D` to
372	`Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor φ` (a *specific* divisor depending on `φ`) and
373	pin its degree to `Module.finrank (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
374	C.left.functionField` (the function-field-extension degree `[K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)]`).
375	The `[Algebra ...]` instance binder pins the intended `Algebra` structure
376	to be the canonical `φ`-induced function-field map (matching the iter-181
377	Pin 3 sig refinement convention). See `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin2.md`
378	(verdict PROCEED) for the body recipe via
379	`Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia`. -/
380	theorem morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor
381	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
382	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
383	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
384	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left]
385	    (φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar)
386	    (_hφ_non_const :
387	      ∀ Q : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar,
388	        φ ≠ toUnit C ≫ Q)
389	    [Algebra (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField C.left.functionField] :
390	    ∃ (D : C.left.WeilDivisor),
391	      D = Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor φ ∧
392	        Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D =
393	          (Module.finrank
394	            (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
395	            C.left.functionField : ℤ) :=
396	  -- Witness `D := Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor φ`; equality `D = poleDivisor φ`
397	  -- is `rfl` definitionally; degree identity delegated to the named helper
398	  -- `Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank` (Tier-3 honest sorry above; body
399	  -- iter-184+ via `Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia` per
400	  -- `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin2.md` Decision 2).
401	  ⟨Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor φ, rfl,
402	    Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank φ _hφ_non_const⟩

404	/-! ## §3. Pin 3 — degree-`1` non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves
405	is an isomorphism (`iso_of_degree_one`)
406	
407	Hartshorne I §6 Corollary 6.12 (the function-field ⇔ smooth projective
408	curve equivalence of categories) specialised to a non-constant
409	degree-`1` morphism: such a morphism induces an isomorphism on function
410	fields, and lifts to an isomorphism of schemes by the equivalence.
411	
412	**Signature refinement (iter-181 Lane I).** The degree-`1` hypothesis
413	is now encoded directly as
414	`Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField = 1` paired with an
415	`[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]` typeclass binder (the
416	intended instance at each call site is the canonical `φ`-induced
417	function-field map). This refines the iter-177 file-skeleton
418	placeholder hypothesis
419	`Nonempty (C'.functionField ≃+* C.functionField)`, which is strictly
420	weaker than what the iter-182+ body needs (the birational-extension
421	argument requires the iso to be *induced by `φ` itself*, not an
422	abstract ring iso). See `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` (Decision 2,
423	DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY) for the analysis. The body in iter-182+ uses
424	either Hartshorne I.6.12 (the equivalence of categories) or the
425	Mathlib `Scheme.Hom.instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom` route to
426	lift the function-field iso to a scheme iso. -/
427	
428	/-- **Degree-`1` non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves is an
429	isomorphism** (Hartshorne I Corollary 6.12 specialised to degree `1`,
430	or equivalently Stacks tag `0AVX`).
431	
432	For smooth proper geometrically irreducible curves `C, C'` over an
433	algebraically closed field `k̄` and a non-constant morphism
434	`φ : C ⟶ C'` such that the induced `k̄`-algebra map on function fields
435	`φ^# : K(C') → K(C)` makes `K(C)` a one-dimensional `K(C')`-module
436	(equivalently `[K(C) : K(C')] = 1`), the morphism `φ` is an
437	isomorphism of `Over (Spec k̄)`-objects.
438	
439	**Signature refinement (iter-181 Lane I).** The degree-`1` hypothesis
440	is encoded as a typeclass binder
441	`[Algebra C'.left.functionField C.left.functionField]` together with
442	the equation `Module.finrank C'.left.functionField C.left.functionField = 1`.
443	The intended `Algebra` instance at every call site is the canonical
444	function-field map induced by `φ` (the composite of
445	`Scheme.Hom.stalkMap` at the generic point with the `IsFractionRing`
446	extension to the fraction-field; see Mathlib
447	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.functionField`). The previous signature took
448	an abstract existence wrapper
449	`Nonempty (C'.functionField ≃+* C.functionField)` which is strictly
450	weaker than what the iter-182+ body needs: the birational-extension
451	argument requires the iso to be **induced by `φ` itself**, not an
452	arbitrary ring iso. See `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` (Decision 2,
453	DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY) for the analysis prompting this refinement.
454	
455	iter-182+ body: invoke the scheme-theoretic argument of Hartshorne's
456	Corollary I.6.12 (Stacks `0AVX`) — equivalently
457	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom`
458	(see `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Steps 1–4):
459	
460	1. Reduce to `φ` finite (proper + quasi-finite ⟹ finite via
461	   `IsFinite.iff_isProper_and_isAffineHom`; quasi-finiteness follows
462	   from the degree-`1` hypothesis collapsing each fibre to a single
463	   point at the generic point and then extending by closedness).
464	2. Apply `Scheme.Hom.instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom` to get
465	   `Scheme.Hom.toNormalization φ : C ⟶ normalization φ` is an iso.
466	3. Identify `normalization φ = C'` via smoothness of `C'` (smooth
467	   proper curve over `k̄` ⟹ regular ⟹ normal). The integral
468	   closure `O_{C'} ↪ φ_* O_C` is then an equality under the
469	   `finrank = 1` hypothesis (rank-`1` torsion-free coherent inclusion
470	   on a Dedekind base is an iso).
471	4. Compose to extract `C ≅ C'` in the slice category.
472	
473	Alternative route (cleaner conceptually but more sheaf API): the
474	pushforward `φ_* O_C` is a coherent `O_{C'}`-module of generic
475	rank `[K(C) : K(C')] = 1`; the inclusion `O_{C'} ↪ φ_* O_C` is an
476	iso of coherent rank-`1` sheaves (torsion-free coherent of generic
477	rank `0` is zero on a Dedekind base), so `φ_* O_C = O_{C'}` and `φ`
478	is the structure morphism of an iso.
479	
480	Blueprint reference: `lem:degree_one_morphism_iso` (Hartshorne I §6
481	Corollary 6.12 p. 45 + IV §2 opening p. 299; Stacks tag `0AVX`). -/
482	theorem iso_of_degree_one
483	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
484	    {C C' : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
485	    [IsProper C.hom] [IsProper C'.hom]
486	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C'.hom]
487	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C'.hom]
488	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsIntegral C'.left]
489	    (φ : C ⟶ C')
490	    (_hφ_non_const : ∀ Q : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C',
491	        φ ≠ toUnit C ≫ Q)
492	    [Algebra C'.left.functionField C.left.functionField]
493	    (_hφ_deg :
494	      Module.finrank C'.left.functionField C.left.functionField = 1) :
495	    Nonempty (C ≅ C') :=
496	  sorry
497	
498	end Scheme
499	
500	end AlgebraicGeometry

62	/-! ## §1. Codim-1 cycle group / Weil divisor group
63	
64	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` (II §6, p. 130) is: `X` is a Noetherian integral
65	separated scheme that is regular in codimension one. Under `(*)`, prime divisors
66	are closed integral subschemes of codimension one, and the local ring at the
67	generic point of a prime divisor is a DVR with quotient field the function field
68	`K(X)`.
69	
70	A prime divisor is encoded by its **generic point** together with the
71	codimension-one witness `Order.coheight point = 1`: the data field `point : X`
72	selects the generic point of the closed integral subscheme, and the predicate
73	field `coheight` enforces codimension one in the specialisation preorder
74	(Hartshorne II §6 p. 130; blueprint pin `def:prime_divisor`). Integrality of the
75	closure is automatic, so no separate integrality witness is needed.
76	-/
77	
78	/-- A **prime divisor** on a scheme `X`: a closed integral subscheme of codimension
79	one, encoded (for a scheme satisfying Hartshorne's condition `(*)`) by its generic
80	point. On a curve, prime divisors correspond bijectively to closed points.
81	
82	The codimension-one witness is the predicate `Order.coheight point = 1` on the
83	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier` (Mathlib's convention is
84	`x ≤ y ↔ y ⤳ x`, so the generic point is the unique maximal element and a
85	generic point of a codim-1 closed integral subscheme has a length-one chain
86	above it). The closure `{point}̄` is automatically irreducible (so the reduced
87	induced subscheme structure is automatically integral); hence no separate
88	integrality field is needed.
89	
90	Blueprint reference: `def:prime_divisor` / `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6
91	p. 130; Stacks 02RW). -/
92	structure Scheme.PrimeDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) where
93	  /-- The generic point of the closed integral subscheme. -/
94	  point : X
95	  /-- Codimension-1 witness: `point` has coheight `1` in the specialisation
96	  preorder on `X.carrier`. Per iter-173 `wd-spec-refine` (`def:prime_divisor`). -/
97	  coheight : Order.coheight point = 1
98	
99	/-- The **Weil divisor group** of a scheme `X` satisfying Hartshorne's condition
100	`(*)`: the free abelian group on the set of prime divisors of `X`,
101	`Div(X) = ⨁_{Y prime divisor of X} ℤ · Y`. An element `D = Σ nᵢ · Yᵢ` with finitely
102	many nonzero coefficients is a **Weil divisor**; if all `nᵢ ≥ 0` it is **effective**.
103	
104	Blueprint reference: `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 130). -/
105	def Scheme.WeilDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type u := X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ
106	
107	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
108	
109	noncomputable instance (X : Scheme.{u}) : AddCommGroup X.WeilDivisor :=
110	  inferInstanceAs (AddCommGroup (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ))
111	
112	instance (X : Scheme.{u}) : Inhabited X.WeilDivisor :=
113	  inferInstanceAs (Inhabited (X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ))
114	
115	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
116	
117	/-! ## §2. Order of a rational function at a prime divisor
118	
119	For a scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, every prime divisor `Y` carries a discrete
120	valuation `v_Y` on the function field `K(X)`. The order of a nonzero rational
121	function `f ∈ K(X)^×` along `Y` is the integer `ord_Y(f) := v_Y(f)`. -/
122	
123	namespace Scheme.RationalMap
124	
125	/-- **Order of a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×` along a prime divisor `Y`.**
126	
127	For `X` satisfying Hartshorne's `(*)`, the local ring `O_{X,η}` at the generic
128	point `η` of `Y` is a discrete valuation ring with quotient field the function
129	field `K(X)`, and `ord_Y(f) = v_Y(f) ∈ ℤ` is the value of the associated normalised
130	discrete valuation on `f`.
131	
132	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
133	divisor and `ord_P(f) = v_P(f)` is the standard DVR valuation at `P`.
134	
135	Blueprint reference: `def:order_at_point` (Hartshorne II §6 pp. 130–131; Stacks 02ME).
136	
137	iter-176 body (per analogist `dvr-rationalmap-order`): the body uses Mathlib's
138	`Ring.ordFrac` (the canonical `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero hom from
139	`Mathlib.RingTheory.OrderOfVanishing.Basic`, Stacks `02MD`) on the stalk
140	`X.presheaf.stalk Y.point`, then projects through `WithZero.log : ℤᵐ⁰ → ℤ`
141	(the canonical projection with junk-on-zero, `Mathlib.Algebra.GroupWithZero.WithZero`).
142	On `f = 0` this gives `order Y 0 = 0` (junk convention from `WithZero.log_zero`).
143	
144	The required Mathlib typeclasses on the stalk are:
145	- `IsNoetherianRing` — from `[IsLocallyNoetherian X]`.
146	- `IsDomain` ⟹ `Nontrivial` — from `[IsIntegral X]`.
147	- `IsFractionRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) X.functionField` — from `[IsIntegral X]`.
148	- `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` — threaded explicitly (the
149	  topological-coheight-to-Krull-dim bridge `Order.coheight Y.point = 1 ⟹
150	  Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` is a Mathlib-upstream-pending
151	  gap; see Stacks `02IZ` / `005X`). -/
152	noncomputable def order {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
153	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)
154	    [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]
155	    (f : X.functionField) : ℤ :=
156	  WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) f)
157	
158	end Scheme.RationalMap
159	
160	/-! ### Regular-in-codimension-one bridge class
161	
162	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` includes "regular in codimension one", i.e.\ every
163	prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a DVR stalk `O_{X,Y.point}`. Mathlib has no direct
164	typeclass for this; we package it as the project-bespoke class
165	`Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne`, with an instance synthesising the per-`Y`
166	Krull-dim-≤-1 condition required by `Scheme.RationalMap.order`. Blueprint pin
167	("Iter-173+ may introduce a `Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne` predicate to
168	abbreviate this"; chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §2 "Standing hypothesis
169	`(*)` in the Lean encoding"). -/
170	
171	/-- Project-bespoke class encoding Hartshorne's "regular in codimension one"
172	clause of `(*)`: every prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a Krull-dim-≤-1 stalk. -/
173	class Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne (X : Scheme.{u}) : Prop where
174	  /-- The defining content: every prime divisor's stalk has Krull dimension at
175	  most one. (For a regular-in-codim-1 scheme the stalk is in fact a DVR; we
176	  only export the weaker Krull-dim bound here, sufficient to invoke
177	  `Ring.ordFrac`.) -/
178	  out : ∀ Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X, Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)
179	
180	/-- Bridge instance: from `[Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]`, typeclass
181	synthesis can derive `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` for every
182	prime divisor `Y`. -/
183	instance {X : Scheme.{u}} [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
184	    (Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X) :
185	    Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
186	  Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.out Y
187	
188	/-- **Hartshorne II.6.1**: for a nonzero rational function `f` on a Noetherian
189	integral scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, the order function `Y ↦ ord_Y(f)` is
190	nonzero at only finitely many prime divisors `Y`. This is the well-definedness
191	side condition for `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal`.
192	
193	iter-177 status: this packages Hartshorne's Lemma 6.1, which Mathlib does not
194	ship. The body is a Mathlib-upstream-pending gap (Stacks tag `02RV` — for a
195	nonzero element `f ∈ K(X)^×` of a Noetherian integral scheme, only finitely
196	many height-one primes can divide either numerator or denominator); the proof
197	factors through `IsLocallyNoetherian X` + the principal-ideal generation of
198	height-1 primes + the finite irreducible-component decomposition of
199	`V(f₀) ∪ V(f∞)`. The chapter pins this as a separate sub-build deferral
200	(`RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §5).
201	
202	The statement is generic in `f` (no `f ≠ 0` hypothesis is threaded): on
203	`f = 0` the function `Y ↦ ord_Y(0) = WithZero.log 0 = 0` has empty support,
204	which is finite, so the conclusion holds vacuously. -/
205	private theorem rationalMap_order_finite_support {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
206	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
207	    (f : X.functionField) :
208	    (Function.support (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor =>
209	      Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)).Finite := by
210	  sorry
211	
212	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
213	
214	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
215	
216	/-! ## §3. Divisor of a closed point on a curve
217	
218	On a smooth proper curve `C` over a field, every closed point `P ∈ C` is a prime
219	divisor (it is closed, integral, of codimension one in the one-dimensional integral
220	scheme `C`). The associated Weil divisor is `[P] = 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`. -/
221	
222	/-- **The Weil divisor associated to a closed point `P` on a curve.** The element
223	`[P] := 1 · P ∈ Div(C)`, i.e. the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one.
224	
225	For a smooth proper curve every closed point is automatically a prime divisor (it
226	is a codimension-one integral closed subscheme of the one-dimensional integral
227	scheme `C`); conversely every prime divisor on a curve is of this form, so an
228	arbitrary divisor on `C` is a finite formal `ℤ`-linear combination
229	`Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]` of closed points.
230	
231	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_closed_point` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137).
232	
233	iter-174 body: the function is junk-defined outside its intended scope. We
234	case-split on `Order.coheight P = 1` (the codimension-one witness of
235	`PrimeDivisor`). On the branch where the equality holds — automatic for a
236	closed point on a one-dimensional integral scheme — we promote `P` to a
237	`PrimeDivisor` via the witness and return `Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1`, i.e.
238	the prime divisor `P` with multiplicity one. On the off-branch (junk regime)
239	we return the zero divisor. The blueprint pins the well-definedness argument
240	"`IsClosed {P}` on a one-dimensional integral scheme ⟹ `coheight P = 1`" as a
241	separate threadable hypothesis at the call site (chapter L330–L340 "Lean
242	signature scope"); see `ofClosedPoint_eq_single` for the equation in the
243	hypothesised regime. -/
244	noncomputable def ofClosedPoint {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
245	    (_hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) : C.WeilDivisor :=
246	  if h : Order.coheight P = 1 then Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 else 0
247	
248	/-- In the hypothesised regime where the closed point `P` has coheight one
249	(the codim-1 condition automatic for a closed point of a one-dimensional
250	integral scheme), `ofClosedPoint P hP` is the prime divisor `P` with
251	multiplicity one. -/
252	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_single {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
253	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P = 1) :
254	    ofClosedPoint P hP = Finsupp.single ⟨P, h⟩ 1 := by
255	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]
256	
257	/-- Off-branch: outside the codim-1 regime, `ofClosedPoint` is junk-defined as
258	the zero divisor. (Only relevant when the user supplies a "closed point" that
259	does not have coheight one in the ambient scheme — e.g. a generic point or a
260	codim-≥2 point on a higher-dimensional scheme.) -/
261	lemma ofClosedPoint_eq_zero {C : Scheme.{u}} (P : C)
262	    (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C)) (h : Order.coheight P ≠ 1) :
263	    ofClosedPoint P hP = 0 := by
264	  simp [ofClosedPoint, h]
265	
266	/-! ## §4. Degree of a divisor over an algebraically closed base -/
267	
268	/-- **Degree of a Weil divisor on a smooth proper curve over `k̄`.**
269	
270	Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every closed point of a smooth proper
271	curve `C` has residue field `k̄`, so each prime divisor `[P]` contributes degree
272	one to the sum, and `deg(D) := Σᵢ nᵢ` is the sum of the integer coefficients of
273	the formal sum `D = Σ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]`.
274	
275	(Over a general field `k` one weights by the residue-field degrees
276	`Σᵢ nᵢ · [κ(Pᵢ) : k]` to recover the geometric degree; the project's RR bridge
277	needs only the `k̄` specialisation.)
278	
279	Blueprint reference: `def:divisor_degree` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 137; Stacks 0BE0).
280	
281	Implementation: `Finsupp.sum D (fun _ n => n)` is the sum of all coefficients of
282	the finitely supported function representing `D`. -/
283	noncomputable def degree (D : X.WeilDivisor) : ℤ :=
284	  (D : X.PrimeDivisor →₀ ℤ).sum (fun _ n => n)
285	
286	/-- **The degree map is a group homomorphism `Div(C) → ℤ`.**
287	
288	`deg(D₁ + D₂) = deg(D₁) + deg(D₂)`, `deg(-D) = -deg(D)`, `deg(0) = 0`. Bundled
289	as an `AddMonoidHom` for downstream use (the linear-equivalence quotient
290	`Cl(C) := Div(C) / im(div)` will inherit a `deg : Cl(C) → ℤ` from this).
291	
292	Blueprint reference: `thm:divisor_degree_hom` (immediate from `def:divisor_degree`).
293	
294	Implementation: built from `Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ)`, the
295	generic Mathlib packaging that lifts a family of `AddMonoidHom`s indexed by the
296	support into a single `AddMonoidHom` on the finsupp. Unfolds to
297	`D.sum (fun _ z ↦ z) = degree D` (see `degree_hom_apply` below). -/
298	noncomputable def degree_hom : X.WeilDivisor →+ ℤ :=
299	  Finsupp.liftAddHom (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ)
300	
301	@[simp]
302	lemma degree_hom_apply (D : X.WeilDivisor) : degree_hom D = degree D :=
303	  Finsupp.liftAddHom_apply (α := X.PrimeDivisor) (M := ℤ) (N := ℤ)
304	    (fun _ ↦ AddMonoidHom.id ℤ) D
305	
306	/-! ## §5. Principal divisors -/
307	
308	/-- **The principal divisor of a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×`.**
309	
310	By Hartshorne's Lemma 6.1, `ord_Y(f) = 0` for all but finitely many prime
311	divisors `Y`, so the formal sum
312	`div(f) := Σ_{Y prime divisor} ord_Y(f) · Y ∈ Div(X)`
313	has finite support and is a well-defined Weil divisor.
314	
315	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, this specialises to
316	`div(f) = Σ_{P closed point} ord_P(f) · [P]`.
317	
318	Blueprint reference: `def:principal_divisor` (Hartshorne II §6 Lemma 6.1 +
319	following definition, p. 131).
320	
321	iter-177 body: the construction uses `Finsupp.ofSupportFinite` with the
322	finite-support witness `rationalMap_order_finite_support`. The latter is a
323	private theorem packaging Hartshorne 6.1; its body is a Mathlib-pending gap
324	(see chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §5 sub-build note) and is left as
325	a `sorry` for an iter-178+ Mathlib-upstream PR. -/
326	noncomputable def principal [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
327	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (f : X.functionField)
328	    (_hf : f ≠ 0) : X.WeilDivisor :=
329	  Finsupp.ofSupportFinite
330	    (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor => Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)
331	    (rationalMap_order_finite_support f)
332	
333	/-- **The principal-divisor map is a group homomorphism `K(X)^× → Div(X)`.**
334	
335	Concretely `div(fg) = div(f) + div(g)`, `div(f⁻¹) = -div(f)`, `div(1) = 0`. Bundled
336	as a `MonoidHom` from the multiplicative units of `K(X)` to `Multiplicative (Div(X))`
337	(equivalently: an additive map `(K(X)^×, ·) → (Div(X), +)`).
338	
339	Blueprint reference: `thm:principal_hom` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 131).
340	
341	iter-177 body: closes coordinate-wise from the DVR identities
342	`v_Y(fg) = v_Y(f) + v_Y(g)`, `v_Y(1) = 0`. The per-`Y` identities live in
343	`Scheme.RationalMap.order` via `Ring.ordFrac` (a `K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` monoid-with-zero
344	hom) and `WithZero.log_mul` / `WithZero.log_one`. -/
345	noncomputable def principal_hom [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
346	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] :
347	    (X.functionField)ˣ →* Multiplicative X.WeilDivisor where
348	  toFun u :=
349	    Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal (↑u : X.functionField) (Units.ne_zero u))
350	  map_one' := by
351	    -- Goal: Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑1 _) = 1.
352	    -- Use `← ofAdd_zero` to rewrite RHS as `Multiplicative.ofAdd 0`,
353	    -- then `congr 1` reduces to `principal ↑1 _ = 0` (Finsupp equality),
354	    -- and `Finsupp.ext` peels to the per-`Y` coordinate identity, which
355	    -- closes by unfolding `order` to `WithZero.log (Ring.ordFrac _ 1) = 0`
356	    -- via `map_one` and `WithZero.log_one`.
357	    rw [← ofAdd_zero]
358	    congr 1
359	    apply Finsupp.ext
360	    intro Y
361	    change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y
362	        (((1 : (X.functionField)ˣ) : X.functionField)) = 0
363	    rw [Units.val_one]
364	    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
365	    rw [map_one, WithZero.log_one]
366	  map_mul' u v := by
367	    -- Goal: Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑(u*v) _) =
368	    --       Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑u _) * Multiplicative.ofAdd (principal ↑v _).
369	    -- Use `← ofAdd_add` to rewrite RHS, then `congr 1` reduces to
370	    -- `principal ↑(u*v) _ = principal ↑u _ + principal ↑v _` (Finsupp
371	    -- equality), and `Finsupp.ext` peels to the per-`Y` coordinate
372	    -- identity `order Y (uv) = order Y u + order Y v`, which closes by
373	    -- `Ring.ordFrac (uv) = Ring.ordFrac u * Ring.ordFrac v` (`map_mul`)
374	    -- and `WithZero.log_mul` (the nonzero hypotheses come from
375	    -- `Units.ne_zero` and `map_ne_zero`).
376	    rw [← ofAdd_add]
377	    congr 1
378	    apply Finsupp.ext
379	    intro Y
380	    change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y ((↑(u * v) : X.functionField))
381	      = Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (↑u : X.functionField)
382	      + Scheme.RationalMap.order Y (↑v : X.functionField)
383	    rw [Units.val_mul]
384	    unfold Scheme.RationalMap.order
385	    rw [map_mul]
386	    exact WithZero.log_mul
387	      ((map_ne_zero _).mpr (Units.ne_zero u))
388	      ((map_ne_zero _).mpr (Units.ne_zero v))
389	
390	/-- **Principal divisors on a complete nonsingular curve have degree zero**
391	(Hartshorne Corollary II.6.10, Stacks 0BE3).
392	
393	For every nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(C)^×` on a smooth proper curve `C`
394	over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
395	`deg(div(f)) = 0 ∈ ℤ`.
396	
397	Blueprint reference: `thm:principal_deg_zero` (Hartshorne II.6 Cor. 6.10 p. 138).
398	
399	The proof (Hartshorne 6.10): if `f ∈ k̄` is constant then `div(f) = 0` and the
400	claim is trivial. Otherwise the inclusion `k̄(f) ⊂ K(C)` exhibits `K(C)` as a
401	finite extension of `k̄(f) ≅ k̄(t)`, so the corresponding morphism
402	`φ : C → ℙ¹_{k̄}` is finite, `div(f) = φ^*([0] - [∞])`, and pullback along a
403	finite morphism multiplies degree by `deg(φ)`. Two auxiliary sub-lemmas
404	(finite morphism induced by a non-constant rational function; multiplicativity
405	of degree under finite pullback, Hartshorne II.6.9) are deferred to follow-up
406	iters of `RR.1`. -/
407	theorem principal_degree_zero {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
408	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
409	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
410	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
411	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
412	    (f : C.left.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0) :
413	    degree (principal f hf) = 0 := by
414	  -- Hartshorne II.6.10 (iter-178 partial). Case-split on whether every
415	  -- prime-divisor order of `f` vanishes. On a complete nonsingular curve,
416	  -- this case split exactly recovers Hartshorne's constant-vs-non-constant
417	  -- split: `(∀ Y, ord_Y f = 0)` ⟺ `f ∈ k̄ \ {0}` (a nowhere-vanishing
418	  -- rational function on a complete curve is constant; that direction is
419	  -- Hartshorne II.6.10's "if `f ∈ k̄` then `(f) = 0`").
420	  by_cases hconst : ∀ Y : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f = 0
421	  · -- Constant branch: every order vanishes ⟹ `principal f hf = 0` ⟹
422	    -- `degree (principal f hf) = degree 0 = 0`.
423	    have hprincipal_zero : principal f hf = 0 := by
424	      apply Finsupp.ext
425	      intro Y
426	      change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f = 0
427	      exact hconst Y
428	    rw [hprincipal_zero]
429	    unfold degree
430	    exact Finsupp.sum_zero_index
431	  · -- Non-constant branch (Hartshorne II.6.10 `f ∉ k̄`): the inclusion
432	    -- `k̄(f) ⊂ K(C)` defines a finite morphism `φ : C → ℙ¹_{k̄}` via the
433	    -- function-field-determines-curve correspondence (Hartshorne I.6.12),
434	    -- and `principal f hf = φ^*([0] - [∞])`. Pullback along a finite
435	    -- morphism of curves multiplies degrees by `deg(φ)` (Hartshorne
436	    -- II.6.9), so `deg(principal f hf) = deg(φ) · deg([0] - [∞]) =
437	    -- deg(φ) · 0 = 0`.
438	    -- This branch is gated on (i) the `φ : C → ℙ¹` construction (Lane 5
439	    -- `RationalCurveIso.lean:morphismToP1OfGlobalSections`, iter-178+),
440	    -- and (ii) the degree-multiplicativity-under-finite-pullback bridge
441	    -- (Hartshorne II.6.9, Mathlib gap).
442	    sorry
443	
444	/-! ## §6. Linear equivalence and the divisor class group -/
445	
446	/-- **Linear equivalence of Weil divisors.**
447	
448	Two Weil divisors `D, D' ∈ Div(X)` are linearly equivalent, written `D ~ D'`, if
449	and only if there exists a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×` with
450	`D - D' = div(f) ∈ Div(X)`.
451	
452	`~` is an equivalence relation (reflexivity from `div(1) = 0`, symmetry from
453	`div(f⁻¹) = -div(f)`, transitivity from `div(fg) = div(f) + div(g)`, all via
454	`thm:principal_hom`). The quotient `Cl(X) := Div(X) / im(div)` is the
455	**divisor class group** of `X`.
456	
457	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, `thm:principal_deg_zero` shows the
458	degree map descends to `deg : Cl(C) → ℤ`.
459	
460	Blueprint reference: `def:linear_equivalence` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 131). -/
461	def LinearEquivalence [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
462	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (D D' : X.WeilDivisor) : Prop :=
463	  ∃ (f : X.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0), D - D' = principal f hf
464	
465	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
466	
467	end AlgebraicGeometry
468

8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: the structure presheaf
10	
11	Sub-file (1/3) of the `StructureSheafModuleK` split (refactor iter-174). This file
12	ships the categorical gap-fills, the sheafification / `Ext` typeclass prerequisites,
13	and the per-open ring-map helpers (1)–(5) that culminate in the presheaf-of-
14	`k`-modules `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 6). Its sibling `SheafProperty.lean`
15	discharges the sheaf condition and bundles `toModuleKSheaf` (helpers 7–8); the
16	sibling `Carriers.lean` carries the downstream finite-length carriers and producer
17	instances.
18	
19	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
20	-/
21	
22	set_option autoImplicit false
23	
24	universe u v
25	
26	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
27	
28	namespace CategoryTheory
29	
30	universe u₂ v₂
31	
32	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D` is additive when `D`
33	is preadditive. The `map_add` field reduces to `f + g = f + g` componentwise after
34	`ext; rfl`. -/
35	instance Functor.const_additive {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
36	    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] : (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Additive where
37	  map_add := by intros; ext; rfl
38	
39	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear when
40	`D` is `Linear R`-enriched. The `[CategoryTheory.Linear R D]` qualification is
41	load-bearing — bare `[Linear R D]` would be parsed as `Linear` of a functor. -/
42	instance Functor.const_linear {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
43	    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] (R : Type*) [Semiring R]
44	    [CategoryTheory.Linear R D] :
45	    (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Linear R where
46	  map_smul := by intros; ext; rfl
47	
48	namespace Adjunction
49	
50	variable {C₁ : Type u} {D₁ : Type u₂} [Category.{v} C₁] [Category.{v₂} D₁]
51	    [Preadditive C₁] [Preadditive D₁] {F : C₁ ⥤ D₁} {G : D₁ ⥤ C₁} (adj : F ⊣ G)
52	    (R : Type*) [Semiring R] [CategoryTheory.Linear R C₁] [CategoryTheory.Linear R D₁]
53	
54	include adj
55	
56	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `G : D ⥤ C` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ⊣ G`,
57	then `F : C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear. Mirrors Mathlib's `left_adjoint_additive`; the
58	`simp` call closes via unit naturality + the `Linear.smul_comp` / `Linear.comp_smul`
59	default simp lemmas. -/
60	lemma left_adjoint_linear [G.Additive] [G.Linear R] : F.Linear R where
61	  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by
62	    simp [adj.homEquiv_unit])
63	
64	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `F : C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ⊣ G`,
65	then `G : D ⥤ C` is `R`-linear. The dual of `left_adjoint_linear`. -/
66	lemma right_adjoint_linear [F.Additive] [F.Linear R] : G.Linear R where
67	  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm.injective (by
68	    simp [adj.homEquiv_counit])
69	
70	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): linear lifting of `Adjunction.homAddEquiv`. Under
71	`[F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]`, the additive equivalence
72	`(F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃+ (X ⟶ G.obj Y)` upgrades to a `LinearEquiv` over `R`. The
73	`change` is load-bearing: it exposes the underlying `homEquiv` form, allowing
74	`simp [homEquiv_unit]` to close via the same naturality argument as in
75	`left_adjoint_linear`. -/
76	noncomputable def homLinearEquiv [F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]
77	    (X : C₁) (Y : D₁) : (F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ G.obj Y) :=
78	  haveI : F.Linear R := adj.left_adjoint_linear R
79	  { adj.homAddEquiv X Y with
80	    map_smul' := fun r f => by
81	      change adj.homEquiv _ _ (r • f) = r • adj.homEquiv _ _ f
82	      simp [adj.homEquiv_unit] }
83	
84	end Adjunction
85	
86	end CategoryTheory
87	
88	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology
89	
90	/-- Phase A step 5 prerequisite (a): sheafification on the topology of opens of
91	any topological space, valued in `ModuleCat k`. Inferable from Mathlib's
92	small-site / concrete-category sheafification API; the prover's task is the
93	universe pinning, mirroring the iter-004 `instHasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp`. -/
94	instance instHasSheafify_Opens_ModuleCatK
95	    (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : TopCat.{u}) :
96	    CategoryTheory.HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
97	      (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
98	  inferInstance
99	
100	/-- Phase A step 5 prerequisite (b): `Ext` on the sheaf category. The universe
101	annotation `HasExt.{u+1}` is forced by the morphism universe of
102	`Sheaf (Opens.gT X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)`; mirrors the iter-004
103	`instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp`. -/
104	noncomputable instance instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_ModuleCatK
105	    (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : TopCat.{u}) :
106	    CategoryTheory.HasExt.{u+1}
107	      (CategoryTheory.Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
108	        (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=
109	  CategoryTheory.HasExt.standard _
110	
111	end AlgebraicGeometry.Cohomology

113	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
114	
115	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
116	
117	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (1): the ring map `k → Γ(C, U)` induced by the
118	structure morphism `C.hom : C.left ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)` and the
119	restriction along `U ≤ ⊤`. -/
120	noncomputable def kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
121	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
122	    (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ C.left.presheaf.obj U :=
123	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫
124	    C.hom.app ⊤ ≫ C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op
125	
126	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (2): `Γ(C, U)` is a `k`-algebra via the ring
127	map of `kToSection`. -/
128	noncomputable instance algebraSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
129	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
130	    Algebra k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
131	  RingHom.toAlgebra (kToSection C U).hom
132	
133	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (3): unfolding lemma for the algebra map. -/
134	lemma algebraMap_eq_kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
135	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
136	    (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)) = (kToSection C U).hom :=
137	  rfl
138	
139	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (4): the structure-morphism algebra map is
140	natural in `U`. -/
141	lemma kToSection_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
142	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
143	    kToSection C U ≫ C.left.presheaf.map f = kToSection C V := by
144	  simp only [kToSection, Category.assoc, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
145	  rfl
146	
147	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (5): corollary of (4) at the level of
148	`algebraMap`. -/
149	lemma algebraMap_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
150	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
151	    (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) r)
152	      = algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj V) r := by
153	  rw [algebraMap_eq_kToSection, algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
154	  exact congrArg (fun (g : (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ _) => g.hom r)
155	    (kToSection_naturality (C := C) f)
156	
157	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
158	
159	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
160	
161	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
162	
163	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (6): the presheaf of `k`-modules built from
164	`O_C` and the structure-morphism algebra structure. -/
165	noncomputable def toModuleKPresheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
166	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
167	  obj U := ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)
168	  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom
169	    { toFun := fun x => (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom x
170	      map_add' := fun x y => by
171	        simp [(C.left.presheaf.map f).hom.map_add]
172	      map_smul' := fun r x => by
173	        simp only [Algebra.smul_def, RingHom.map_mul, RingHom.id_apply]
174	        congr 1
175	        exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality
176	          (C := C) f r }
177	  map_id U := by
178	    ext x
179	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
180	      ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
181	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_id U)) x
182	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
183	    ext x
184	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
185	      ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
186	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_comp f g)) x
187	
188	/-- Object-evaluation simp lemma for `toModuleKPresheaf`. Definitionally true
189	by construction; tagged `@[simp]` so consumers can rewrite without unfolding
190	the constructor. Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007). -/
191	@[simp] lemma toModuleKPresheaf_obj (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
192	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
193	    (toModuleKPresheaf C).obj U = ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
194	  rfl
195	
196	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: the sheaf property and bundled structure sheaf
10	
11	Sub-file (2/3) of the `StructureSheafModuleK` split (refactor iter-174). This file
12	discharges the sheaf condition for `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 7) and bundles the
13	result as `toModuleKSheaf` (helper 8); the forget-and-recover natural iso to the
14	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf is also packaged here. The downstream
15	finite-length carriers live in the sibling `Carriers.lean`.
16	
17	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
18	-/
19	
20	set_option autoImplicit false
21	
22	universe u v
23	
24	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
25	
26	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
27	
28	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
29	
30	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (7): the presheaf of (6) is a sheaf for the
31	Grothendieck topology of opens of `C.left.toTopCat`. -/
32	lemma toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
33	    Presheaf.IsSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
34	      (toModuleKPresheaf C) := by
35	  rw [Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _ (CategoryTheory.forget (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
36	  convert (Presheaf.isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_forget _ _
37	      (CategoryTheory.forget CommRingCat.{u})).mp C.left.sheaf.property using 1
38	
39	/-- Phase A step 5 main: the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme as a sheaf
40	of `k`-modules. Bundles `toModuleKPresheaf` (helper 6) and
41	`toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` (helper 7) into the standard `Sheaf` shape. -/
42	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
43	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
44	  ⟨toModuleKPresheaf C, toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf C⟩
45	
46	/-- Phase A step 5 polish (iter-007): forget-and-recover natural iso between
47	the iter-006 `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf and the iter-004
48	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued structure sheaf. The two sheaves agree on the nose at
49	the underlying-presheaf level: forgetting from `ModuleCat k` to `AddCommGrpCat`
50	via `forget₂` recovers the iter-004 `toAbSheaf C.left`. Closure body
51	`Iso.refl _`; probe-confirmed (`lean_run_code`, iter-007 plan-agent). -/
52	noncomputable def toModuleKSheaf_forgetCompare
53	    (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
54	    (sheafCompose (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
55	        (forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} k) AddCommGrpCat.{u})).obj (toModuleKSheaf C)
56	      ≅ toAbSheaf C.left :=
57	  Iso.refl _
58	
59	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
